051045.cherokee: ᏗᎨᏥᎤᏍᏕᏎᎸᎯᏃ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏈᏓ ᏅᏓᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏛ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᎢ, ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎨᏥᏐᏅᏰᎸ ᎨᏥᏁᎸ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ.
051045.english: And they of the circumcision which believed were astonished, as many as came with Peter, because that on the Gentiles also was poured out the gift of the Holy Ghost.
051045.phonetic: Di-ge-tsi-u-s-de-se-lv-hi-no a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi ni-ga-dv Qui-da nv-da-gv-wa-s-da-wa-dv-dv u-tsa-ti u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-se-i, nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi na-s-quo ge-tsi-so-nv-ye-lv ge-tsi-ne-lv Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do.

051046.cherokee: ᏚᎾᏛᎦᏁᎴᏰᏃ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏓᏂᏬᏂᏍᎬ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᎸᏉᏗᏍᎬ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ.  ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏈᏓ ᎤᏁᏤ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ;
051046.english: For they heard them speak with tongues, and magnify God. Then answered Peter,
051046.phonetic: Du-na-dv-ga-ne-le-ye-no tsu-da-le-nv-dv da-ni-wo-ni-s-gv a-le a-ni-lv-quo-di-s-gv U-ne-la-nv-hi.  Hna-quo-no Qui-da u-ne-tse hi-a nu-we-se-i;

051047.cherokee: ᏥᎪ ᎩᎶ ᏳᏅᏍᏙᎯ ᎠᎹ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎨᎦᏬᏍᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᏥᎨᏥᏁᎸ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎠᏴ ᎡᎩᏁᎸᎢ?
051047.english: Can any man forbid water, that these should not be baptized, which have received the Holy Ghost as well as we?
051047.phonetic: Tsi-go gi-lo yu-nv-s-do-hi a-ma hi-a na-s-gi di-ge-ga-wo-s-di-yi na-s-gi na-s-quo Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do tsi-ge-tsi-ne-lv na-s-gi-ya a-yv e-gi-ne-lv-i?

051048.cherokee: ᏕᎤᏁᏤᎴᏃ ᏗᎨᎦᏬᏍᏗᏱ ᏕᎤᏙᏍᏛ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ.  ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎬᏩᏔᏲᏎᎴ ᎢᎸᏍᎩ ᏧᏒᎯᏛ ᎤᏪᏗᏱ.
051048.english: And he commanded them to be baptized in the name of the Lord. Then prayed they him to tarry certain days.
051048.phonetic: De-u-ne-tse-le-no di-ge-ga-wo-s-di-yi de-u-do-s-dv U-gv-wi-yu-hi.  Hna-quo-no gv-wa-ta-yo-se-le i-lv-s-gi tsu-sv-hi-dv u-we-di-yi.

051101.cherokee: ᎨᏥᏅᏏᏛᏃ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᎵᏅᏟ ᏧᏗᏱ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏁ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏚᎾᏓᏂᎸᏨ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ.
051101.english: And the apostles and brethren that were in Judaea heard that the Gentiles had also received the word of God.
051101.phonetic: Ge-tsi-nv-si-dv-no a-le a-na-li-nv-tli Tsu-di-yi a-ne-hi u-na-dv-ga-ne na-s-gi tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi na-s-quo du-na-da-ni-lv-tsv ka-no-he-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga.

051102.english: And when Peter was come up to Jerusalem, they that were of the circumcision contended with him,

051103.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᎡᎢ; ᏂᏗᎨᏥᎤᏍᏕᏎᎸᎾ ᏕᎯᏴᏎᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏓᏴᏁᎢ.
051103.english: Saying, Thou wentest in to men uncircumcised, and didst eat with them.
051103.phonetic: Hi-a nu-ni-we-e-i; ni-di-ge-tsi-u-s-de-se-lv-na de-hi-yv-se-le-i, a-le na-s-gi i-tsu-la-ha i-tsa-li-s-da-yv-ne-i.

051104.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏈᏓ ᎤᎴᏅᎮ ᏚᏃᎮᎮᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᏱᎸᏎᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ;
051104.english: But Peter rehearsed the matter from the beginning, and expounded it by order unto them, saying,
051104.phonetic: A-se-no Qui-da u-le-nv-he du-no-he-he-le ni-ga-dv o-s-dv u-yi-lv-se-i, hi-a nu-we-se-i;

051105.cherokee: ᏦᏈ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎨᏙᎲᎩ ᎦᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗᏍᎬᎩ; ᏣᎦᎵᎰᏃ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎾᏆᎵᏍᏓᏁᎸ ᎠᏆᏁᎳᏫᏎᎸᎩ, ᎠᏖᎵᏙ ᎠᎩᎪᎲᎩ ᎡᎳᏗ ᏅᏓᏳᏍᏗᏗᏒᎩ, ᎡᏆ ᎠᏄᏬ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏅᏓᏳᏓᎴᏅᎯ, ᏅᎩ ᏂᏚᏅᏏᏴ ᏥᏚᏂᏂᏴᏐᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎠᏴ ᎠᎩᎷᏤᎸᎩ.
051105.english: I was in the city of Joppa praying: and in a trance I saw a vision, A certain vessel descend, as it had been a great sheet, let down from heaven by four corners; and it came even to me:
051105.phonetic: Tso-qui ga-du-hv ge-do-hv-gi ga-da-do-li-s-di-s-gv-gi; tsa-ga-li-ho-no na-s-gi-ya na-qua-li-s-da-ne-lv a-qua-ne-la-wi-se-lv-gi, a-te-li-do a-gi-go-hv-gi e-la-di nv-da-yu-s-di-di-sv-gi, e-qua a-nu-wo na-s-gi-ya-i ga-lv-la-di nv-da-yu-da-le-nv-hi, nv-gi ni-du-nv-si-yv tsi-du-ni-ni-yv-so-i; a-le a-yv a-gi-lu-tse-lv-gi.

051106.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎠᏆᎦᏙᏍᏔᏅ ᎠᏆᏓᏅᏖᎸᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏕᏥᎪᎥᎩ ᏅᎩ ᏗᏂᏅᏌᏗ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎢᎾᎨ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᏓᎾᏏᏂᏙᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎦᎸᎶ ᎠᏂᏃᎯᎵᏙᎯ.
051106.english: Upon the which when I had fastened mine eyes, I considered, and saw fourfooted beasts of the earth, and wild beasts, and creeping things, and fowls of the air.
051106.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no a-qua-ga-do-s-ta-nv a-qua-da-nv-te-lv-gi a-le de-tsi-go-v-gi nv-gi di-ni-nv-sa-di e-lo-hi a-ne-hi a-le i-na-ge a-ne-hi a-le a-na-da-na-si-ni-do-hi a-le ga-lv-lo a-ni-no-hi-li-do-hi.

051107.cherokee: ᎧᏁᎬᏃ ᎠᏆᏛᎦᏅᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎾᎩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᏔᎴᎲᎦ ᏈᏓ, ᎯᎷᎦ ᎠᎴ ᎭᎵᏍᏓᏴᎲᎦ.
051107.english: And I heard a voice saying unto me, Arise, Peter; slay and eat.
051107.phonetic: Ka-ne-gv-no a-qua-dv-ga-nv-gi hi-a na-gi-we-se-lv-gi; Ta-le-hv-ga Qui-da, hi-lu-ga a-le ha-li-s-da-yv-hv-ga.

051108.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎾᎩᏪᏒᎩ; ᏞᏍᏗ, ᏣᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᏝᏰᏃ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎦᏓᎭ ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏅᎦᎸᎲᎾ ᏳᏴᎶ ᏥᎣᎵ.
051108.english: But I said, Not so, Lord: for nothing common or unclean hath at any time entered into my mouth.
051108.phonetic: A-se-no hi-a na-gi-we-sv-gi; Tle-s-di, Tsa-gv-wi-yu-hi, tla-ye-no i-lv-hi-yu go-hu-s-di ga-da-ha a-le ni-ga-nv-ga-lv-hv-na yu-yv-lo tsi-o-li.

051109.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎧᏁᎬ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎯᎠ ᏅᏛᎠᎩᏪᏎᎸᎩ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏛᎤᎶᏒᎩ; ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏅᎦᎸᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏞᏍᏗ ᎦᏓᎭ ᏲᏎᎮᏍᏗ.
051109.english: But the voice answered me again from heaven, What God hath cleansed, that call not thou common.
051109.phonetic: A-se-no ka-ne-gv ta-li-ne hi-a nv-dv-a-gi-we-se-lv-gi ga-lv-la-di dv-u-lo-sv-gi; U-ne-la-nv-hi u-nv-ga-lv-dv na-s-gi tle-s-di ga-da-ha yo-se-he-s-di.

051110.cherokee: ᏦᎢᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏔᎵᏁ ᏭᎵᏌᎳᏓᏅᎩ.
051110.english: And this was done three times: and all were drawn up again into heaven.
051110.phonetic: Tso-i-no na-s-gi hi-a nu-li-s-ta-nv-gi, ni-ga-dv-no ga-lv-la-di ta-li-ne wu-li-sa-la-da-nv-gi.

051111.cherokee: ᎬᏂᏳᏉᏃ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏦᎢ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᏂᏙᎾᎥ ᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏉᎸᎢ, ᎬᎩᏯᏅᎰᎸᎯ ᏏᏌᎵᏱ ᏅᏓᎨᏥᏅᏒᎯ.
051111.english: And, behold, immediately there were three men already come unto the house where I was, sent from Caesarea unto me.
051111.phonetic: Gv-ni-yu-quo-no gi-la-quo i-yv-dv tso-i i-ya-ni-dv a-ni-s-ga-ya a-ni-do-na-v a-da-ne-lv na-hna a-quo-lv-i, gv-gi-ya-nv-ho-lv-hi Si-sa-li-yi nv-da-ge-tsi-nv-sv-hi.

051112.cherokee: ᎠᏓᏅᏙᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎾᎩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏂᏗᏣᏢᏫᏎᎲᎾ ᏘᏍᏓᏩᏚᎦ.  ᎾᏍᏉᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏑᏓᎵ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᎢᏓᏓᏅᏟ ᎬᏆᎵᎪᏁᎸᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᏗᎦᏁᎸ ᏬᎩᏴᎸᎩ.
051112.english: And the spirit bade me go with them, nothing doubting. Moreover these six brethren accompanied me, and we entered into the man's house:
051112.phonetic: A-da-nv-do-no hi-a na-gi-we-se-lv-gi; go-hu-s-di ni-di-tsa-tlv-wi-se-hv-na ti-s-da-wa-du-ga.  Na-s-quo-no hi-a na-s-gi su-da-li i-ya-ni-dv i-da-da-nv-tli gv-qua-li-go-ne-lv-gi, a-le na-s-gi a-s-ga-ya di-ga-ne-lv wo-gi-yv-lv-gi.

051113.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎣᎩᏃᏁᎸᎩ ᎤᎪᎲᎢ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎦᏁᎸᎢ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏙᎨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᏦᏈ ᏘᏅᎵ ᎠᏂᏫᏅ, ᎠᎴ ᏫᏯᏅ ᏌᏩᏂ ᏈᏓ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ;
051113.english: And he shewed us how he had seen an angel in his house, which stood and said unto him, Send men to Joppa, and call for Simon, whose surname is Peter;
051113.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no o-gi-no-ne-lv-gi u-go-hv-i di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi u-wa-sv ga-ne-lv-i na-s-gi ga-do-ge hi-a nu-we-se-le-i; Tso-qui ti-nv-li a-ni-wi-nv, a-le wi-ya-nv Sa-wa-ni Qui-da tsu-do-i-dv;

051114.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏓᏣᏃᏁᎵ ᎢᏣᏛᏁᏗᏱ ᏣᎵᏍᏕᎸᏗᏱ ᏂᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏗᏣᏤᎵ ᏏᏓᏁᎸᎢ.
051114.english: Who shall tell thee words, whereby thou and all thy house shall be saved.
051114.phonetic: Na-s-gi da-tsa-no-ne-li i-tsa-dv-ne-di-yi tsa-li-s-de-lv-di-yi ni-hi a-le ni-ga-dv di-tsa-tse-li si-da-ne-lv-i.

051115.cherokee: ᎠᏆᎴᏅᎲᏃ ᎠᎩᏬᏂᏒ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎡᎳᏗ ᏄᏛᏁᎸᎩ, ᎤᏂᎷᏤᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎠᏴ ᏥᎩᎷᏤᎸ ᏗᏓᎴᏂᏍᎬᎢ.
051115.english: And as I began to speak, the Holy Ghost fell on them, as on us at the beginning.
051115.phonetic: A-qua-le-nv-hv-no a-gi-wo-ni-sv Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do e-la-di nu-dv-ne-lv-gi, u-ni-lu-tse-gi, na-s-gi-ya a-yv tsi-gi-lu-tse-lv di-da-le-ni-s-gv-i.

051116.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᏆᏅᏓᏛᎩ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏁᏨᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏥᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᏣᏂ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ ᎠᎹ ᏓᏓᏬᏍᏗᏍᎬᎩ, ᏂᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᏙᏓᏰᏣᏬᏍᏔᏂ.
051116.english: Then remembered I the word of the Lord, how that he said, John indeed baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized with the Holy Ghost.
051116.phonetic: Hna-quo-no a-qua-nv-da-dv-gi U-gv-wi-yu-hi u-ne-tsv-i, hi-a tsi-nu-we-sv-gi; Tsa-ni u-do-hi-yu-hi a-ma da-da-wo-s-di-s-gv-gi, ni-hi-s-gi-ni Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do do-da-ye-tsa-wo-s-ta-ni.

051117.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏧᏁᎸᎯ ᏱᎩ ᎤᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏥᎩᏁᎸᎩ ᎠᏴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎡᏙᎢᏳᏅᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ, ᎦᏙ ᎠᏴ ᎨᏎ ᏰᎵ ᎦᏥᏲᏍᏙᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏎ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ.
051117.english: Forasmuch then as God gave them the like gift as he did unto us, who believed on the Lord Jesus Christ; what was I, that I could withstand God?
051117.phonetic: I-yu-no U-ne-la-nv-hi tsu-ne-lv-hi yi-gi u-da-ne-di ge-sv na-s-gi-ya tsi-gi-ne-lv-gi a-yv na-s-gi e-do-i-yu-nv-hi U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv, ga-do a-yv ge-se ye-li ga-tsi-yo-s-do-da-ne-di ge-se U-ne-la-nv-hi.

051118.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎡᎳᏪ ᎤᏅᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᎸᏉᏔᏁ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ:  ᎾᏍᏉᎧᏂ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᏕᎤᏁᎸ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛ ᏧᏂᏁᏟᏴᏍᏗᏱ ᎬᏂᏛ ᏩᎵᏱᎶᎯᎯ.
051118.english: When they heard these things, they held their peace, and glorified God, saying, Then hath God also to the Gentiles granted repentance unto life.
051118.phonetic: U-na-dv-ga-nv-no na-s-gi e-la-we u-nv-ne-i, a-le u-ni-lv-quo-ta-ne U-ne-la-nv-hi, hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i:  na-s-quo-ka-ni U-ne-la-nv-hi tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi de-u-ne-lv du-na-da-nv-dv tsu-ni-ne-tli-yv-s-di-yi gv-ni-dv wa-li-yi-lo-hi-hi.

051119.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏗᎦᎴᏲᏨᎯᏃ ᎾᎯᏳ ᎤᏲ ᏥᏄᎾᎵᏍᏓᏁᎴ, ᏨᏧᏓᎴᏅᎮ ᏍᏗᏫ, ᏈᏂᏏ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏭᏁᏙᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏌᏈ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏘᎣᎩ, ᎠᏂᏧᏏᏉ ᎤᏅᏒ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᏓᏂᏃᎮᎮᎮᎢ.
051119.english: Now they which were scattered abroad upon the persecution that arose about Stephen travelled as far as Phenice, and Cyprus, and Antioch, preaching the word to none but unto the Jews only.
051119.phonetic: U-na-di-ga-le-yo-tsv-hi-no na-hi-yu u-yo tsi-nu-na-li-s-da-ne-le, tsv-tsu-da-le-nv-he S-di-wi, Qui-ni-si i-yv-dv wu-ne-do-le-i, a-le Sa-qui, a-le V-ti-o-gi, A-ni-tsu-si-quo u-nv-sv ka-no-he-dv da-ni-no-he-he-he-i.

051120.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎢᎦᏛ ᏌᏈ ᎠᎴ ᏌᎵᏂ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎨᏎᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎥᏘᎣᎩ ᏭᏂᎷᏨ ᏚᏂᏬᏁᏔᏁ ᎠᏂᎪᎢ, ᎠᏂᏃᎮᏍᎨ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ.
051120.english: And some of them were men of Cyprus and Cyrene, which, when they were come to Antioch, spake unto the Grecians, preaching the Lord Jesus.
051120.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no i-ga-dv Sa-qui a-le Sa-li-ni a-ne-hi ge-se-i, na-s-gi V-ti-o-gi wu-ni-lu-tsv du-ni-wo-ne-ta-ne A-ni-go-i, a-ni-no-he-s-ge o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa u-gv-wa-li.

051121.cherokee: ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯᏃ ᎤᏬᏰᏂ ᏔᎵ ᎤᏛᏗᏕᎨᎢ, ᎤᏂᏤᏖᏃ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏁᎢ, ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯᏃ ᎢᏗᏢ ᏭᎾᎦᏔᎲᏍᏔᏁᎢ.
051121.english: And the hand of the Lord was with them: and a great number believed, and turned unto the Lord.
051121.phonetic: U-gv-wi-yu-hi-no u-wo-ye-ni ta-li u-dv-di-de-ge-i, u-ni-tse-te-no u-no-hi-yu-ne-i, U-gv-wi-yu-hi-no i-di-tlv wu-na-ga-ta-hv-s-ta-ne-i.

051122.cherokee: ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗᏃ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏁᎢ, ᎤᏂᏅᏎᏃ ᏆᏂᏆ ᎥᏖᎣᎩ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏭᏪᏓᏍᏗᏱ.
051122.english: Then tidings of these things came unto the ears of the church which was in Jerusalem: and they sent forth Barnabas, that he should go as far as Antioch.
051122.phonetic: Tsu-na-ne-lo-di-no u-na-da-tlu-gv Tsi-lu-si-li-mi a-ne-hi na-s-gi hi-a u-na-dv-ga-ne-i, u-ni-nv-se-no Qua-ni-qua V-te-o-gi i-yv-dv wu-we-da-s-di-yi.

051123.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏭᎷᏨ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎪᎲ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎤᎵᎮᎵᏤᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏔᏲᏎᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏛ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏓᏗᏍᏗᏱ ᏧᏂᏂᏴᏗᏱ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ.
051123.english: Who, when he came, and had seen the grace of God, was glad, and exhorted them all, that with purpose of heart they would cleave unto the Lord.
051123.phonetic: Na-s-gi wu-lu-tsv a-le u-go-hv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv-i, u-li-he-li-tse-i, a-le du-ta-yo-se-le ni-ga-dv u-li-ni-gi-dv u-na-da-nv-da-di-s-di-yi tsu-ni-ni-yv-di-yi U-gv-wi-yu-hi.

051124.cherokee: ᎤᏓᏅᏘᏰᏃ ᎨᏎ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎧᎵᏨᎯ ᎨᏎ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎠᎴ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.  ᎤᏂᏣᏖᏃ ᏴᏫ ᎬᏩᏁᏉᏤᎴ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ.
051124.english: For he was a good man, and full of the Holy Ghost and of faith: and much people was added unto the Lord.
051124.phonetic: U-da-nv-ti-ye-no ge-se a-s-ga-ya, a-le u-ka-li-tsv-hi ge-se Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do a-le go-hi-yu-di ge-sv-i.  U-ni-tsa-te-no yv-wi gv-wa-ne-quo-tse-le U-gv-wi-yu-hi.

051125.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏆᏂᏆ ᏓᏌ ᏭᎶᏎ ᎤᏲᎸᏎ ᏐᎳ;
051125.english: Then departed Barnabas to Tarsus, for to seek Saul:
051125.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Qua-ni-qua Da-sa wu-lo-se u-yo-lv-se So-la;

051126.cherokee: ᎤᏩᏛᎲᏃ ᎥᏖᎣᎩ ᎤᏘᏃᎴᎢ.  ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏑᏕᏘᏴᏛ ᎾᎿᎭᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬ ᏓᏂᎳᏫᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏖ ᏴᏫ ᏚᏁᏲᏁᎢ.  ᎥᏖᎣᎩᏃ ᎢᎬᏱᏱ ᎠᏂᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎨᎪᏎᎴ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ.
051126.english: And when he had found him, he brought him unto Antioch. And it came to pass, that a whole year they assembled themselves with the church, and taught much people. And the disciples were called Christians first in Antioch.
051126.phonetic: U-wa-dv-hv-no V-te-o-gi u-ti-no-le-i.  Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-ne-i, na-s-gi su-de-ti-yv-dv na-hna tsu-na-ne-lo-di u-na-da-tlu-gv da-ni-la-wi-ge-i, a-le u-ni-tsa-te yv-wi du-ne-yo-ne-i.  V-te-o-gi-no i-gv-yi-yi A-ni-ga-lo-ne-dv ge-go-se-le a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi.

051127.cherokee: ᎾᎯᏳᏃ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏅᏓᏳᏂᎶᏒᎯ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎤᏂᎷᏤ ᎥᏖᎣᎩ.
051127.english: And in these days came prophets from Jerusalem unto Antioch.
051127.phonetic: Na-hi-yu-no Tsi-lu-si-li-mi nv-da-yu-ni-lo-sv-hi a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi u-ni-lu-tse V-te-o-gi.

051128.cherokee: ᎠᏏᏴᏫᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎡᎩᏩ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ ᏕᎤᎴᏁᎢ, ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎤᏩᏔᏁ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏄᏩᏁᎴ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᎪᏄᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎬᎾᏛᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎩ ᎾᎯᏳ ᎦᎶᏗ ᏏᏌ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᎨᏒᎩ.
051128.english: And there stood up one of them named Agabus, and signified by the spirit that there should be great dearth throughout all the world: which came to pass in the days of Claudius Caesar.
051128.phonetic: A-si-yv-wi-no na-s-gi E-gi-wa tsu-do-i-dv de-u-le-ne-i, a-da-nv-do u-wa-ta-ne gv-ni-ge-sv nu-wa-ne-le u-tsa-ti u-go-nu-lo-hi-s-di-yi ni-gv-na-dv-i; na-s-gi-no nu-li-s-ta-nv-gi na-hi-yu Ga-lo-di Si-sa u-gv-wi-yu-hi tsi-ge-sv-gi.

051129.cherokee: ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩᏃ ᎠᏂᏏᏴᏫᎭ ᏰᎵ ᎢᎬᏩᎾᏛᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏚᏄᎪᏔᏁ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏕᎸᏙᏗ ᎠᎾᎵᏅᏟ ᏧᏗᏱ ᎠᏁᎯ.
051129.english: Then the disciples, every man according to his ability, determined to send relief unto the brethren which dwelt in Judaea:
051129.phonetic: A-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi-no a-ni-si-yv-wi-ha ye-li i-gv-wa-na-dv-ne-di ge-sv du-nu-go-ta-ne u-na-da-nv-s-di-yi u-na-li-s-de-lv-do-di a-na-li-nv-tli Tsu-di-yi a-ne-hi.

051130.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᎾᏛᏁᎴᎢ, ᏫᏚᏂᏁᎴ ᏗᎨᎦᏁᎶᏗ ᏆᏂᏆ ᎠᎴ ᏐᎳ ᏚᎾᎨᏅᏕᎢ.
051130.english: Which also they did, and sent it to the elders by the hands of Barnabas and Saul.
051130.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no nu-na-dv-ne-le-i, wi-du-ni-ne-le di-ge-ga-ne-lo-di Qua-ni-qua a-le So-la du-na-ge-nv-de-i.

051201.cherokee: ᎾᎯᏳᏃ ᎡᎶᏛ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᎴᏅᎮ ᏚᏕᏯᏙᏔᏁ ᎢᎦᏛ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬᎢ.
051201.english: Now about that time Herod the king stretched forth his hands to vex certain of the church.
051201.phonetic: Na-hi-yu-no E-lo-dv u-gv-wi-yu-hi u-le-nv-he du-de-ya-do-ta-ne i-ga-dv tsu-na-ne-lo-di u-na-da-tlu-gv-i.

051202.cherokee: ᎠᏰᎳᏍᏗᏃ ᎦᏅᎯᏛ ᎤᏩᏔᏁ ᎤᎴ ᏥᎻ ᏣᏂ ᏗᎾᏓᏅᏟ.
051202.english: And he killed James the brother of John with the sword.
051202.phonetic: A-ye-la-s-di-no ga-nv-hi-dv u-wa-ta-ne u-le Tsi-mi Tsa-ni di-na-da-nv-tli.

051203.cherokee: ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒᏃ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎠᏂᏰᎸᏍᎬ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ, ᏈᏓ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏂᏴᎮᎢ.  ᎾᎯᏳᏃ ᎾᎪᏔᏅᎾ ᎦᏚ ᎠᎩᏍᏗᏱ ᎨᏎᎢ.
051203.english: And because he saw it pleased the Jews, he proceeded further to take Peter also. (Then were the days of unleavened bread.)
051203.phonetic: U-do-le-ho-sv-no o-si-yu a-ni-ye-lv-s-gv A-ni-tsu-si, Qui-da na-s-quo u-ni-yv-he-i.  Na-hi-yu-no na-go-ta-nv-na ga-du a-gi-s-di-yi ge-se-i.

051204.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏴᎲᏃ ᏗᏓᏍᏚᏗᏱ ᎤᏴᏔᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏲᎯᏎᎴ ᏅᎩ ᎢᏗᏂᎢᏛ ᏅᎩ ᏄᎾᏓᏡᎬ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᎦᏘᏗᏍᎩ, ᎧᏃᎯᏰᎩ ᏗᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏱ ᎤᎶᏐᏅᎯ ᏴᏫ ᏧᎾᏄᎪᏫᏎᏗᏱ ᎤᏰᎸᏎᎢ.
051204.english: And when he had apprehended him, he put him in prison, and delivered him to four quaternions of soldiers to keep him; intending after Easter to bring him forth to the people.
051204.phonetic: U-ni-yv-hv-no di-da-s-du-di-yi u-yv-ta-ne-i, a-le du-yo-hi-se-le nv-gi i-di-ni-i-dv nv-gi nu-na-da-tlu-gv a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi gv-wa-ga-ti-di-s-gi, ka-no-hi-ye-gi di-li-s-da-yv-di-yi u-lo-so-nv-hi yv-wi tsu-na-nu-go-wi-se-di-yi u-ye-lv-se-i.

051205.cherokee: ᏈᏓᏃ ᎠᏥᏍᏚᎮᎢ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏗᏳ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏖ ᎠᎾᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎮ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏥᏍᏕᎸᏗᏱ.
051205.english: Peter therefore was kept in prison: but prayer was made without ceasing of the church unto God for him.
051205.phonetic: Qui-da-no a-tsi-s-du-he-i; a-se-no tsu-na-ne-lo-di u-na-da-tlu-gv u-li-ni-gi-di-yu u-na-da-nv-te a-na-da-do-li-s-da-ne-he U-ne-la-nv-hi na-s-gi a-tsi-s-de-lv-di-yi.

051206.cherokee: ᎾᎯᏳᏃ ᎡᎶᏛ ᎤᎾᏄᎪᏫᏏᏗᏎᎢ, ᎾᎯᏳ ᏒᏃᏱ ᏈᏓ ᎦᎵᎮ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᏓᏂᏅᎬᎢ, ᏔᎵ ᏧᏓᏕᏒᏛ ᏓᎦᎸᏍᏕᎢ, ᎠᏂᎦᏘᏯᏃ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏳᎶᏗ ᎠᏂᏂ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎨ ᏗᏓᏍᏚᏗᏱ.
051206.english: And when Herod would have brought him forth, the same night Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains: and the keepers before the door kept the prison.
051206.phonetic: Na-hi-yu-no E-lo-dv u-na-nu-go-wi-si-di-se-i, na-hi-yu sv-no-yi Qui-da ga-li-he a-ye-li a-ni-ta-li a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi da-ni-nv-gv-i, ta-li tsu-da-de-sv-dv da-ga-lv-s-de-i, a-ni-ga-ti-ya-no ga-lo-hi-s-di-yu-lo-di a-ni-ni a-ni-ya-wi-s-ge di-da-s-du-di-yi.

051207.cherokee: ᎬᏂᏳᏉᏃ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎤᎴᏂᎴ ᎦᏅᎬᎢ, ᎢᎦᏃ ᎤᏙᏤ ᏗᏓᏍᏚᏗᏱ; ᎤᏩᏂᎸᏃ ᏈᏓ ᎠᏍᏆᎨᏂ, ᏕᎤᎴᏔᏁᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏔᎴᎲᎦ ᏞᎩᏳ.  ᏧᏓᏕᏒᏛᏃ ᏚᏙᎠᏎ ᏧᏬᏰᏂ.
051207.english: And, behold, the angel of the Lord came upon him, and a light shined in the prison: and he smote Peter on the side, and raised him up, saying, Arise up quickly. And his chains fell off from his hands.
051207.phonetic: Gv-ni-yu-quo-no di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi U-gv-wi-yu-hi u-tse-li u-le-ni-le ga-nv-gv-i, i-ga-no u-do-tse di-da-s-du-di-yi; u-wa-ni-lv-no Qui-da a-s-qua-ge-ni, de-u-le-ta-ne-i, hi-a nu-we-se-i; Ta-le-hv-ga tle-gi-yu.  Tsu-da-de-sv-dv-no du-do-a-se tsu-wo-ye-ni.

051208.cherokee: ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎭᏓᏠᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᏔᎳᏑᎸᎦ.  ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᏛᏁᎴᎢ.  ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎭᏄᏬᏣ ᎠᎴ ᏍᎩᏍᏓᏩᏚᎦ.
051208.english: And the angel said unto him, Gird thyself, and bind on thy sandals. And so he did. And he saith unto him, Cast thy garment about thee, and follow me.
051208.phonetic: Di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi-no hi-a nu-we-se-le-i; Ha-da-tlo-ga, a-le ta-la-su-lv-ga.  Na-s-gi-no nu-dv-ne-le-i.  Hi-a-no nu-we-se-le-i; Ha-nu-wo-tsa a-le s-gi-s-da-wa-du-ga.

051209.cherokee: ᎤᏄᎪᏨᏃ ᎤᏍᏓᏩᏛᏎᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏯᎦᏔᎮ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ ᎨᏒ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᏄᏛᏁᎸᎢ, ᎠᏆᏁᎳᏫᏎᏉ ᎡᎵᏍᎨᎢ.
051209.english: And he went out, and followed him; and wist not that it was true which was done by the angel; but thought he saw a vision.
051209.phonetic: U-nu-go-tsv-no u-s-da-wa-dv-se-i; a-le v-tla ya-ga-ta-he u-do-hi-yu-hi ge-sv di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi nu-dv-ne-lv-i, a-qua-ne-la-wi-se-quo e-li-s-ge-i.

051210.cherokee: ᎢᎬᏱᏱᏃ ᎠᎴ ᏔᎵᏁ, ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎬ ᎤᏂᎶᏒ ᏭᏂᎷᏤ ᏔᎷᎩᏍᎩ ᏗᏍᏚᏛ ᏗᎦᏚᎲ ᏫᎦᎾᏄᎪᎢᏍᏗᏍᎬᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏩᏒᏉ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᏚᎢᎡᎴᎢ.  ᎤᏂᏄᎪᏨᏃ ᏌᏉ ᎦᎳᏅᏛ ᎤᏂᎶᏎᎢ, ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎤᏓᏓᎴᏔᏁᎢ.
051210.english: When they were past the first and the second ward, they came unto the iron gate that leadeth unto the city; which opened to them of his own accord: and they went out, and passed on through one street; and forthwith the angel departed from him.
051210.phonetic: I-gv-yi-yi-no a-le ta-li-ne, a-ni-ya-wi-s-gv u-ni-lo-sv wu-ni-lu-tse ta-lu-gi-s-gi di-s-du-dv di-ga-du-hv wi-ga-na-nu-go-i-s-di-s-gv-i, na-s-gi u-wa-sv-quo u-na-li-s-du-i-e-le-i.  U-ni-nu-go-tsv-no sa-quo ga-la-nv-dv u-ni-lo-se-i, gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi u-da-da-le-ta-ne-i.

051211.cherokee: ᏈᏓᏃ ᎤᏓᏅᏘᏌᏅ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎿᎭᏉ ᎦᏙᎴᎣᎯ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏧᏅᏒ ᎤᏤᎵ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏇᏓᎴᏒ ᎡᎶᏛ ᏥᏓᎩᏂᏴᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎤᏚᎩ ᏧᏅᏒ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ.
051211.english: And when Peter was come to himself, he said, Now I know of a surety, that the Lord hath sent his angel, and hath delivered me out of the hand of Herod, and from all the expectation of the people of the Jews.
051211.phonetic: Qui-da-no u-da-nv-ti-sa-nv hi-a nu-we-se-i; Hna-quo ga-do-le-o-hi u-do-hi-yu-hi U-gv-wi-yu-hi tsu-nv-sv u-tse-li di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi, a-le a-que-da-le-sv E-lo-dv tsi-da-gi-ni-yv-sv-i, a-le ni-ga-v u-du-gi tsu-nv-sv A-ni-tsu-si.

051212.cherokee: ᎤᏓᏅᏖᎸᏃ ᏭᎷᏤ ᏗᎦᏁᎸ ᎺᎵ, ᏣᏂ ᎹᎦ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ ᎤᏥ, ᎾᎿᎭᎤᏂᏣᏖ ᏓᏂᎳᏫᎡ ᎠᎾᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗᏍᎨᎢ.
051212.english: And when he had considered the thing, he came to the house of Mary the mother of John, whose surname was Mark; where many were gathered together praying.
051212.phonetic: U-da-nv-te-lv-no wu-lu-tse di-ga-ne-lv Me-li, Tsa-ni Ma-ga tsu-do-i-dv u-tsi, na-hna u-ni-tsa-te da-ni-la-wi-e a-na-da-do-li-s-di-s-ge-i.

051213.cherokee: ᏈᏗᏃ ᏭᏩᏂᎸ ᎠᏍᏚᎲ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᏛ ᎶᏗ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ ᏧᏛᏓᏍᏔᏅᏎᎢ;
051213.english: And as Peter knocked at the door of the gate, a damsel came to hearken, named Rhoda.
051213.phonetic: Qui-di-no wu-wa-ni-lv a-s-du-hv ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi, a-dv Lo-di tsu-do-i-dv tsu-dv-da-s-ta-nv-se-i;

051214.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏬᎵᏨ ᏈᏓ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎬᎢ ᎣᏒ ᎤᏰᎸᏒ ᏅᏓᎵᏍᏙᏔᏁ ᏄᏍᏚᎢᏒᎾᏉ ᎨᏎ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᏚᏍᏆᎸᏔᏁᏉ ᏭᏴᎴᎢ ᎠᎴ ᏭᏃᏁ ᏈᏓ ᎦᏙᎬ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏳᎶᏗ.
051214.english: And when she knew Peter's voice, she opened not the gate for gladness, but ran in, and told how Peter stood before the gate.
051214.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-wo-li-tsv Qui-da ga-wo-ni-s-gv-i o-sv u-ye-lv-sv nv-da-li-s-do-ta-ne nu-s-du-i-sv-na-quo ge-se ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi, du-s-qua-lv-ta-ne-quo wu-yv-le-i a-le wu-no-ne Qui-da ga-do-gv ga-lo-hi-s-di-yu-lo-di.

051215.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᏣᎸᏃᏘᏉ.  ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏍᏓᏱᏕᏉ ᎧᏁᎬᎢ, ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏍᏗ, ᎤᏛᏁᎢ.  ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᏓᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯᏉ.
051215.english: And they said unto her, Thou art mad. But she constantly affirmed that it was even so. Then said they, It is his angel.
051215.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-gv-wa-we-se-le-i, Tsa-lv-no-ti-quo.  A-se-no u-s-da-yi-de-quo ka-ne-gv-i, U-do-hi-yu-hi na-s-gi nu-s-di, u-dv-ne-i.  Hi-a-no nu-ni-we-se-i; da-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi-quo.

051216.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏈᏓ ᎠᏏᏉ ᏗᎬᏂᎮᎢ; ᎤᏂᏍᏚᎢᏒᏃ ᎬᏩᎪᎮᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏎᎢ.
051216.english: But Peter continued knocking: and when they had opened the door, and saw him, they were astonished.
051216.phonetic: A-se-no Qui-da a-si-quo di-gv-ni-he-i; u-ni-s-du-i-sv-no gv-wa-go-he-i a-le u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-se-i.

051217.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏬᏰᏂ ᎤᏖᎸᏅ ᎡᎳᏪ ᎤᏅᏗᏱ, ᏚᏃᏁᎴ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏄᎪᏫᏒ ᏗᏓᏍᏚᏗᏱ.  ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏫᏗᏥᏃᎲᏏ ᏥᎻ ᎠᎴ ᏦᏣᏓᏅᏟ.  ᎤᏄᎪᏤᏃ ᎢᎸᎯᏢ ᏭᎶᏎᎢ.
051217.english: But he, beckoning unto them with the hand to hold their peace, declared unto them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. And he said, Go shew these things unto James, and to the brethren. And he departed, and went into another place.
051217.phonetic: A-se-no u-wo-ye-ni u-te-lv-nv e-la-we u-nv-di-yi, du-no-ne-le nu-li-s-ta-nv U-gv-wi-yu-hi u-nu-go-wi-sv di-da-s-du-di-yi.  A-le hi-a nu-we-se-i; Hi-a na-s-gi wi-di-tsi-no-hv-si Tsi-mi a-le tso-tsa-da-nv-tli.  U-nu-go-tse-no i-lv-hi-tlv wu-lo-se-i.

051218.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏍᏗᏉ ᏳᎾᏕᏯᏔᏁᎴ ᎠᏂᏯᏫᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏈᏓ ᏫᏄᏛᏁᏔᏅᎢ.
051218.english: Now as soon as it was day, there was no small stir among the soldiers, what was become of Peter.
051218.phonetic: I-ga-no nu-li-s-ta-nv v-tla u-s-di-quo yu-na-de-ya-ta-ne-le a-ni-ya-wi-s-gi na-s-gi Qui-da wi-nu-dv-ne-ta-nv-i.

051219.cherokee: ᎡᎶᏛᏃ ᎤᏲᎸ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏠᏨ ᏚᎪᎵᏰᎡ ᎬᏩᎦᏘᏛᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᏤ ᏧᏂᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗᏱ.  ᏧᏗᏱᏃ ᎤᏓᏅᏒ, ᏏᏌᎵᏱ ᏭᏕᏁᎢ.
051219.english: And when Herod had sought for him, and found him not, he examined the keepers, and commanded that they should be put to death. And he went down from Judaea to Caesarea, and there abode.
051219.phonetic: E-lo-dv-no u-yo-lv a-le u-tlo-tsv du-go-li-ye-e gv-wa-ga-ti-dv-hi a-le u-ne-tse tsu-ni-yo-hu-hi-s-di-yi.  Tsu-di-yi-no u-da-nv-sv, Si-sa-li-yi wu-de-ne-i.

051220.cherokee: ᎡᎶᏛᏃ ᏚᏔᎳᏬᎯᏎᎴ ᏓᏯ ᎠᎴ ᏌᏙᏂ ᎠᏁᎯ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᏌᏉ ᎢᎦᎦᏛ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎴᎢ, ᎤᎾᎵᎢᏛᏃ ᏆᎳᏍᏓ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏂᏏᏗᏱ ᎠᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗ, ᏙᎯᏱ ᎢᏳᏅᏁᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏔᏲᏎᎴᎢ; ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎪᎯᏰᏃ ᎤᎵᏍᏕᎸᏙᏕ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎪᎯ.
051220.english: And Herod was highly displeased with them of Tyre and Sidon: but they came with one accord to him, and, having made Blastus the king's chamberlain their friend, desired peace; because their country was nourished by the king's country.
051220.phonetic: E-lo-dv-no du-ta-la-wo-hi-se-le Da-ya a-le Sa-do-ni a-ne-hi; a-se-no sa-quo i-ga-ga-dv gv-wa-lu-tse-le-i, u-na-li-i-dv-no Qua-la-s-da u-gv-wi-yu-hi u-ni-si-di-yi a-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-di, do-hi-yi i-yu-nv-ne-di-yi u-ni-ta-yo-se-le-i; u-na-tse-li-go-hi-ye-no u-li-s-de-lv-do-de u-gv-wi-yu-hi u-tse-li-go-hi.

051221.cherokee: ᏓᏒᏍᏛᏃ ᎤᏍᏆᎸᎲ ᎡᎶᏛ ᎤᏄᏬᎥ ᎤᏂᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᎾᏄᏬᏍᏗ, ᎤᏪᏁ ᎢᏅ ᎢᎦᏘ ᎦᏍᎩᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎵᏥᏙᏁᎴᎢ.
051221.english: And upon a set day Herod, arrayed in royal apparel, sat upon his throne, and made an oration unto them.
051221.phonetic: Da-sv-s-dv-no u-s-qua-lv-hv E-lo-dv u-nu-wo-v u-ni-gv-wi-yu-hi u-na-nu-wo-s-di, u-we-ne i-nv i-ga-ti ga-s-gi-lv-i, a-le du-li-tsi-do-ne-le-i.

051222.cherokee: ᏴᏫᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᏁᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎧᏁᎦ, ᎥᏝᏃ ᏴᏫ.
051222.english: And the people gave a shout, saying, It is the voice of a god, and not of a man.
051222.phonetic: Yv-wi-no u-ne-la-ne-i, hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; Ga-lv-la-di e-hi ka-ne-ga, v-tla-no yv-wi.

051223.cherokee: ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎯ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎤᏩᏂᎴᎢ, ᏅᏧᎵᏍᏙᏔᏁ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏄᎸᏉᏔᏅᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.  ᏥᏍᎪᏴᏃ ᎬᏩᏯᎥ ᎤᏲᎱᏎᎢ.
051223.english: And immediately the angel of the Lord smote him, because he gave not God the glory: and he was eaten of worms, and gave up the ghost.
051223.phonetic: Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv di-ka-hna-wa-di-do-hi U-gv-wi-yu-hi u-tse-li u-wa-ni-le-i, nv-tsu-li-s-do-ta-ne U-ne-la-nv-hi nu-lv-quo-ta-nv-na ge-sv-i.  Tsi-s-go-yv-no gv-wa-ya-v u-yo-hu-se-i.

051224.cherokee: ᎧᏃᎮᏛᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎠᏛᏃᏍᎨᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎧᏁᏉᎨᎢ.
051224.english: But the word of God grew and multiplied.
051224.phonetic: Ka-no-he-dv-s-gi-ni U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li a-dv-no-s-ge-i a-le ka-ne-quo-ge-i.

051225.cherokee: ᏆᏂᏉᏃ ᎠᎴ ᏐᎳ ᏭᏂᏍᏆᏛ ᎨᏥᏁᏤᎸᎢ, ᎤᏂᎷᏤ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏗᎤᏂᎶᏎᎢ, ᎤᎾᏘᏃᎴᏃ ᏣᏂ ᎹᎦ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ.
051225.english: And Barnabas and Saul returned from Jerusalem, when they had fulfilled their ministry, and took with them John, whose surname was Mark.
051225.phonetic: qua-ni-quo-no a-le so-la wu-ni-s-qua-dv ge-tsi-ne-tse-lv-i, u-ni-lu-tse tsi-lu-si-li-mi di-u-ni-lo-se-i, u-na-ti-no-le-no tsa-ni ma-ga tsu-do-i-dv.

051301.cherokee: ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗᏃ ᎥᏘᎣᎩ ᏧᎾᏓᏡᎬ ᎠᏁᎲᎩ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎾᏕᏲᎲᏍᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏆᏂᏆ, ᎠᎴ ᏏᎻᏂ ᎾᎦ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᎷᏏᏯ ᏌᎵᏂ ᎡᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎹᏁᏂ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᏧᎾᏛᏒᎯ ᎡᎶᏛ ᏅᎩ ᎢᎦᏚᎩ ᎨᏒ ᏍᎦᏚᎩ ᎠᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏐᎳ.
051301.english: Now there were in the church that was at Antioch certain prophets and teachers; as Barnabas, and Simeon that was called Niger, and Lucius of Cyrene, and Manaen, which had been brought up with Herod the tetrarch, and Saul.
051301.phonetic: Tsu-na-ne-lo-di-no V-ti-o-gi tsu-na-da-tlu-gv a-ne-hv-gi gi-lo i-yu-na-s-di a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi a-le di-na-de-yo-hv-s-gi, na-s-gi Qua-ni-qua, a-le Si-mi-ni Na-ga tsu-do-i-dv, a-le Lu-si-ya Sa-li-ni e-hi, a-le Ma-ne-ni na-s-gi i-tsu-la-ha tsu-na-dv-sv-hi E-lo-dv nv-gi i-ga-du-gi ge-sv s-ga-du-gi a-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-di, a-le So-la.

051302.cherokee: ᎠᎾᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎲᏃ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎹᏟ ᎠᏅᏍᎬᎢ, ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏗᏍᎩᏯᏓᏓᎴᏓᏏ ᏆᏂᏆ ᎠᎴ ᏐᎳ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᎦᏥᏯᏑᏰᏒ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ.
051302.english: As they ministered to the Lord, and fasted, the Holy Ghost said, Separate me Barnabas and Saul for the work whereunto I have called them.
051302.phonetic: A-na-da-do-li-s-da-ne-hv-no U-gv-wi-yu-hi a-le a-ma-tli a-nv-s-gv-i, Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do hi-a nu-we-se-i; Di-s-gi-ya-da-da-le-da-si Qua-ni-qua a-le So-la tsu-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi ga-tsi-ya-su-ye-sv u-gv-wa-li.

051303.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᎹᏟ ᎤᏅᏅ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏓᏙᎵᏍᏔᏅ ᎠᎴ ᏚᎾᏏᏔᏛ, ᏚᏂᏅᏎᎢ.
051303.english: And when they had fasted and prayed, and laid their hands on them, they sent them away.
051303.phonetic: Hna-quo-no a-ma-tli u-nv-nv a-le u-na-da-do-li-s-ta-nv a-le du-na-si-ta-dv, du-ni-nv-se-i.

051304.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎤᏂᏅᏒ, ᏏᎷᏏᏱ ᏭᏂᎶᏎᎢ, ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᏫᎤᏁᏅ ᏥᏳᎯ ᏫᎤᎾᏣᏁ ᏌᏈ ᏫᎤᏂᎷᏤᎢ.
051304.english: So they, being sent forth by the Holy Ghost, departed unto Seleucia; and from thence they sailed to Cyprus.
051304.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do u-ni-nv-sv, Si-lu-si-yi wu-ni-lo-se-i, na-hna-no wi-u-ne-nv tsi-yu-hi wi-u-na-tsa-ne Sa-qui wi-u-ni-lu-tse-i.

051305.cherokee: ᏌᎳᎻᏃ ᎠᏁᏙᎲᎢ ᎤᏂᏃᎮᎴ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏧᏂᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ, ᎠᎾᏘᏁᎮᏃ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏣᏂ ᎤᏂᏍᏕᎸᎯᏙᎯ.
051305.english: And when they were at Salamis, they preached the word of God in the synagogues of the Jews: and they had also John to their minister.
051305.phonetic: Sa-la-mi-no a-ne-do-hv-i u-ni-no-he-le A-ni-tsu-si tsu-ni-la-wi-i-s-di-yi ka-no-he-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga, a-na-ti-ne-he-no na-s-quo Tsa-ni u-ni-s-de-lv-hi-do-hi.

051306.cherokee: ᎤᏂᎶᏐᏅᏃ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᎠᎹᏰᎵ ᎨᏒᎢ ᎨᏆ ᎢᏴᏛ, ᎾᎿᎭᎤᏂᏩᏛᎮ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏧᏏ ᏆᏥᏌ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎠᏙᏂᏍᎩ, ᎤᏠᎾᏍᏗ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ,
051306.english: And when they had gone through the isle unto Paphos, they found a certain sorcerer, a false prophet, a Jew, whose name was Bar-jesus:
051306.phonetic: U-ni-lo-so-nv-no ni-gv-na-dv a-ma-ye-li ge-sv-i Ge-qua i-yv-dv, na-hna u-ni-wa-dv-he gi-lo i-yu-s-di A-tsu-si Qua-tsi-sa tsu-do-i-dv, a-do-ni-s-gi, u-tlo-na-s-di a-do-le-ho-s-gi,

051307.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏁᏙᎮ ᎾᎿᎭᎦᏓ ᎠᎲ ᎠᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗ ᏌᏥᏯ ᏉᎳ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ ᎠᎦᏔᎿᎭᎢ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏫᏚᏯᏅᎮ ᏆᏂᏆ ᎠᎴ ᏐᎳ ᎤᏔᏲᎴ ᎤᏛᎪᏗᏱ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ.
051307.english: Which was with the deputy of the country, Sergius Paulus, a prudent man; who called for Barnabas and Saul, and desired to hear the word of God.
051307.phonetic: Na-s-gi a-ne-do-he na-hna ga-da a-hv a-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-di Sa-tsi-ya Quo-la tsu-do-i-dv a-ga-ta-hna-i a-s-ga-ya, na-s-gi wi-du-ya-nv-he Qua-ni-qua a-le So-la u-ta-yo-le u-dv-go-di-yi ka-no-he-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga.

051308.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎡᎵᏑ ᎠᏙᏂᏍᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᏄᏍᏗ ᏕᎤᏙᎥ ᎠᏁᏢᏔᏅᎯ, ᎤᏂᎭᎷᎩᏍᎨᎢ, ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎨ ᎠᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗ ᎤᎦᏔᎲᎡᏗᏱ ᎤᏬᎯᏳᏒᎢ.
051308.english: But Elymas the sorcerer (for so is his name by interpretation) withstood them, seeking to turn away the deputy from the faith.
051308.phonetic: A-se-no E-li-su a-do-ni-s-gi, na-s-gi-ye-no nu-s-di de-u-do-v a-ne-tlv-ta-nv-hi, u-ni-ha-lu-gi-s-ge-i, u-du-li-s-ge a-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-di u-ga-ta-hv-e-di-yi u-wo-hi-yu-sv-i.

051309.cherokee: ᏐᎳᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏉᎳ ᏥᏚᏙᎥ, ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎤᎧᎵᏨᎯ ᎨᏎ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏯᏅᏒᎯ ᏚᎧᎿᎭᏁᎢ,
051309.english: Then Saul, (who also is called Paul,) filled with the Holy Ghost, set his eyes on him,
051309.phonetic: So-la-no na-s-gi na-s-quo Quo-la tsi-du-do-v, Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do u-ka-li-tsv-hi ge-se na-s-gi u-ya-nv-sv-hi du-ka-hna-ne-i,

051310.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎦᎶᏄᎮᏛ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎫᏥᏛ ᏣᎧᎵᏨᎯ, ᎠᏍᎩᎾ ᎤᏪᏥ, ᎭᏡᏗᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᎥ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏝᏍᎪ ᏴᏘᏑᎵᎪᏥ ᎤᏣᏘᏂ ᏂᏕᎲᏁᎲ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᏅᏃᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏧᏤᎵᎦ?
051310.english: And said, O full of all subtilty and all mischief, thou child of the devil, thou enemy of all righteousness, wilt thou not cease to pervert the right ways of the Lord?
051310.phonetic: Hi-a nu-we-se-i; Ga-lo-nu-he-dv a-le u-ne-gu-tsi-dv tsa-ka-li-tsv-hi, A-s-gi-na u-we-tsi, ha-tlu-di-s-gi ni-ga-v du-yu-go-dv ge-sv-i, tla-s-go yv-ti-su-li-go-tsi u-tsa-ti-ni ni-de-hv-ne-hv du-yu-go-dv nv-no-hi U-ne-la-nv-hi tsu-tse-li-ga?

051311.cherokee: Ꭷ ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏣᏏᏔᏓ, ᎠᎴ ᏙᏘᎨᏩᏥ ᎥᏝ ᏱᎪᏩᏘᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᏅᏙ ᎢᎸᏍᎩ ᎢᎪᎯᏛ.  ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎤᎵᏏᎩ ᎤᏭᏢᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎡᏙᎮ ᏚᏲᎮ ᎩᎶ ᎬᏬᏱᏂᏙᎯ.
051311.english: And now, behold, the hand of the Lord is upon thee, and thou shalt be blind, not seeing the sun for a season. And immediately there fell on him a mist and a darkness; and he went about seeking some to lead him by the hand.
051311.phonetic: Ka gv-ni-yu-quo U-gv-wi-yu-hi tsa-si-ta-da, a-le do-ti-ge-wa-tsi v-tla yi-go-wa-ti-s-ge-s-di nv-do i-lv-s-gi i-go-hi-dv.  Gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv u-li-si-gi u-wu-tlv-ne-i, a-le e-do-he du-yo-he gi-lo gv-wo-yi-ni-do-hi.

051312.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗ ᎤᎪᎲ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎢ, ᎤᏬᎯᏳᏁᎢ, ᎠᏍᏆᏂᎪᏍᎨ ᏄᏍᏛ ᏓᎾᏕᏲᎲᏍᎬ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ.
051312.english: Then the deputy, when he saw what was done, believed, being astonished at the doctrine of the Lord.
051312.phonetic: Hna-quo-no a-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-di u-go-hv na-s-gi nu-li-s-ta-nv-i, u-wo-hi-yu-ne-i, a-s-qua-ni-go-s-ge nu-s-dv da-na-de-yo-hv-s-gv U-gv-wi-yu-hi u-tse-li-ga.

051313.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏉᎳ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎨᏆ ᏫᎤᎾᏣᏅ ᏭᏂᎷᏤ ᏆᎩ ᎦᏚᎲ ᏆᎻᏈᎵᏱ.  ᏣᏂᏃ ᎤᎾᏓᏓᎴᏓᏁᎸ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏫᎤᎶᏎᎢ.
051313.english: Now when Paul and his company loosed from Paphos, they came to Perga in Pamphylia: and John departing from them returned to Jerusalem.
051313.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Quo-la a-le na-s-gi a-ne-hi Ge-qua wi-u-na-tsa-nv wu-ni-lu-tse Qua-gi ga-du-hv Qua-mi-qui-li-yi.  Tsa-ni-no u-na-da-da-le-da-ne-lv Tsi-lu-si-li-mi wi-u-lo-se-i.

051314.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏆᎩ ᏫᎤᎾᏂᎩᏒ ᏭᏂᎷᏤ ᎥᏘᎣᎩ ᎦᏚᎲ ᏆᏏᏗᏱ; ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬᏃ ᎢᎦ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᏭᏂᏴᎸ ᎤᎾᏅᏁᎢ.
051314.english: But when they departed from Perga, they came to Antioch in Pisidia, and went into the synagogue on the sabbath day, and sat down.
051314.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no Qua-gi wi-u-na-ni-gi-sv wu-ni-lu-tse V-ti-o-gi ga-du-hv Qua-si-di-yi; u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv-no i-ga di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi wu-ni-yv-lv u-na-nv-ne-i.

051315.cherokee: ᏚᏂᎪᎵᏰᎥᏃ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ, ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎨᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏎ ᎯᎠ ᏫᏂᏚᏂᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏯ ᎢᏓᏓᏅᏟ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏗᎨᏥᏬᏁᏙᏗ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏴᏫ, ᎢᏥᏁᎩ.
051315.english: And after the reading of the law and the prophets the rulers of the synagogue sent unto them, saying, Ye men and brethren, if ye have any word of exhortation for the people, say on.
051315.phonetic: Du-ni-go-li-ye-v-no di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di a-le a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi, di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi ge-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-di u-na-da-nv-se hi-a wi-ni-du-ni-we-se-le-i; I-tsi-s-ga-ya i-da-da-nv-tli, i-yu-no di-ge-tsi-wo-ne-do-di ge-se-s-di yv-wi, i-tsi-ne-gi.

051316.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᏚᎴᏅ, ᎤᏬᏰᏂᏃ ᎤᏖᎸᏅ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏯ ᎢᏏᎵ ᏧᏪᏥ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎢᏥᎾᏰᏍᎩ, ᎢᏣᏛᏓᏍᏓ.
051316.english: Then Paul stood up, and beckoning with his hand said, Men of Israel, and ye that fear God, give audience.
051316.phonetic: Quo-la-no du-le-nv, u-wo-ye-ni-no u-te-lv-nv, hi-a nu-we-se-i; I-tsi-s-ga-ya I-si-li tsu-we-tsi a-le U-ne-la-nv-hi i-tsi-na-ye-s-gi, i-tsa-dv-da-s-da.

051317.cherokee: ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎯᎠ ᏴᏫ ᎢᏏᎵ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ ᏚᏑᏰᏎ ᏗᎩᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏌᎳᏓᏁ ᎯᎠ ᏴᏫ ᎠᏁᏙᎯᏉ ᏥᎨᏎ ᎢᏥᏈᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎧᏃᎨᏂ ᎤᏌᎳᏛ ᎤᏩᏔᏁ ᎾᎿ ᎭᏙᏧᏄᎪᏫᏎᎢ.
051317.english: The God of this people of Israel chose our fathers, and exalted the people when they dwelt as strangers in the land of Egypt, and with an high arm brought he them out of it.
051317.phonetic: U-ne-la-nv-hi hi-a yv-wi I-si-li u-na-tse-li-ga du-su-ye-se di-gi-ga-yv-li-ge-i, a-le du-sa-la-da-ne hi-a yv-wi a-ne-do-hi-quo tsi-ge-se I-tsi-qui-yi, a-le ka-no-ge-ni u-sa-la-dv u-wa-ta-ne na-hna do-tsu-nu-go-wi-se-i.

051318.cherokee: ᏅᎦᏍᎪᎯᏃ ᏧᏕᏘᏴᏛ ᎢᎪᎯᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᎤᏪᎵᏎ ᏄᎾᏛᏁᎵᏙᎸ ᎢᎾᎨᎢ.
051318.english: And about the time of forty years suffered he their manners in the wilderness.
051318.phonetic: Nv-ga-s-go-hi-no tsu-de-ti-yv-dv i-go-hi-dv u-ne-la-gi u-we-li-se nu-na-dv-ne-li-do-lv i-na-ge-i.

051319.cherokee: ᏚᏒᏅᏃ ᎦᎵᏉᎩ ᎢᏳᎾᏓᎴᎩ ᏴᏫ ᎨᎾᏂ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏤᎵ ᎦᏙᎯ ᏚᏯᏙᎮᎴ ᏓᎾᏎᏍᎬᎢ.
051319.english: And when he had destroyed seven nations in the land of Chanaan, he divided their land to them by lot.
051319.phonetic: Du-sv-nv-no ga-li-quo-gi i-yu-na-da-le-gi yv-wi Ge-na-ni a-ne-hi, na-s-gi u-na-tse-li ga-do-hi du-ya-do-he-le da-na-se-s-gv-i.

051320.cherokee: ᎣᏂᏃ ᏚᏪᎧᏁᎴ ᏗᏄᎪᏗᏍᎩ ᏅᎩᏧᏈ ᎯᏍᎩᏍᎪᎯ ᏧᏕᏘᏴᏛ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎢᎪᎯᏛ, ᏌᎻ ᎠᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᏤᎮ ᎢᏯᏍᏘ.
051320.english: And after that he gave unto them judges about the space of four hundred and fifty years, until Samuel the prophet.
051320.phonetic: O-ni-no du-we-ka-ne-le di-nu-go-di-s-gi nv-gi-tsu-qui hi-s-gi-s-go-hi tsu-de-ti-yv-dv i-yv-dv i-go-hi-dv, Sa-mi a-do-le-ho-s-gi tse-he i-ya-s-ti.

051321.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎤᏂᏔᏲᎴ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ; ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯᏃ ᏚᏪᎧᏁᎴ ᏐᎳ ᎩᏌ ᎤᏪᏥ ᏇᏂ ᏧᏴᏫ, ᏅᎦᏍᎪᎯ ᏧᏕᏘᏴᏛ ᎢᎪᎯᏛ.
051321.english: And afterward they desired a king: and God gave unto them Saul the son of Cis, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, by the space of forty years.
051321.phonetic: Hna-quo-no u-ni-ta-yo-le u-gv-wi-yu-hi; U-ne-la-nv-hi-no du-we-ka-ne-le So-la Gi-sa u-we-tsi Que-ni tsu-yv-wi, nv-ga-s-go-hi tsu-de-ti-yv-dv i-go-hi-dv.

051322.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏪᎧᎲᏒ, ᏚᎾᏄᎪᏫᏎᎴ ᏕᏫ ᎤᎾᏤᎵ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏧᏁᎢᏍᏔᏁ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏥᏩᏛᎲ ᏕᏫ ᏤᏏ ᎤᏪᏥ, ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏥᏰᎸᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏛᏛᏁᎵ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎦᏓᏅᏖᏍᎬᎢ.
051322.english: And when he had removed him, he raised up unto them David to be their king; to whom also he gave testimony, and said, I have found David the son of Jesse, a man after mine own heart, which shall fulfill all my will.
051322.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-we-ka-hv-sv, du-na-nu-go-wi-se-le De-wi u-na-tse-li u-gv-wi-yu-hi i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi; na-s-gi na-s-quo tsu-ne-i-s-ta-ne hi-a tsi-nu-we-se-i; Tsi-wa-dv-hv De-wi Tse-si u-we-tsi, a-s-ga-ya o-s-dv tsi-ye-lv-i, na-s-gi nv-dv-dv-ne-li ni-ga-v ga-da-nv-te-s-gv-i.

051323.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᏢᏔᏅᏛ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎤᏚᎢᏍᏛᎢ ᏚᎾᏄᎪᏫᏎᎸ ᎢᏏᎵ ᎤᏂᏍᏕᎵᏍᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏌ;
051323.english: Of this man's seed hath God according to his promise raised unto Israel a Saviour, Jesus:
051323.phonetic: Na-s-gi u-ne-tlv-ta-nv-dv ge-sv U-ne-la-nv-hi na-s-gi-ya u-du-i-s-dv-i du-na-nu-go-wi-se-lv I-si-li u-ni-s-de-li-s-gi, na-s-gi Tsi-sa;

051324.cherokee: ᎠᏏᏉᏃ ᏂᎦᎷᎬᎾ ᏥᎨᏎᎢ, ᏣᏂ ᏧᎵᏥᏙᏁᎸᎯ ᎨᏎ ᎾᏂᎥ ᏴᏫ ᎢᏏᎵ, ᏧᏃᎮᎮᎸᎯ ᎨᏎ ᏗᏓᏬᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎦᏁᏟᏴᏍᏗ ᎣᏓᏅᏛ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ.
051324.english: When John had first preached before his coming the baptism of repentance to all the people of Israel.
051324.phonetic: A-si-quo-no ni-ga-lu-gv-na tsi-ge-se-i, Tsa-ni tsu-li-tsi-do-ne-lv-hi ge-se na-ni-v yv-wi I-si-li, tsu-no-he-he-lv-hi ge-se di-da-wo-s-di ge-sv ga-ne-tli-yv-s-di o-da-nv-dv u-gv-wa-li.

051325.cherokee: ᏣᏂᏃ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎠᎧᎵᎢᎮ ᏧᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎦᎪ ᏍᎩᏰᎵᏎᎭ?  ᎥᏝ ᎠᏴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏱᎩ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎣᏂ ᏓᏯᎢ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᎳᏑᎸ ᎥᏝ ᎠᏴ ᏰᎵ ᏗᎬᎩᎧᏁᏴᏗ ᏱᎩ.
051325.english: And as John fulfilled his course, he said, Whom think ye that I am? I am not he. But, behold, there cometh one after me, whose shoes of his feet I am not worthy to loose.
051325.phonetic: Tsa-ni-no hna-quo a-ka-li-i-he tsu-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv-i, hi-a nu-we-se-i; Ga-go s-gi-ye-li-se-ha?  V-tla a-yv na-s-gi yi-gi, a-se-no gv-ni-yu-quo o-ni da-ya-i na-s-gi du-la-su-lv v-tla a-yv ye-li di-gv-gi-ka-ne-yv-di yi-gi.

051326.cherokee: ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏯ ᎢᏓᏓᏅᏟ, ᎡᏆᎭᎻ ᏅᏓᏳᏁᏢᏔᏅᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏣᏓᏑᏰᏍᏗ ᎡᏥᎾᏰᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ, ᏂᎯ ᎢᏣᏛᎪᏗ ᎡᏥᏰᎸᎾᏁᎸ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎠᎵᏍᏕᎸᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎧᏃᎮᏍᎩ.
051326.english: Men and brethren, children of the stock of Abraham, and whosoever among you feareth God, to you is the word of this salvation sent.
051326.phonetic: I-tsi-s-ga-ya i-da-da-nv-tli, E-qua-ha-mi nv-da-yu-ne-tlv-ta-nv-dv, a-le gi-lo i-tsa-da-su-ye-s-di e-tsi-na-ye-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi, ni-hi i-tsa-dv-go-di e-tsi-ye-lv-na-ne-lv ka-no-he-dv na-s-gi hi-a a-li-s-de-lv-do-di ge-sv ka-no-he-s-gi.

051327.cherokee: ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻᏰᏃ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᎾᏤᎵ ᎤᏂᎬᏫᏳᏌᏕᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎬᏩᎦᏔᎲᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎾᏂᎦᏔᎲᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᏁᎬ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᏄᎾᏙᏓᏈᏒ ᏥᏓᏂᎪᎵᏰᎠ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᏃᎯᏳᏔᏅ ᏥᏚᏄᎪᏓᏁᎴᎢ.
051327.english: For they that dwell at Jerusalem, and their rulers, because they knew him not, nor yet the voices of the prophets which are read every sabbath day, they have fulfilled them in condemning him.
051327.phonetic: Tsi-lu-si-li-mi-ye-no a-ne-hi, a-le tsu-na-tse-li u-ni-gv-wi-yu-sa-de-gi, na-s-gi ni-gv-wa-ga-ta-hv-na ge-sv i-yu-s-di a-le na-s-quo na-ni-ga-ta-hv-na ge-sv i-yu-s-di a-ni-ne-gv a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi nu-na-do-da-qui-sv tsi-da-ni-go-li-ye-a, na-s-gi du-no-hi-yu-ta-nv tsi-du-nu-go-da-ne-le-i.

051328.cherokee: ᏄᏂᏩᏛᎲᎾᏃ ᎨᏎ ᏰᎵ ᎬᏩᏲᎱᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏂᏔᏲᏎᎴ ᏆᎴᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏥᎢᏍᏗᏱ.
051328.english: And though they found no cause of death in him, yet desired they Pilate that he should be slain.
051328.phonetic: Nu-ni-wa-dv-hv-na-no ge-se ye-li gv-wa-yo-hu-hi-s-di ge-sv-i, a-se-no u-ni-ta-yo-se-le Qua-le-di na-s-gi a-tsi-i-s-di-yi.

051329.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏛᏃ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎪᏪᎸ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏥᏃᎮᏍᎬᎢ, ᎤᎾᏕᏎ ᎠᏓ ᎨᏛᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏤᎵᏍᏛ ᎤᏂᏅᏁᎢ.
051329.english: And when they had fulfilled all that was written of him, they took him down from the tree, and laid him in a sepulchre.
051329.phonetic: U-ni-s-qua-dv-no ni-ga-v go-we-lv na-s-gi a-tsi-no-he-s-gv-i, u-na-de-se a-da ge-dv-i, a-le a-tse-li-s-dv u-ni-nv-ne-i.

051330.english: But God raised him from the dead:

051331.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏬᎯᏤ ᎬᏩᎪᏩᏘᏍᎨ ᎨᎵᎵ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏓᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏛ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᎤᏂᎷᏨᎯ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏤᎵ ᎬᏩᏃᎮᏍᎩ ᏴᏫ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲᎢ.
051331.english: And he was seen many days of them which came up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, who are his witnesses unto the people.
051331.phonetic: A-le u-wo-hi-tse gv-wa-go-wa-ti-s-ge Ge-li-li na-s-gi nv-da-gv-wa-s-da-wa-dv-dv i-tsu-la-ha Tsi-lu-si-li-mi u-ni-lu-tsv-hi; na-s-gi tsu-tse-li gv-wa-no-he-s-gi yv-wi a-ni-ga-ta-hv-i.

051332.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏂᎯ ᎢᏨᏃᎮᎮᎭ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᎨᏥᏚᎢᏍᏓᏁᎴ ᏗᎩᎦᏴᎵᎨᎢ,
051332.english: And we declare unto you glad tidings, how that the promise which was made unto the fathers,
051332.phonetic: A-se-no ni-hi i-tsv-no-he-he-ha o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv na-s-gi tsi-ge-tsi-du-i-s-da-ne-le di-gi-ga-yv-li-ge-i,

051333.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ ᏂᎬᏁᎸ ᎠᏴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏁᏥ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏌ ᏕᎤᎴᏔᏅᎢ; ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᎬᏅ ᏥᎪᏪᎳ ᏗᎧᏃᎩᏍᏗᏱ ᏔᎵᏁᎢ; "ᎠᏇᏥ ᏂᎯ, ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ ᏍᏆᏕᏁᎸ."
051333.english: God hath fulfilled the same unto us their children, in that he hath raised up Jesus again; as it is also written in the second psalm, Thou art my Son, this day have I begotten thee.
051333.phonetic: Na-s-gi u-ne-la-nv-hi u-do-hi-yu-hi ni-gv-ne-lv a-yv na-s-gi tsu-ne-tsi, na-s-gi Tsi-sa de-u-le-ta-nv-i; na-s-gi-ya na-s-quo hi-a tsi-ni-gv-nv tsi-go-we-la Di-ka-no-gi-s-di-yi ta-li-ne-i; "A-que-tsi ni-hi, go-hi i-ga s-qua-de-ne-lv."

051334.cherokee: ᎾᏃ ᏕᎤᎴᏔᏅᎢ ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎢ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᎪᎯᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᎤᏪᏎᎢ; "ᏓᏨᏁᎵ ᎤᏲᎢᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᏕᏫ."
051334.english: And as concerning that he raised him up from the dead, now no more to return to corruption, he said on this wise, I will give you the sure mercies of David.
051334.phonetic: Na-no de-u-le-ta-nv-i u-yo-hu-sv-i hna-quo u-go-hi-s-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i, hi-a ni-u-we-se-i; "Da-tsv-ne-li u-yo-i-s-di ni-ge-sv-na o-s-dv i-yu-li-s-da-ne-di De-wi."

051335.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏪᎠ ᏅᏩᏓᎴ ᎪᏪᎸᎢ; "ᎾᏍᎦᏅᎾ ᏣᏤᎵ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᏭᎪᎯ ᎦᎯᏰᎵᏎᏗ ᏱᎩ."
051335.english: Wherefore he saith also in another psalm, Thou shalt not suffer thine Holy One to see corruption.
051335.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di na-s-quo hi-a ni-ga-we-a nv-wa-da-le go-we-lv-i; "Na-s-ga-nv-na tsa-tse-li v-tla u-ne-la-gi wu-go-hi ga-hi-ye-li-se-di yi-gi."

051336.cherokee: ᏕᏫᏰᏃ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏚᏛᏁᎸ ᎾᎯᏳ ᎤᎾᏕᏅᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏓᏅᏖᏍᎬ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ, ᎤᎸᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᏓᏂᎾᎥ ᎠᏥᏅᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎪᏎᎢ.
051336.english: For David, after he had served his own generation by the will of God, fell on sleep, and was laid unto his fathers, and saw corruption:
051336.phonetic: De-wi-ye-no go-hu-s-di du-dv-ne-lv na-hi-yu u-na-de-nv-hi U-ne-la-nv-hi a-da-nv-te-s-gv na-s-gi-ya-i, u-lv-ne-i, a-le tsu-ga-yv-li-ge da-ni-na-v a-tsi-nv-ne-i, a-le u-go-se-i.

051337.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏧᎴᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎥᏝ ᏳᎪᏎᎢ.
051337.english: But he, whom God raised again, saw no corruption.
051337.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ni U-ne-la-nv-hi tsu-le-ta-nv-hi ge-sv v-tla yu-go-se-i.

051338.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᏣᏅᏖᏍᏗ ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏯ ᏗᏓᏓᏅᏟ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ [ᎠᏍᎦᏯ] ᎢᏳᏩᏂᏌᏛ ᎡᏥᏃᏁᎭ ᎦᏰᏥᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏅᏨᎢ.
051338.english: Be it known unto you therefore, men and brethren, that through this man is preached unto you the forgiveness of sins:
051338.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di i-tsa-nv-te-s-di i-tsi-s-ga-ya di-da-da-nv-tli, na-s-gi hi-a [a-s-ga-ya] i-yu-wa-ni-sa-dv e-tsi-no-ne-ha ga-ye-tsi-do-li-s-di ge-sv i-tsi-s-ga-nv-tsv-i.

051339.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᎭ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ ᎠᎾᏚᏓᎴᎭ ᏂᎦᎥ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎨᏣᏚᏓᎳᎡᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏥᎨᏒ ᎼᏏ ᎤᏤᎵ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᏕᏥᎧᎿᎭᏩᏗᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ.
051339.english: And by him all that believe are justified from all things, from which ye could not be justified by the law of Moses.
051339.phonetic: A-le na-s-gi nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-ha ni-ga-dv a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi a-na-du-da-le-ha ni-ga-v tsu-da-le-nv-dv, na-s-gi ge-tsa-du-da-la-e-di ni-ge-sv-na tsi-ge-sv Mo-si u-tse-li di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di de-tsi-ka-hna-wa-di-sv i-yu-s-di.

051340.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᏤᏯᏔᎮᏍᏗ ᏞᏍᏗ ᎢᏥᎷᏤᎸᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏥᎾᏂᏪ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ;
051340.english: Beware therefore, lest that come upon you, which is spoken of in the prophets;
051340.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di i-tse-ya-ta-he-s-di tle-s-di i-tsi-lu-tse-lv-gi hi-a tsi-na-ni-we a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi;

051341.cherokee: "ᏗᏣᎧᏅᎦ, ᎢᏥᏂᏆᏘᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏥᏍᏆᏂᎪᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏣᏗᏒᏅ; ᎠᏴᏰᏃ ᎪᎯ ᎢᏤᎲ ᏓᎩᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎥᏝ ᏴᎨᏦᎯᏳᎲᎦ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏎ ᎩᎶ ᏱᏥᏁᎮᎮᎸ."
051341.english: Behold, ye despisers, and wonder, and perish: for I work a work in your days, a work which ye shall in no wise believe, though a man declare it unto you.
051341.phonetic: "Di-tsa-ka-nv-ga, i-tsi-ni-qua-ti-hi, a-le i-tsi-s-qua-ni-go-hi a-le i-tsa-di-sv-nv; a-yv-ye-no go-hi i-tse-hv da-gi-lv-wi-s-da-ne di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di, na-s-gi di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di v-tla yv-ge-tso-hi-yu-hv-ga na-s-quo a-se gi-lo yi-tsi-ne-he-he-lv."

051342.cherokee: ᎠᏂᏧᏏᏃ ᎤᏂᏄᎪᏨ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ, ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏂᏔᏲᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎨᏥᏃᎮᎮᏗᏱ ᏔᎵᏁ ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬᎢ.
051342.english: And when the Jews were gone out of the synagogue, the Gentiles besought that these words might be preached to them the next sabbath.
051342.phonetic: A-ni-tsu-si-no u-ni-nu-go-tsv di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi, tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi u-ni-ta-yo-le na-s-gi hi-a ge-tsi-no-he-he-di-yi ta-li-ne u-na-do-da-qua-s-gv-i.

051343.cherokee: ᏓᏂᎳᏫᎥᏃ ᏚᏂᏰᎵᏒ ᎤᏂᏣᏖ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎾᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎯ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏧᎾᎵᎪᏁᎸᎯ ᏚᏂᏍᏓᏩᏛᏎ ᏉᎳ ᎠᎴ ᏆᏂᏆ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏚᏂᏬᏁᏔᏁ ᏚᏂᏔᏲᏎᎴ ᏧᏂᏲᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
051343.english: Now when the congregation was broken up, many of the Jews and religious proselytes followed Paul and Barnabas: who, speaking to them, persuaded them to continue in the grace of God.
051343.phonetic: Da-ni-la-wi-v-no du-ni-ye-li-sv u-ni-tsa-te A-ni-tsu-si a-le U-ne-la-nv-hi a-na-da-do-li-s-da-ne-hi A-ni-tsu-si tsu-na-li-go-ne-lv-hi du-ni-s-da-wa-dv-se Quo-la a-le Qua-ni-qua, na-s-gi-no du-ni-wo-ne-ta-ne du-ni-ta-yo-se-le tsu-ni-yo-hi-s-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na U-ne-la-nv-hi u-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv-i.

051344.cherokee: ᏔᎵᏁᏃ ᏫᎤᎾᏙᏓᏋ ᎠᎴᏉ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎦᏚᎲ ᏂᏚᏂᏔᏫᏤ ᎤᎾᏛᏓᏍᏔᏂᎴ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ.
051344.english: And the next sabbath day came almost the whole city together to hear the word of God.
051344.phonetic: Ta-li-ne-no wi-u-na-do-da-quv a-le-quo ni-ga-dv ga-du-hv ni-du-ni-ta-wi-tse u-na-dv-da-s-ta-ni-le ka-no-he-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga.

051345.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ, ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏚᏂᎪᎲ, ᎤᏂᎧᎵᏤ ᎠᏛᏳᎨᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏬᏂᏍᎬ ᎠᎾᏡᏗᏍᎨ ᏉᎳ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎬ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎬᎢ, ᎤᏂᎪᏁᎶᏍᎨᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏐᏢᎢᏍᏗᏍᎨᎢ.
051345.english: But when the Jews saw the multitudes, they were filled with envy, and spake against those things which were spoken by Paul, contradicting and blaspheming.
051345.phonetic: A-se-no A-ni-tsu-si, u-ni-tsa-ti du-ni-go-hv, u-ni-ka-li-tse a-dv-yu-ge-di ge-sv-i, a-le a-ni-wo-ni-s-gv a-na-tlu-di-s-ge Quo-la ni-ga-we-s-gv ga-wo-ni-s-gv-i, u-ni-go-ne-lo-s-ge-i a-le a-ni-so-tlv-i-s-di-s-ge-i.

051346.cherokee: ᎾᏉᏃ ᏉᎳ ᎠᎴ ᏆᏂᏆ ᎧᏁᏉᎨ ᎾᏂᏍᎦᎢᎲᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᏂᎯ ᎢᎬᏱ ᎠᏎ ᎡᏥᏃᎮᎮᏗ ᎨᏒᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎢᏢᏉ ᏥᏂᏨᏁᎭ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏨᏒ ᎢᏣᏓᏅᏖᏍᎬ ᏰᎵ ᎦᏰᏥᏁᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏥᏥᏰᎸ ᎬᏂᏛ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᏗᏁᎲ ᏬᏍᏓᎦᏔᎲᏍᏓ.
051346.english: Then Paul and Barnabas waxed bold, and said, It was necessary that the word of God should first have been spoken to you: but seeing ye put it from you, and judge yourselves unworthy of everlasting life, lo, we turn to the Gentiles.
051346.phonetic: Na-quo-no Quo-la a-le Qua-ni-qua ka-ne-quo-ge na-ni-s-ga-i-hv-na ge-sv-i, hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; Ni-hi i-gv-yi a-se e-tsi-no-he-he-di ge-sv-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li ka-no-he-dv; a-se-no i-tlv-quo tsi-ni-tsv-ne-ha a-le i-tsv-sv i-tsa-da-nv-te-s-gv ye-li ga-ye-tsi-ne-di ni-ge-sv-na tsi-tsi-ye-lv gv-ni-dv, gv-ni-yu-quo tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi di-ne-hv wo-s-da-ga-ta-hv-s-da.

051347.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᏄᏍᏗ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎣᎩᏂᏁᏤᎸ, [ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏪᎭ;] "ᎬᏯᎧᏅ ᎢᎦ ᏔᏘᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ, ᎤᎵᏍᏕᎸᏙᏗ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎡᎶᎯ ᏂᎬᎾᏛᎢ."
051347.english: For so hath the Lord commanded us, saying, I have set thee to be a light of the Gentiles, that thou shouldest be for salvation unto the ends of the earth.
051347.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ye-no nu-s-di U-gv-wi-yu-hi o-gi-ni-ne-tse-lv, [hi-a ni-ga-we-ha;] "Gv-ya-ka-nv i-ga ta-ti-s-da-ne-di-yi tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi, u-li-s-de-lv-do-di i-tsa-li-s-do-di-yi e-lo-hi ni-gv-na-dv-i."

051348.cherokee: ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛᏃ ᏴᏫ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ ᎤᎾᎵᎮᎵᏤᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᎸᏉᏔᏁ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏁ ᎾᏂᎥ ᎬᏂᏛ ᎤᏂᏩᏛᏗ ᎢᎨᎬᏁᎸᎯ.
051348.english: And when the Gentiles heard this, they were glad, and glorified the word of the Lord: and as many as were ordained to eternal life believed.
051348.phonetic: Tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv-no yv-wi na-s-gi u-na-dv-ga-nv u-na-li-he-li-tse-i, a-le u-ni-lv-quo-ta-ne U-gv-wi-yu-hi u-tse-li ka-no-he-dv, a-le u-no-hi-yu-ne na-ni-v gv-ni-dv u-ni-wa-dv-di i-ge-gv-ne-lv-hi.

051349.english: And the word of the Lord was published throughout all the region.
051349.phonetic: U-gv-wi-yu-hi-no u-tse-li ka-no-he-dv du-ye-li-se na-hna ni-gv-na-dv-i.

051350.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏚᏂᏖᎸᏁ ᎨᏥᎸᏉᏗ ᎠᏂᎨᏴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎾᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᎦᏚᎲ ᏗᎨᎦᏁᎶᏗ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏅᏂᏌᏁ ᎦᎨᏥᏐᏢᏙᏗᏱ ᏉᎳ ᎠᎴ ᏆᏂᏆ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏂᎨᎯᏙᎴ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
051350.english: But the Jews stirred up the devout and honourable women, and the chief men of the city, and raised persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and expelled them out of their coasts.
051350.phonetic: A-se-no A-ni-tsu-si du-ni-te-lv-ne ge-tsi-lv-quo-di a-ni-ge-yv U-ne-la-nv-hi a-na-da-do-li-s-da-ne-hi, a-le na-hna ga-du-hv di-ge-ga-ne-lo-di a-ni-s-ga-ya, a-le na-s-gi nu-nv-ni-sa-ne ga-ge-tsi-so-tlv-do-di-yi Quo-la a-le Qua-ni-qua, a-le du-ni-ge-hi-do-le u-na-tse-li-go-hi ge-sv-i.

051351.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏚᏂᏅᎪᎥᏓᏁᎴᏉ ᎪᏍᏚ ᏧᎾᎳᏏᏕᏂ ᎤᏓᏅᎵᏰᎥᎢ, ᎢᎪᏂᏯᏃ ᏭᏂᎷᏤᎢ.
051351.english: But they shook off the dust of their feet against them, and came unto Iconium.
051351.phonetic: A-se-no du-ni-nv-go-v-da-ne-le-quo go-s-du tsu-na-la-si-de-ni u-da-nv-li-ye-v-i, I-go-ni-ya-no wu-ni-lu-tse-i.

051352.cherokee: ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏂᎧᎵᏨᎯ ᎨᏎ ᎠᎾᎵᎮᎵᎬᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ.
051352.english: And the disciples were filled with joy, and with the Holy Ghost.
051352.phonetic: A-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi-no u-ni-ka-li-tsv-hi ge-se a-na-li-he-li-gv-i a-le Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do.

051401.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁ ᎢᎪᏂᏯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᏭᏂᏴᎴ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏧᏂᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎠᏂᏬᏂᏍᎬ ᏅᏧᎵᏍᏙᏔᏁ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏂᎪᎢ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏁᎢ.
051401.english: And it came to pass in Iconium, that they went both together into the synagogue of the Jews, and so spake, that a great multitude both of the Jews and also of the Greeks believed.
051401.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-ne I-go-ni-ya, na-s-gi i-tsu-la-ha wu-ni-yv-le A-ni-tsu-si tsu-ni-la-wi-i-s-di-yi, a-le nu-s-dv a-ni-wo-ni-s-gv nv-tsu-li-s-do-ta-ne u-ni-tsa-ti A-ni-tsu-si a-le na-s-quo A-ni-go-i u-no-hi-yu-ne-i.

051402.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏄᏃᎯᏳᏒᎾ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏚᏂᏖᎸᏁ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᏅᏂᏌᏁ ᏗᏂᏍᎦᎩ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁ ᎠᎾᎵᏅᏟ.
051402.english: But the unbelieving Jews stirred up the Gentiles, and made their minds evil affected against the brethren.
051402.phonetic: A-se-no nu-no-hi-yu-sv-na A-ni-tsu-si du-ni-te-lv-ne tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi du-na-da-nv-dv-i, na-s-gi-no nu-nv-ni-sa-ne di-ni-s-ga-gi nu-li-s-ta-ne a-na-li-nv-tli.

051403.cherokee: ᎤᏬᎯᏤᏃ ᎾᎿᎭᎤᏁᏙᎴᎢ, ᎾᏂᏍᎦᎢᎲᎾ ᎠᏂᏬᏂᏍᎨ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨ ᎤᎾᎵᏍᎦᏍᏙᏛ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏍᏓᏱᏗᏍᎨ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏣᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏍᎩ, ᏓᏁᎮ ᏧᏃᏰᏂ ᏧᏅᏙᏗᏱ ᎤᏰᎸᏛ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ.
051403.english: Long time therefore abode they speaking boldly in the Lord, which gave testimony unto the word of his grace, and granted signs and wonders to be done by their hands.
051403.phonetic: U-wo-hi-tse-no na-hna u-ne-do-le-i, na-ni-s-ga-i-hv-na a-ni-wo-ni-s-ge nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge u-na-li-s-ga-s-do-dv U-gv-wi-yu-hi, na-s-gi a-s-da-yi-di-s-ge ka-no-he-dv u-wa-sv u-da-do-li-tsa-dv ka-no-he-s-gi, da-ne-he tsu-no-ye-ni tsu-nv-do-di-yi u-ye-lv-dv a-le u-s-qua-ni-go-di tsu-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi.

051404.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᎾᎿᎭᎦᏚᎲ ᎠᏁᎯ ᏔᎵ ᏄᎾᏓᏕᎢ; ᎢᎦᏛ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏚᎾᎵᎪᏁᎴᎢ, ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎨᏥᏅᏏᏛ.
051404.english: But the multitude of the city was divided: and part held with the Jews, and part with the apostles.
051404.phonetic: U-ni-tsa-ti-no na-hna ga-du-hv a-ne-hi ta-li nu-na-da-de-i; i-ga-dv A-ni-tsu-si du-na-li-go-ne-le-i, i-ga-dv-no ge-tsi-nv-si-dv.

051405.cherokee: ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛᏃ ᏴᏫ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎨᎦᏁᎶᏗ ᎠᎾᏁᎷᎩᏍᎨ ᏗᎬᏩᏂᏐᏢᏙᏗᏱ ᎠᎴ ᏅᏯ ᏧᏅᏂᏍᏙᏗᏱ,
051405.english: And when there was an assault made both of the Gentiles, and also of the Jews with their rulers, to use them despitefully, and to stone them,
051405.phonetic: Tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv-no yv-wi a-le A-ni-tsu-si a-le di-ge-ga-ne-lo-di a-na-ne-lu-gi-s-ge di-gv-wa-ni-so-tlv-do-di-yi a-le nv-ya tsu-nv-ni-s-do-di-yi,

051406.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎾᎵᏘᏎᎢ, ᎵᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏓᏈ ᎵᎨᎣᏂᏯ ᏕᎦᏚᎲ ᏭᏂᎶᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᎬᏩᏚᏫᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ.
051406.english: They were ware of it, and fled unto Lystra and Derbe, cities of Lycaonia, and unto the region that lieth round about:
051406.phonetic: Na-s-gi u-na-dv-ga-ne-i, a-le du-na-li-ti-se-i, Li-s-di a-le Da-qui Li-ge-o-ni-ya de-ga-du-hv wu-ni-lo-se-i, a-le na-hna gv-wa-du-wi-dv ge-sv-i.

051407.english: And there they preached the gospel.

051408.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏬᎴ ᎵᏍᏗ ᎠᏲᎤᎵ ᏧᎳᏏᏕᏂ, ᎾᏲᎤᎴ ᎤᏕᏅ, ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᎤᏪᏙᎸᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
051408.english: And there sat a certain man at Lystra, impotent in his feet, being a cripple from his mother's womb, who never had walked:
051408.phonetic: Gi-lo-no i-yu-s-di a-s-ga-ya u-wo-le Li-s-di a-yo-u-li tsu-la-si-de-ni, na-yo-u-le u-de-nv, i-lv-hi-yu u-we-do-lv-hi ni-ge-sv-na.

051409.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏛᎦᏁ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎬ ᏉᎳ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏯᏅᏒᎯ ᏚᎧᎿᎭᏅ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᎤᎲ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏅᏬᏗ,
051409.english: The same heard Paul speak: who stedfastly beholding him, and perceiving that he had faith to be healed,
051409.phonetic: Na-s-gi u-dv-ga-ne ga-wo-ni-s-gv Quo-la, na-s-gi u-ya-nv-sv-hi du-ka-hna-nv a-le u-do-le-ho-sv u-hv go-hi-yu-di ge-sv a-nv-wo-di,

051410.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎ ᎠᏍᏓᏯ; ᎦᏥᏃᏍᏛ ᎭᎴᎲᎦ.  ᏚᎵᏔᏗᏅᏎᏃ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏪᏙᎴᎢ.
051410.english: Said with a loud voice, Stand upright on thy feet. And he leaped and walked.
051410.phonetic: Hi-a nu-we-se a-s-da-ya; Ga-tsi-no-s-dv ha-le-hv-ga.  Du-li-ta-di-nv-se-no a-le u-we-do-le-i.

051411.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᎤᏂᎪᎲ ᏄᏛᏁᎸ ᏉᎳ ᎤᏂᏌᎳᏓᏁ ᎠᏂᏁᎬᎢ, ᎵᎨᎣᏂᏯ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎤᏂᏬᏂᎯᏍᏗ ᎤᏅᏔᏁ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎠᎾᏠᎠᏏᎦ ᏴᏫ ᏄᎾᏍᏛ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ.
051411.english: And when the people saw what Paul had done, they lifted up their voices, saying in the speech of Lycaonia, The gods are come down to us in the likeness of men.
051411.phonetic: U-ni-tsa-ti-no u-ni-go-hv nu-dv-ne-lv Quo-la u-ni-sa-la-da-ne a-ni-ne-gv-i, Li-ge-o-ni-ya a-ne-hi u-ni-wo-ni-hi-s-di u-nv-ta-ne hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; Ga-lv-la-di a-ne-hi a-na-tlo-a-si-ga yv-wi nu-na-s-dv na-s-gi-ya i-yu-na-s-di.

051412.cherokee: ᏆᏂᏆᏃ ᏧᏈᏓ ᏚᏃᎡᎢ, ᏉᎳᏃ ᎹᎫᎵ ᏚᏃᎡᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎩ ᎨᏎᎢ.
051412.english: And they called Barnabas, Jupiter; and Paul, Mercurius, because he was the chief speaker.
051412.phonetic: Qua-ni-qua-no Tsu-qui-da du-no-e-i, Quo-la-no Ma-gu-li du-no-e-i, na-s-gi-ye-no nu-gv-wi-yu-sv a-li-tsi-do-hv-s-gi ge-se-i.

051413.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎶᏎᎯ ᏧᏈᏓ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎢᎬᏱᏗᏢ ᎪᏢᏒᎢ, ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏳᎶᏗ ᏚᏘᏃᎴ ᏩᎦ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎪᏛᎢᏛ ᎤᏂᏥᎸᏅᎯ ᏕᎤᏃᎴᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎨᏒ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎤᏁᎳᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏰᎸᏎᎢ.
051413.english: Then the priest of Jupiter, which was before their city, brought oxen and garlands unto the gates, and would have done sacrifice with the people.
051413.phonetic: Hna-quo-no a-tsi-lv--ge-lo-se-hi Tsu-qui-da na-s-gi ga-du-hv i-gv-yi-di-tlv go-tlv-sv-i, ga-lo-hi-s-di-yu-lo-di du-ti-no-le wa-ga a-le di-go-dv-i-dv u-ni-tsi-lv-nv-hi de-u-no-le-i, na-s-gi u-wa-sv a-le u-ni-tsa-ti ge-sv a-tsi-lv--u-ne-la-s-di-yi u-ye-lv-se-i.

051414.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎨᏥᏅᏏᏛ ᏆᏂᏆ ᎠᎴ ᏉᎳ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ ᏚᏂᏣᎦᎸᎮ ᏧᎾᏄᏬ, ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᏴᏫ ᏗᏁᏙᎲ ᏫᏚᎾᏗᏢᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎤᏁᎷᏁᎢ,
051414.english: Which when the apostles, Barnabas and Paul, heard of, they rent their clothes, and ran in among the people, crying out,
051414.phonetic: A-se-no ge-tsi-nv-si-dv Qua-ni-qua a-le Quo-la u-na-dv-ga-nv du-ni-tsa-ga-lv-he tsu-na-nu-wo, u-ni-tsa-ti-no yv-wi di-ne-do-hv wi-du-na-di-tlv-s-ta-ne-i, u-ne-lu-ne-i,

051415.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎨᎢ; ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏯ, ᎦᏙᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏣᏛᏁᎭ?  ᎠᏴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏴᏫᏉ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏂᏣᎵᏍᏓᏁᎲ ᎢᏲᎩᎾᎵᏍᏓᏁᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏨᏯᎵᏥᏙᏁᎭ ᏗᏥᏲᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏎᏉᏉ ᏂᏣᏛᏁᎲᎢ, ᏫᏣᎦᏔᎲᏍᏗᏱᏃ ᎬᏂᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎢᏗᏢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏬᏢᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ ᎦᎸᎶᎢ ᎠᎴ ᎦᏙᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎺᏉᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏂᎥ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏁᎯ;
051415.english: And saying, Sirs, why do ye these things? We also are men of like passions with you, and preach unto you that ye should turn from these vanities unto the living God, which made heaven, and earth, and the sea, and all things that are therein:
051415.phonetic: Hi-a na-ni-we-s-ge-i; I-tsi-s-ga-ya, ga-do-no na-s-gi hi-a ni-tsa-dv-ne-ha?  A-yv na-s-quo yv-wi-quo na-s-gi-ya ni-tsa-li-s-da-ne-hv i-yo-gi-na-li-s-da-ne-hi, a-le i-tsv-ya-li-tsi-do-ne-ha di-tsi-yo-hi-s-di-yi hi-a a-se-quo-quo ni-tsa-dv-ne-hv-i, wi-tsa-ga-ta-hv-s-di-yi-no gv-ni-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi i-di-tlv, na-s-gi u-wo-tlv-nv-hi tsi-gi ga-lv-lo-i a-le ga-do-hi a-le a-me-quo-hi a-le na-ni-v na-hna a-ne-hi;

051416.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎸᎯᏳ ᏥᎨᏒᎩ ᎤᎾᏁᎳᎩ ᎤᏅᏒ ᏚᏂᏅᏅ ᏩᏂᎶᎯ ᏚᏪᎵᏎᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏄᎾᏓᎴᏒ ᏴᏫ;
051416.english: Who in times past suffered all nations to walk in their own ways.
051416.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-lv-hi-yu tsi-ge-sv-gi u-na-ne-la-gi u-nv-sv du-ni-nv-nv wa-ni-lo-hi du-we-li-se-le ni-ga-dv nu-na-da-le-sv yv-wi;

051417.cherokee: ᎥᏝᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᏩᎩᏂᎪᎯ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎢᏯᏋᏁᎯ ᏳᏪᎵᏎᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᎩᎦᏃᏓᏁᎲᎢ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᏗᎦᎶᏍᎬᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᎦᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓᏁᎲ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏛᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᏓᏕᏘᏴᎯᏒᎢ, ᏗᎩᎾᏫ ᏓᎧᎵᎢᏍᏗᏍᎬ ᎠᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᎮᎵᏍᏗ.
051417.english: Nevertheless he left not himself without witness, in that he did good, and gave us rain from heaven, and fruitful seasons, filling our hearts with food and gladness.
051417.phonetic: V-tla-s-gi-ni-no-nv wa-gi-ni-go-hi gv-ni-ge-sv i-ya-quv-ne-hi yu-we-li-se-i, na-s-gi o-s-dv du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv-i, a-le i-gi-ga-no-da-ne-hv-i ga-lv-la-di di-ga-lo-s-gv-i, a-le i-ga-li-s-go-lv-da-ne-hv u-tsa-ti go-hu-s-di u-dv-hi-s-di-yi ni-da-de-ti-yv-hi-sv-i, di-gi-na-wi da-ka-li-i-s-di-s-gv a-li-s-da-yv-di a-le u-li-he-li-s-di.

051418.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎬᎢ ᎦᏂᎳ ᏚᏂᏲᏍᏙᏓᏁᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎬᏩᏁᎴᏗᏱ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏖᏍᎬᎢ.
051418.english: And with these sayings scarce restrained they the people, that they had not done sacrifice unto them.
051418.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no na-ni-we-s-gv-i ga-ni-la du-ni-yo-s-do-da-ne-le u-ni-tsa-ti a-tsi-lv--gv-wa-ne-le-di-yi a-na-da-nv-te-s-gv-i.

051419.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᏂᎷᏤ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎥᏘᎣᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎢᎪᏂᏯ ᏅᏓᏳᏂᎶᏒᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᏂᏔᏲᏎᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ, ᏉᎳᏃ ᏅᏯ ᏕᎬᏩᏂᏍᏔᏅ ᎬᏩᎾᏌᎾᏫᏛᎮ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎬᏩᏄᎯᏎᎢ, ᎤᏲᎱᏒ ᎠᏁᎵᏍᎨᎢ.
051419.english: And there came thither certain Jews from Antioch and Iconium, who persuaded the people, and, having stoned Paul, drew him out of the city, supposing he had been dead.
051419.phonetic: A-se-no u-ni-lu-tse A-ni-tsu-si V-ti-o-gi a-le I-go-ni-ya nv-da-yu-ni-lo-sv-hi, na-s-gi du-ni-ta-yo-se-le u-ni-tsa-ti, Quo-la-no nv-ya de-gv-wa-ni-s-ta-nv gv-wa-na-sa-na-wi-dv-he ga-du-hv gv-wa-nu-hi-se-i, u-yo-hu-sv a-ne-li-s-ge-i.

051420.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᎾᎵᎪᎯ ᏕᎬᏩᏚᏫᏍᏕᎢ ᏚᎴᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎦᏚᎲ ᏭᏴᎴᎢ.  ᎤᎩᏨᏛᏃ ᏆᏂᏆ ᎤᎾᏂᎩᏎᎢ, ᏓᏈ ᏭᏂᎶᏎᎢ.
051420.english: Howbeit, as the disciples stood round about him, he rose up, and came into the city: and the next day he departed with Barnabas to Derbe.
051420.phonetic: A-se-no u-na-li-go-hi de-gv-wa-du-wi-s-de-i du-le-ne-i, a-le di-ga-du-hv wu-yv-le-i.  U-gi-tsv-dv-no Qua-ni-qua u-na-ni-gi-se-i, Da-qui wu-ni-lo-se-i.

051421.cherokee: ᎤᎾᎵᏥᏙᏁᎸᏃ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᎪᏗᏳ ᏚᎾᎵᎪᏔᏅ, ᏔᎵᏁ ᎵᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎢᎪᏂᏯ ᎠᎴ ᎥᏘᎣᎩ ᏫᎤᏂᎶᏎᎢ,
051421.english: And when they had preached the gospel to that city, and had taught many, they returned again to Lystra, and to Iconium, and Antioch,
051421.phonetic: U-na-li-tsi-do-ne-lv-no o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv na-s-gi ga-du-hv-i, a-le a-ni-go-di-yu du-na-li-go-ta-nv, ta-li-ne Li-s-di a-le I-go-ni-ya a-le V-ti-o-gi wi-u-ni-lo-se-i,

051422.cherokee: ᏓᎾᎵᏂᎪᎯᏍᏗᏍᎨ ᏚᎾᏓᏅᏛ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ, ᏓᏂᏔᏲᏎᎮ ᏧᏂᏲᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᏂᏃᏁᎮ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎠᎩᎵᏴ ᎢᎩᎶᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᏫᎩᏴᏍᏗᏱ.
051422.english: Confirming the souls of the disciples, and exhorting them to continue in the faith, and that we must through much tribulation enter into the kingdom of God.
051422.phonetic: Da-na-li-ni-go-hi-s-di-s-ge du-na-da-nv-dv a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi, da-ni-ta-yo-se-he tsu-ni-yo-hi-s-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na go-hi-yu-di ge-sv-i, a-le da-ni-no-ne-he u-tsa-ti a-gi-li-yv i-gi-lo-hi-s-di ge-sv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-go-hi wi-gi-yv-s-di-yi.

051423.cherokee: ᏚᏂᎧᏅᏃ ᏗᎨᎦᏁᎶᏘ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᏚᎾᏓᏡᏩᏗᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏓᏙᎵᏍᏔᏅ ᎠᎹᏟ ᎠᏅᏍᎬᎢ, ᏚᏂᏲᎯᏎᎴ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏅᎯ.
051423.english: And when they had ordained them elders in every church, and had prayed with fasting, they commended them to the Lord, on whom they believed.
051423.phonetic: Du-ni-ka-nv-no di-ge-ga-ne-lo-ti tsu-na-ne-lo-di du-na-da-tlu-wa-di-sv-i, a-le u-na-da-do-li-s-ta-nv a-ma-tli a-nv-s-gv-i, du-ni-yo-hi-se-le U-gv-wi-yu-hi na-s-gi U-gv-wi-yu-hi u-no-hi-yu-nv-hi.

051424.english: And after they had passed throughout Pisidia, they came to Pamphylia.

051425.english: And when they had preached the word in Perga, they went down into Attalia:

051426.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᏥᏳᎯ ᏭᎾᏣᏅ ᎥᏘᎣᎩ ᏭᏂᎷᏤᎢ, ᎾᎿᎭᏗᎨᏥᏲᏒᎯ ᏥᎨᏎ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏂᏙᎵᏍᏗᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏛᎯ ᏥᎨᏎᎢ.
051426.english: And thence sailed to Antioch, from whence they had been recommended to the grace of God for the work which they fulfilled.
051426.phonetic: Na-hna-no tsi-yu-hi wu-na-tsa-nv V-ti-o-gi wu-ni-lu-tse-i, na-hna di-ge-tsi-yo-sv-hi tsi-ge-se U-ne-la-nv-hi u-ni-do-li-s-di-yi, na-s-gi tsu-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di-yi na-s-gi hna-quo u-ni-s-qua-dv-hi tsi-ge-se-i.

051427.cherokee: ᎤᏂᎷᏥᎸᏃ ᎠᎴ ᏚᏂᎳᏫᏛ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬ ᎤᏂᏃᎮᎴ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏄᎾᏛᏁᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏍᏚᎢᎡᎸ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ.
051427.english: And when they were come, and had gathered the church together, they rehearsed all that God had done with them, and how he had opened the door of faith unto the Gentiles.
051427.phonetic: U-ni-lu-tsi-lv-no a-le du-ni-la-wi-dv tsu-na-ne-lo-di u-na-da-tlu-gv u-ni-no-he-le ni-ga-v U-ne-la-nv-hi nu-na-dv-ne-lv-i, a-le du-s-du-i-e-lv tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi go-hi-yu-di ge-sv ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi.

051428.english: And there they abode long time with the disciples.

051501.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᏧᏗᏱ ᏅᏓᏳᏂᎶᏒᎯ ᏚᏁᏲᏁ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏟ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᏤᏥᎤᏍᏕᏎᎸᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎢᎨᏎᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎼᏏ ᏂᎦᏪᏍᎬᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᏴᎦᏰᏥᏍᏕᎸ.
051501.english: And certain men which came down from Judaea taught the brethren, and said, Except ye be circumcised after the manner of Moses, ye cannot be saved.
051501.phonetic: Gi-lo-no i-yu-na-s-di Tsu-di-yi nv-da-yu-ni-lo-sv-hi du-ne-yo-ne a-na-da-nv-tli hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; I-yu-no tse-tsi-u-s-de-se-lv-hi ni-ge-sv-na i-ge-se-s-di, na-s-gi Mo-si ni-ga-we-s-gv-i, v-tla yv-ga-ye-tsi-s-de-lv.

051502.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᎠᎴ ᏆᏂᏆ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᏂᏲᏠᎯᏎᎸ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏗᏒᎸ ᎠᏂᏬᏂᏍᎬ ᎾᏍᎩ, ᏚᏄᎪᏔᏁ ᏉᎳ ᎠᎴ ᏆᏂᏆ ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᎤᏁᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᏧᎾᏛᏛᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᎨᏥᏅᏏᏛ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎨᎦᏁᎶᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎠᏂᏱᎵᏙᎲ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ.
051502.english: When therefore Paul and Barnabas had no small dissension and disputation with them, they determined that Paul and Barnabas, and certain other of them, should go up to Jerusalem unto the apostles and elders about this question.
051502.phonetic: Quo-la-no a-le Qua-ni-qua u-tsa-ti u-ni-yo-tlo-hi-se-lv a-le u-na-di-sv-lv a-ni-wo-ni-s-gv na-s-gi, du-nu-go-ta-ne Quo-la a-le Qua-ni-qua a-le gi-lo i-yu-na-s-di nv-wa-na-da-le Tsi-lu-si-li-mi u-ne-nv-s-di-yi tsu-na-dv-dv-nv-s-di-yi ge-tsi-nv-si-dv a-le di-ge-ga-ne-lo-di na-s-gi hi-a a-ni-yi-li-do-hv u-gv-wa-li.

051503.cherokee: ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗᏃ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏫᏗᎬᏩᏂᎧᏅ ᎤᏂᎶᏎ ᏈᏂᏏ ᎠᎴ ᏌᎺᎵᏱ ᎠᏂᏃᎮᏍᎨ ᎠᎾᎦᏔᎲᏍᎬ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ; ᎤᏩᏘᏃ ᏚᎾᎵᎨᎵᏍᏔᏁ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏟ.
051503.english: And being brought on their way by the church, they passed through Phenice and Samaria, declaring the conversion of the Gentiles: and they caused great joy unto all the brethren.
051503.phonetic: Tsu-na-ne-lo-di-no u-na-da-tlu-gv i-yv-dv wi-di-gv-wa-ni-ka-nv u-ni-lo-se Qui-ni-si a-le Sa-me-li-yi a-ni-no-he-s-ge a-na-ga-ta-hv-s-gv tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi; u-wa-ti-no du-na-li-ge-li-s-ta-ne ni-ga-dv a-na-da-nv-tli.

051504.cherokee: ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻᏃ ᎤᏂᎷᏨ ᎦᎬᏩᎾᏓᏂᎸᏤ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬ ᎠᎴ ᎨᏥᏅᏏᏛ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎨᎦᏁᎶᏗ; ᎤᏂᏃᎮᎴᏃ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏄᏛᏁᎸ ᎤᏂᏍᏕᎸᎯᏙᎸᎢ.
051504.english: And when they were come to Jerusalem, they were received of the church, and of the apostles and elders, and they declared all things that God had done with them.
051504.phonetic: Tsi-lu-si-li-mi-no u-ni-lu-tsv ga-gv-wa-na-da-ni-lv-tse tsu-na-ne-lo-di u-na-da-tlu-gv a-le ge-tsi-nv-si-dv a-le di-ge-ga-ne-lo-di; u-ni-no-he-le-no ni-ga-v U-ne-la-nv-hi nu-dv-ne-lv u-ni-s-de-lv-hi-do-lv-i.

051505.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏚᎾᎴᏁ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᏆᎵᏏ ᎤᎾᎵᎪᏒ ᎠᏁᎳ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏅᎯ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ, ᎠᏎ ᏗᎨᏥᎤᏍᏕᏎᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎧᏁᏤᏗ ᏧᏂᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗᏱ ᎼᏏ ᎤᏁᏨᎯ.
051505.english: But there rose up certain of the sect of the Pharisees which believed, saying, That it was needful to circumcise them, and to command them to keep the law of Moses.
051505.phonetic: A-se-no du-na-le-ne gi-lo i-yu-na-s-di A-ni-qua-li-si u-na-li-go-sv a-ne-la na-s-gi u-no-hi-yu-nv-hi, hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i, A-se di-ge-tsi-u-s-de-se-di, a-le di-ka-ne-tse-di tsu-ni-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di-yi Mo-si u-ne-tsv-hi.

051506.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᏂᏃᎮᏗᏱ ᏚᏂᎳᏫᏤ ᎨᏥᏅᏏᏛ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎨᎦᏁᎶᏗ.
051506.english: And the apostles and elders came together for to consider of this matter.
051506.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no hna-quo u-ni-no-he-di-yi du-ni-la-wi-tse ge-tsi-nv-si-dv a-le di-ge-ga-ne-lo-di.

051507.cherokee: ᎤᏣᏘᏃ ᎤᏂᏬᏂᏒ ᏈᏓ ᏕᎤᎴᏁᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ; ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏯ ᎢᏓᏓᏅᏟ, ᏂᎯ ᎢᏥᎦᏔᎭ ᎿᎭᏉ ᏰᎵ ᎪᎯᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏴ ᎠᏆᏑᏰᏒ ᎢᎦᏓᏡᎬ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᎠᏴ ᎬᏆᏛᎦᏁᏗᏱ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ, ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏗᏱ.
051507.english: And when there had been much disputing, Peter rose up, and said unto them, Men and brethren, ye know how that a good while ago God made choice among us, that the Gentiles by my mouth should hear the word of the gospel, and believe.
051507.phonetic: U-tsa-ti-no u-ni-wo-ni-sv Qui-da de-u-le-ne-i, hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i; I-tsi-s-ga-ya i-da-da-nv-tli, ni-hi i-tsi-ga-ta-ha hna-quo ye-li go-hi-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi a-yv a-qua-su-ye-sv i-ga-da-tlu-gv tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi a-yv gv-qua-dv-ga-ne-di-yi o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv, na-s-gi-no u-no-hi-yu-di-yi.

051508.cherokee: ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯᏃ ᏧᏂᎾᏫ ᏗᎦᏔᎯ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏄᏩᏁᎸᎩ ᏕᎨᎦᏓᏂᎸᏨᎢ, ᏕᎠᏁᎲ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ, ᎠᏴ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏥᎩᏁᎸᎩ.
051508.english: And God, which knoweth the hearts, bare them witness, giving them the Holy Ghost, even as he did unto us;
051508.phonetic: U-ne-la-nv-hi-no tsu-ni-na-wi di-ga-ta-hi gv-ni-ge-sv nu-wa-ne-lv-gi de-ge-ga-da-ni-lv-tsv-i, de-a-ne-hv Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do, a-yv na-s-gi-ya tsi-gi-ne-lv-gi.

051509.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏗᎦᏓᎴᎿᎭᎢ ᏱᏂᎬᏁᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ, ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏕᎦᏅᎦᎸᏓᏁᎲ ᏧᏂᎾᏫ.
051509.english: And put no difference between us and them, purifying their hearts by faith.
051509.phonetic: A-le v-tla di-ga-da-le-hna-i yi-ni-gv-ne-le na-s-gi, go-hi-yu-di ge-sv de-ga-nv-ga-lv-da-ne-hv tsu-ni-na-wi.

051510.cherokee: Ꭷ ᎦᏙᏃ ᎢᎡᏥᎪᎵᏰ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ, ᏕᏥᎩᎳᎾᎳᏗᏍᎬ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎩᎦᏴᎵᎨ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎢᎬᏒ ᏰᎵ ᎨᎦᎩᎳᎾᎳᏗᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏥᎨᏒᎩ.
051510.english: Now therefore why tempt ye God, to put a yoke upon the neck of the disciples, which neither our fathers nor we were able to bear?
051510.phonetic: Ka ga-do-no i-e-tsi-go-li-ye U-ne-la-nv-hi, de-tsi-gi-la-na-la-di-s-gv a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi, na-s-gi di-gi-ga-yv-li-ge a-le na-s-quo i-gv-sv ye-li ge-ga-gi-la-na-la-di-s-di ni-ge-sv-na tsi-ge-sv-gi.

051511.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎢᏙᎯᏳᎲᏍᎦ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎬᏩᎦᏘᏯ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᎠᏴ ᎡᎩᏍᏕᎸᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎨᏥᏍᏕᎸᏗ ᏥᎩ.
051511.english: But we believe that through the grace of the Lord Jesus Christ we shall be saved, even as they.
051511.phonetic: A-se-no i-do-hi-yu-hv-s-ga na-s-gi U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv gv-wa-ga-ti-ya u-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv a-yv e-gi-s-de-lv-di ge-sv-i, na-s-gi-ya na-s-gi ge-tsi-s-de-lv-di tsi-gi.

051512.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎨᏒ ᎡᎳᏪ ᎤᏅᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎾᏛᏓᏍᏓᏁᎴ ᏆᏂᏆ ᎠᎴ ᏉᎳ ᎠᏂᏃᎮᏍᎬ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎤᏰᎸᏛ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏅᏗᏍᎬ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᏓᏁᏩᏗᏒᎢ.
051512.english: Then all the multitude kept silence, and gave audience to Barnabas and Paul, declaring what miracles and wonders God had wrought among the Gentiles by them.
051512.phonetic: Hna-quo-no ni-ga-dv u-ni-tsa-ti ge-sv e-la-we u-nv-ne-i, a-le du-na-dv-da-s-da-ne-le Qua-ni-qua a-le Quo-la a-ni-no-he-s-gv ni-ga-v u-ye-lv-dv a-le u-s-qua-ni-go-di U-ne-la-nv-hi na-s-gi u-nv-di-s-gv du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi da-ne-wa-di-sv-i.

051513.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎡᎳᏪ ᎤᏅᏅ, ᏥᎻ ᎤᏁᏤ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏯ ᎢᏓᏓᏅᏟ ᎠᏴ ᏍᎩᏯᏛᏓᏍᏓᏏ.
051513.english: And after they had held their peace, James answered, saying, Men and brethren, hearken unto me:
051513.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no e-la-we u-nv-nv, Tsi-mi u-ne-tse hi-a nu-we-se-i; I-tsi-s-ga-ya i-da-da-nv-tli a-yv s-gi-ya-dv-da-s-da-si.

051514.cherokee: ᏌᏩᏂ ᎤᏃᎮᎸ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎢᎬᏱᏱ ᏚᏩᏛᎲᎢ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ, ᎾᎿᎭᏧᏪᎴᏍᏗᏱ ᏴᏫ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᏙᎥ ᏗᎨᎪᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
051514.english: Simeon hath declared how God at the first did visit the Gentiles, to take out of them a people for his name.
051514.phonetic: Sa-wa-ni u-no-he-lv U-ne-la-nv-hi i-gv-yi-yi du-wa-dv-hv-i tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi, na-hna tsu-we-le-s-di-yi yv-wi na-s-gi du-do-v di-ge-go-s-do-di-yi.

051515.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏂᏕᎦᎵᏍᏗᎭ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎤᏂᏁᏨᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏥᏂᎬᏅ ᏥᎪᏪᎳ;
051515.english: And to this agree the words of the prophets; as it is written,
051515.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no hi-a na-s-gi-ya ni-de-ga-li-s-di-ha a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi u-ni-ne-tsv-i, hi-a tsi-ni-gv-nv tsi-go-we-la;

051516.cherokee: "ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᏍᏗ ᏛᏥᎷᏥ ᎠᎴ ᏛᏥᎵᏦᏘᏌᏂ ᏕᏫ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎦᎵᏦᏛᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎡᎳᏗ ᎤᏪᏡᏒᎯ ᏥᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏛᏥᎵᏦᏘᏌᏂ ᎤᎵᏦᏙᎠᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏛᏥᎧᎯᏌᏂ.
051516.english: After this I will return, and will build again the tabernacle of David, which is fallen down; and I will build again the ruins thereof, and I will set it up:
051516.phonetic: "Na-s-gi-no nu-li-s-ta-ne-s-di dv-tsi-lu-tsi a-le dv-tsi-li-tso-ti-sa-ni De-wi u-tse-li ga-li-tso-dv-i, na-s-gi e-la-di u-we-tlu-sv-hi tsi-gi, a-le dv-tsi-li-tso-ti-sa-ni u-li-tso-do-a-sv-i, a-le dv-tsi-ka-hi-sa-ni.

051517.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᎾᏓᎴ ᏴᏫ ᎬᏩᏂᏯᏍᏗ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᏓᏆᏙᎥ ᏗᎨᎪᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ, ᎠᏗᎭ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏧᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎯ ᏥᎩ."
051517.english: That the residue of men might seek after the Lord, and all the Gentiles, upon whom my name is called, saith the Lord, who doeth all these things.
051517.phonetic: Na-s-gi nu-na-da-le yv-wi gv-wa-ni-ya-s-di i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi U-gv-wi-yu-hi, a-le na-s-quo ni-ga-dv tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi da-qua-do-v di-ge-go-s-ta-nv-hi tsi-gi, a-di-ha U-gv-wi-yu-hi, na-s-gi hi-a ni-ga-dv tsu-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hi tsi-gi."

051518.english: Known unto God are all his works from the beginning of the world.

051519.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏴ ᎦᏓᏅᏖᏍᎬ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᏓᏕᏯᏙᏙᏗᏱᏉ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᎠᏁᎲ ᎤᎾᏖᎴᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎢᏗᏢ ᏭᎾᎦᏔᎲᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ;
051519.english: Wherefore my sentence is, that we trouble not them, which from among the Gentiles are turned to God:
051519.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di a-yv ga-da-nv-te-s-gv hi-a nu-s-di, na-s-gi di-da-de-ya-do-do-di-yi-quo ni-ge-sv-na tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi a-ne-hv u-na-te-le-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi i-di-tlv wu-na-ga-ta-hv-s-ta-nv-hi tsi-gi;

051520.cherokee: ᏗᏙᏪᎳᏁᏗᏱᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᏧᏂᏲᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎦᏓᎭ ᎢᏧᎵᏍᏔᏅᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓᏁᎸᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏗᏰᎸᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏕᎵᏛ ᏗᏂᏏᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎬᏬᏍᏔᏅᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎩᎬ.
051520.english: But that we write unto them, that they abstain from pollutions of idols, and from fornication, and from things strangled, and from blood.
051520.phonetic: Di-do-we-la-ne-di-yi-quo-s-gi-ni tsu-ni-yo-hi-s-di-yi ga-da-ha i-tsu-li-s-ta-nv-hi na-s-gi di-li-s-go-lv-da-ne-lv-hi U-ne-la-nv-hi di-ye-lv-hi, a-le u-de-li-dv di-ni-si-di ge-sv-i, a-le go-hu-s-di gv-wo-s-ta-nv-hi, a-le gi-gv.

051521.cherokee: ᎼᏏᏰᏃ ᎡᏘ ᏅᏓᎬᏩᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏕᎦᏚᏩᏗᏒ ᏕᎤᏪᎭ ᎬᏩᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎩ, ᏓᏥᎪᎵᏰᏍᎬᎢ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᏄᎾᏙᏓᏈᏒᎢ.
051521.english: For Moses of old time hath in every city them that preach him, being read in the synagogues every sabbath day.
051521.phonetic: Mo-si-ye-no e-ti nv-da-gv-wa-da-le-nv-dv de-ga-du-wa-di-sv de-u-we-ha gv-wa-li-tsi-do-hv-s-gi, da-tsi-go-li-ye-s-gv-i di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi nu-na-do-da-qui-sv-i.

051522.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎤᏂᏰᎸᏁ ᎨᏥᏅᏏᏛ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎨᎦᏁᎶᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬ ᏧᏂᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᏧᎾᏑᏰᏛ ᎤᏅᏒ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏓᏏ ᏆᏏᏆ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ ᎠᎴ ᏌᏱᎳ ᎨᏥᎸᏉᏗ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏢᎢ, ᏉᎳ ᎠᎴ ᏆᏂᏆ ᎢᏧᎳᎭ ᎤᏁᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᎥᏘᏍᎩ,
051522.english: Then pleased it the apostles and elders, with the whole church, to send chosen men of their own company to Antioch with Paul and Barnabas; namely, Judas surnamed Barsabas, and Silas, chief men among the brethren:
051522.phonetic: Hna-quo-no o-si-yu u-ni-ye-lv-ne ge-tsi-nv-si-dv a-le di-ge-ga-ne-lo-di a-le ni-ga-dv tsu-na-ne-lo-di u-na-da-tlu-gv tsu-ni-nv-s-di-yi a-ni-s-ga-ya tsu-na-su-ye-dv u-nv-sv u-na-da-tlu-gv-i, na-s-gi Tsu-da-si Qua-si-qua tsu-do-i-dv a-le Sa-yi-la ge-tsi-lv-quo-di a-na-da-nv-tlv-i, Quo-la a-le Qua-ni-qua i-tsu-la-ha u-ne-nv-s-di-yi V-ti-s-gi,

051523.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏂᏅᏍᏗ ᎤᏃᏪᎳᏁ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏅᏁᎴᎢ; ᎣᎩᏅᏏᏛ ᎠᎴ ᏦᎦᏁᎶᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎣᏣᏓᏅᏟ ᏫᏨᏲᎵᎦ ᎢᏣᏓᏅᏟ ᏗᏣᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᎥᏘᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏏᎵᏱ ᎠᎴ ᏏᎵᏏᏱ ᎢᏤᎯ.
051523.english: And they wrote letters by them after this manner; The apostles and elders and brethren send greeting unto the brethren which are of the Gentiles in Antioch and Syria and Cilicia:
051523.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-ni-nv-s-di u-no-we-la-ne hi-a nu-nv-ne-le-i; O-gi-nv-si-dv a-le tso-ga-ne-lo-di a-le o-tsa-da-nv-tli wi-tsv-yo-li-ga i-tsa-da-nv-tli di-tsa-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi V-ti-s-gi a-le Si-li-yi a-le Si-li-si-yi i-tse-hi.

051524.cherokee: ᎣᎬᏛᎦᏅ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᎪᎦᏓᏑᏰᏛ ᎠᏂᏬᏂᏍᎬ ᎨᏣᏕᏯᏙᏔᏅᎢ ᏕᎨᏥᎦᏔᎲᎡᎲ ᏗᏣᏓᏅᏙ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎬᎢ; ᎠᏎ ᏤᏥᎤᏍᏕᏎᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎢᏥᏍᏆᏂᎪᏙᏗ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏦᏥᏁᏤᎸᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ;
051524.english: Forasmuch as we have heard, that certain which went out from us have troubled you with words, subverting your souls, saying, Ye must be circumcised, and keep the law: to whom we gave no such commandment:
051524.phonetic: O-gv-dv-ga-nv gi-lo i-yu-na-s-di go-ga-da-su-ye-dv a-ni-wo-ni-s-gv ge-tsa-de-ya-do-ta-nv-i de-ge-tsi-ga-ta-hv-e-hv di-tsa-da-nv-do, hi-a na-ni-we-s-gv-i; A-se tse-tsi-u-s-de-se-di a-le di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di i-tsi-s-qua-ni-go-do-di; na-s-gi i-yu-s-di tso-tsi-ne-tse-lv-hi ni-ge-sv-na;

051525.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏧᎵᏍᏙᏔᏅ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎣᎩᏰᎸᏅ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏌᏉ ᎢᎦᎦᏛ, ᏗᏤᎲ ᏦᏥᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᏦᏣᏑᏰᏛ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᏧᏂᏍᏓᏩᏛᏍᏗᏱ ᏦᏥᎨᏳᎯ ᏆᏂᏆ ᎠᎴ ᏉᎳ,
051525.english: It seemed good unto us, being assembled with one accord, to send chosen men unto you with our beloved Barnabas and Paul,
051525.phonetic: Na-s-gi nv-tsu-li-s-do-ta-nv o-si-yu o-gi-ye-lv-nv ni-ga-dv sa-quo i-ga-ga-dv, di-tse-hv tso-tsi-nv-s-di-yi tso-tsa-su-ye-dv a-ni-s-ga-ya tsu-ni-s-da-wa-dv-s-di-yi tso-tsi-ge-yu-hi Qua-ni-qua a-le Quo-la,

051526.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᏓᏅᏅ ᏧᎾᎵᏍᎪᎸᏔᏅᎯ, ᎢᎦᏤᎵ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᏕᎤᏙᎥ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏗᏙᏗᏍᎬᎢ.
051526.english: Men that have hazarded their lives for the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
051526.phonetic: Na-s-gi a-ni-s-ga-ya da-nv-nv tsu-na-li-s-go-lv-ta-nv-hi, i-ga-tse-li U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv de-u-do-v nv-di-ga-li-s-di-do-di-s-gv-i.

051527.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏙᏥᏅᎵ ᏧᏓᏏ ᎠᎴ ᏌᏱᎳ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏂᏁᎬᎢ ᎤᏠᏱ ᏓᎨᏥᏃᎮᎮᎵ.
051527.english: We have sent therefore Judas and Silas, who shall also tell you the same things by mouth.
051527.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di do-tsi-nv-li Tsu-da-si a-le Sa-yi-la, na-s-gi na-s-quo a-ni-ne-gv-i u-tlo-yi da-ge-tsi-no-he-he-li.

051528.cherokee: ᎣᏏᏳᏰᏃ ᎤᏰᎸᏅ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏴ ᎣᏏᏳ ᏍᎩᏰᎸᏅ, ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏗᏨᏯᏰᏅᏙᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎯᎠ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏎ ᎢᏯᏛᏁᏗ ᏥᎩ;
051528.english: For it seemed good to the Holy Ghost, and to us, to lay upon you no greater burden than these necessary things;
051528.phonetic: O-si-yu-ye-no u-ye-lv-nv Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do, a-le a-yv o-si-yu s-gi-ye-lv-nv, go-hu-s-di di-tsv-ya-ye-nv-do-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na, hi-a u-wa-sv na-s-gi a-se i-ya-dv-ne-di tsi-gi;

051529.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᏥᏲᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏗᏰᎸᎯ ᏗᎵᏍᎪᎸᏓᏁᎸᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎩᎬ, ᎠᎴ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎬᏬᏍᏔᏅᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏕᎵᏛ ᏗᏂᏏᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.  ᎢᏳᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏱᏗᏤᏯᏙᏤᎸ ᎣᏏᏳ ᏱᏂᏣᏛᎦ.  ᏙᎯᏱ ᏂᏣᏛᎿᎭᏕᎨᏍᏗ.
051529.english: That ye abstain from meats offered to idols, and from blood, and from things strangled, and from fornication: from which if ye keep yourselves, ye shall do well. Fare ye well.
051529.phonetic: Na-s-gi di-tsi-yo-hi-s-di-yi a-li-s-da-yv-di U-ne-la-nv-hi di-ye-lv-hi di-li-s-go-lv-da-ne-lv-hi, a-le gi-gv, a-le go-hu-s-di gv-wo-s-ta-nv-hi, a-le u-de-li-dv di-ni-si-di ge-sv-i.  I-yu-no na-s-gi yi-di-tse-ya-do-tse-lv o-si-yu yi-ni-tsa-dv-ga.  Do-hi-yi ni-tsa-dv-hna-de-ge-s-di.

051530.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏕᎨᏥᏲᏒ ᎥᏘᎣᎩ ᏭᏂᎷᏤᎢ, ᏚᏂᎳᏫᏛᏃ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏚᏂᏅᏁᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎪᏪᎵ.
051530.english: So when they were dismissed, they came to Antioch: and when they had gathered the multitude together, they delivered the epistle:
051530.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no de-ge-tsi-yo-sv V-ti-o-gi wu-ni-lu-tse-i, du-ni-la-wi-dv-no u-ni-tsa-ti du-ni-nv-ne-le na-s-gi go-we-li.

051531.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏂᎪᎵᏰᎥ, ᎤᎾᎵᎮᎵᏤ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎨ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏓᎦᎵᏍᏓᏗᏍᎩ ᎨᏒᎢ
051531.english: Which when they had read, they rejoiced for the consolation.
051531.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-ni-go-li-ye-v, u-na-li-he-li-tse nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-ge na-s-gi a-da-ga-li-s-da-di-s-gi ge-sv-i

051532.cherokee: ᏧᏓᏏᏃ ᎠᎴ ᏌᏱᎳ, ᎤᏣᏖ ᎢᎧᏁᏨᎯ ᏚᏂᏬᏁᏔᏁ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏟ ᎠᎴ ᏚᎾᎵᏂᎪᎯᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎾᏍᏉᏰᏃ ᎤᏅᏒ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ ᎨᏎᎢ.
051532.english: And Judas and Silas, being prophets also themselves, exhorted the brethren with many words, and confirmed them.
051532.phonetic: Tsu-da-si-no a-le Sa-yi-la, u-tsa-te i-ka-ne-tsv-hi du-ni-wo-ne-ta-ne a-na-da-nv-tli a-le du-na-li-ni-go-hi-s-ta-ne-i, na-s-quo-ye-no u-nv-sv a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi ge-se-i.

051533.cherokee: ᎢᎸᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᏬᎯᏨ ᎤᏁᏙᎸ, ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏯᏛ ᏕᎬᏩᏂᏲᏎ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏟ ᎨᏥᏅᏏᏛ ᏗᏁᎲ ᏭᏂᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ.
051533.english: And after they had tarried there a space, they were let go in peace from the brethren unto the apostles.
051533.phonetic: I-lv-s-gi-no nu-wo-hi-tsv u-ne-do-lv, nv-wa-do-hi-ya-dv de-gv-wa-ni-yo-se a-na-da-nv-tli ge-tsi-nv-si-dv di-ne-hv wu-ni-lo-hi-s-di-yi.

051534.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏌᏱᎳ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎤᏰᎸᏁ ᎾᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᏗᎩᏯᏍᏗᏱ.
051534.english: Notwithstanding it pleased Silas to abide there still.
051534.phonetic: A-se-no Sa-yi-la o-si-yu u-ye-lv-ne na-hna-quo u-di-gi-ya-s-di-yi.

051535.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᎠᎴ ᏆᏂᏆ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎬᏁᏙᎮ ᎥᏘᎣᎩ ᏓᎾᏕᏲᎲᏍᎨᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎨ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏂᏣᏖ ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴᎢ.
051535.english: Paul also and Barnabas continued in Antioch, teaching and preaching the word of the Lord, with many others also.
051535.phonetic: Quo-la-no a-le Qua-ni-qua na-s-quo gv-ne-do-he V-ti-o-gi da-na-de-yo-hv-s-ge-i, a-le a-na-li-tsi-do-hv-s-ge ka-no-he-dv U-gv-wi-yu-hi u-tse-li-ga, a-le na-s-quo u-ni-tsa-te nv-wa-na-da-le-i.

051536.cherokee: ᎢᎸᏍᎩᏃ ᏫᏄᏒᎸ ᏉᎳ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ ᏆᏂᏆ; Ꭷ ᏔᎵᏁ ᏥᏙᏂᏩᏛᎱᎦ ᎢᏓᏓᏅᏟ ᏂᎦᎥ ᏕᎦᏚᏩᏗᏒ ᎾᎿᎭᎩᎾᎵᏥᏙᏂᏙᎸ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ, ᏫᎾᏙᎴᎰᎯ ᏄᎾᏛᎿᎭᏕᎬᎢ.
051536.english: And some days after Paul said unto Barnabas, Let us go again and visit our brethren in every city where we have preached the word of the Lord, and see how they do.
051536.phonetic: I-lv-s-gi-no wi-nu-sv-lv Quo-la hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi Qua-ni-qua; Ka ta-li-ne tsi-do-ni-wa-dv-hu-ga i-da-da-nv-tli ni-ga-v de-ga-du-wa-di-sv na-hna gi-na-li-tsi-do-ni-do-lv ka-no-he-dv U-gv-wi-yu-hi u-tse-li-ga, wi-na-do-le-ho-hi nu-na-dv-hna-de-gv-i.

051537.cherokee: ᏆᏂᏆᏃ ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎬᎩ ᎤᎾᏘᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᏣᏂ ᎹᎦ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ.
051537.english: And Barnabas determined to take with them John, whose surname was Mark.
051537.phonetic: Qua-ni-qua-no u-du-li-s-gv-gi u-na-ti-nv-s-di-yi Tsa-ni Ma-ga tsu-do-i-dv.

051538.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏉᎳ ᎥᏝ ᎣᏏᏳ ᏳᏰᎸᏁ ᎤᎾᏘᏅᏍᏗᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎤᎾᏓᏓᎴᏓᏁᎴ ᏆᎻᏈᎵᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᏳᎾᎵᎪᏁᎴ ᏚᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎵᏙᎲᎢ.
051538.english: But Paul thought not good to take him with them, who departed from them from Pamphylia, and went not with them to the work.
051538.phonetic: A-se-no Quo-la v-tla o-si-yu yu-ye-lv-ne u-na-ti-nv-s-di-yi, na-s-gi-ye-no u-na-da-da-le-da-ne-le Qua-mi-qui-li-yi, a-le v-tla yu-na-li-go-ne-le du-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-li-do-hv-i.

051539.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᏂᏲᏠᎯᏎᎸᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏫᏕᎬᏩᎾᎦᎴᏅᎲᎩ; ᏆᏂᏆᏃ ᎤᏘᏅᏒᎩ ᎹᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᏳᎯ ᏭᏣᏅ ᏌᏈ ᏭᎶᏒᎩ.
051539.english: And the contention was so sharp between them, that they departed asunder one from the other: and so Barnabas took Mark, and sailed unto Cyprus;
051539.phonetic: A-le u-tsa-ti u-ni-yo-tlo-hi-se-lv-gi, na-s-gi i-yu-s-di wi-de-gv-wa-na-ga-le-nv-hv-gi; Qua-ni-qua-no u-ti-nv-sv-gi Ma-ga, a-le tsi-yu-hi wu-tsa-nv Sa-qui wu-lo-sv-gi.

051540.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᎤᏑᏰᏒ ᏌᏱᎳ, ᎤᏂᎩᏒᎩ, ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏟ ᏕᎬᏩᏲᏒ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏪᏙᎵᏍᏗᏱ.
051540.english: And Paul chose Silas, and departed, being recommended by the brethren unto the grace of God.
051540.phonetic: Quo-la-no u-su-ye-sv Sa-yi-la, u-ni-gi-sv-gi, a-na-da-nv-tli de-gv-wa-yo-sv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-we-do-li-s-di-yi.

051541.cherokee: ᏏᎵᏱᏃ ᎠᎴ ᏏᎵᏏᏱ ᎤᎶᏒᎩ, ᏓᎵᏂᎪᎯᏍᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᏚᎾᏓᏡᏩᏗᏒᎢ.
051541.english: And he went through Syria and Cilicia, confirming the churches.
051541.phonetic: Si-li-yi-no a-le Si-li-si-yi u-lo-sv-gi, da-li-ni-go-hi-s-di-s-gv-gi tsu-na-ne-lo-di du-na-da-tlu-wa-di-sv-i.

051601.cherokee: ᏓᏈᏃ ᎠᎴ ᎵᏍᏗ ᏭᎷᏨᎩ, ᎬᏂᏳᏉᏃ ᎾᎿᎭᎡᎲᎩ ᎩᎶ ᎢᎨᏍᏗ ᎪᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ, ᏗᎹᏗ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎠᏧᏏ, ᎠᎨᏴ ᎪᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ ᎤᏥ ᎨᏒᎩ, ᎤᏙᏓᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᎪᎢ ᎨᏒᎩ.
051601.english: Then came he to Derbe and Lystra: and, behold, a certain disciple was there, named Timotheus, the son of a certain woman, which was a Jewess, and believed; but his father was a Greek:
051601.phonetic: Da-qui-no a-le Li-s-di wu-lu-tsv-gi, gv-ni-yu-quo-no na-hna e-hv-gi gi-lo i-ge-s-di go-hi-yu-hv-s-gi, Di-ma-di tsu-do-i-dv, A-tsu-si, a-ge-yv go-hi-yu-hv-s-gi u-tsi ge-sv-gi, u-do-da-s-gi-ni A-go-i ge-sv-gi.

051602.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎬᏩᏃᎮᏍᎩ ᎨᏒᎩ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏟ ᎵᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎢᎪᏂᏯ ᎠᏁᎯ.
051602.english: Which was well reported of by the brethren that were at Lystra and Iconium.
051602.phonetic: Na-s-gi o-si-yu gv-wa-no-he-s-gi ge-sv-gi a-na-da-nv-tli Li-s-di a-le I-go-ni-ya a-ne-hi.

051603.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏉᎳ ᎤᏚᎸᎲᎩ ᎤᏘᏅᏍᏗᏱ; ᎤᎱᏍᏕᏎᎸᎩᏃ ᏅᏓᏳᎵᏍᏙᏔᏅᎩ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎾᎿᎭᏓᏁᏩᏗᏒᎢ, ᏂᎦᏛᏰᏃ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲᎩ ᎤᏙᏓ ᎠᎪᎢ ᎨᏒᎢ.
051603.english: Him would Paul have to go forth with him; and took and circumcised him because of the Jews which were in those quarters: for they knew all that his father was a Greek.
051603.phonetic: Na-s-gi Quo-la u-du-lv-hv-gi u-ti-nv-s-di-yi; u-hu-s-de-se-lv-gi-no nv-da-yu-li-s-do-ta-nv-gi A-ni-tsu-si na-hna da-ne-wa-di-sv-i, ni-ga-dv-ye-no a-ni-ga-ta-hv-gi u-do-da A-go-i ge-sv-i.

051604.cherokee: ᏕᎦᏚᏩᏗᏒᏃ ᎠᏂᎶᏍᎬᎢ, ᏓᏂᏲᎯᏎᎲᎩ ᎤᏂᏍᏆᏂᎪᏙᏗ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏄᎪᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏥᏅᏏᏛ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎨᎦᏁᎶᏗ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᎠᏁᎯ.
051604.english: And as they went through the cities, they delivered them the decrees for to keep, that were ordained of the apostles and elders which were at Jerusalem.
051604.phonetic: De-ga-du-wa-di-sv-no a-ni-lo-s-gv-i, da-ni-yo-hi-se-hv-gi u-ni-s-qua-ni-go-do-di di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di, na-s-gi tsu-nu-go-ta-nv-hi ge-tsi-nv-si-dv a-le di-ge-ga-ne-lo-di Tsi-lu-si-li-mi a-ne-hi.

051605.cherokee: ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗᏃ ᏚᎾᏓᏡᏩᏗᏒ ᏚᎾᎵᏂᎪᏒᎩ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᏚᎩᏨᏂᏒ ᎠᏂᎪᏙᏍᎬᎩ.
051605.english: And so were the churches established in the faith, and increased in number daily.
051605.phonetic: Tsu-na-ne-lo-di-no du-na-da-tlu-wa-di-sv du-na-li-ni-go-sv-gi go-hi-yu-di ge-sv-i, a-le ni-du-gi-tsv-ni-sv a-ni-go-do-s-gv-gi.

051606.cherokee: ᎤᏂᎶᏐᏅᏃ ᏈᏥᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎨᎴᏏᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎤᏂᏅᏍᏓᏕᎸ ᎤᎾᎵᏥᏙᏗᏱ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎡᏏᏱ,
051606.english: Now when they had gone throughout Phrygia and the region of Galatia, and were forbidden of the Holy Ghost to preach the word in Asia,
051606.phonetic: U-ni-lo-so-nv-no Qui-tsi-yi a-le Ge-le-si-yi, a-le Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do u-ni-nv-s-da-de-lv u-na-li-tsi-do-di-yi ka-no-he-dv E-si-yi,

051607.cherokee: ᎻᏏᏱ ᏭᏂᎷᏨ, ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏖᎸᎩ ᏈᏗᏂᏱ ᏭᏂᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᏎᏃ ᎥᏝ ᎤᎾᏁᎳᎩ ᏱᏚᏪᎵᏎᎴ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ.
051607.english: After they were come to Mysia, they assayed to go into Bithynia: but the Spirit suffered them not.
051607.phonetic: Mi-si-yi wu-ni-lu-tsv, u-na-da-nv-te-lv-gi Qui-di-ni-yi wu-ni-lo-hi-s-di-yi, a-se-no v-tla u-na-ne-la-gi yi-du-we-li-se-le A-da-nv-do.

051608.english: And they passing by Mysia came down to Troas.

051609.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᏫᏎᎸᎩ ᏒᏃᏱ; ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎹᏏᏙᏂ ᎡᎯ ᎦᏙᎨᎢ, ᎤᏔᏲᏎᎮᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎮᎢ; ᎹᏏᏙᏂ ᏫᎷᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏫᏍᎩᏍᏕᎸ.
051609.english: And a vision appeared to Paul in the night; There stood a man of Macedonia, and prayed him, saying, Come over into Macedonia, and help us.
051609.phonetic: Quo-la-no u-ne-la-wi-se-lv-gi sv-no-yi; gi-lo i-yu-s-di a-s-ga-ya Ma-si-do-ni e-hi ga-do-ge-i, u-ta-yo-se-he-i, hi-a nu-we-se-he-i; Ma-si-do-ni wi-lu-gi, a-le wi-s-gi-s-de-lv.

051610.cherokee: ᎤᏁᎳᏫᏎᎸᏃ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎣᎦᏁᎶᏔᏅᎩ ᎹᏏᏙᏂ ᏬᎩᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᎣᏣᏙᎴᎰᏍᎬᎩ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎣᎩᏯᏂᏍᎬᎢ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏦᏣᎵᏥᏙᏁᏗᏱ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ.
