051610.english: And after he had seen the vision, immediately we endeavored to go into Macedonia, assuredly gathering that the Lord had called us for to preach the gospel unto them.
051610.phonetic: U-ne-la-wi-se-lv-no gi-la-quo i-yv-dv o-ga-ne-lo-ta-nv-gi Ma-si-do-ni wo-gi-lo-hi-s-di-yi, o-tsa-do-le-ho-s-gv-gi U-gv-wi-yu-hi o-gi-ya-ni-s-gv-i na-s-gi tso-tsa-li-tsi-do-ne-di-yi o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv.

051611.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏠᎠᏏ ᏥᏳᎯ ᎣᎦᏣᏅ ᏧᏳᎪᏗ ᏬᎩᏅᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᏎᎹᏞᏏ ᏬᎩᏃᎸᎩ; ᎤᎩᏨᏛᏃ ᏂᎠᏆᎵᏏ ᏬᎩᏃᎸᎩ.
051611.english: Therefore loosing from Troas, we came with a straight course to Samothracia, and the next day to Neapolis;
051611.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no Tlo-a-si tsi-yu-hi o-ga-tsa-nv tsu-yu-go-di wo-gi-nv-s-ta-nv-gi, Se-ma-tle-si wo-gi-no-lv-gi; u-gi-tsv-dv-no Ni-a-qua-li-si wo-gi-no-lv-gi.

051612.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᏫᎣᎦᏣᏅ, ᏈᎵᎩᏱ ᏬᎩᏃᎸᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎾᎿᎭᎢᏗᏢ ᎹᏏᏙᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏛ ᎤᏂᏚᎲ ᏥᎩ.  ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎢᎸᏍᎩ ᏧᏒᎯᏛ ᎣᎨᏙᎸᎩ.
051612.english: And from thence to Philippi, which is the chief city of that part of Macedonia, and a colony: and we were in that city abiding certain days.
051612.phonetic: Na-hna-no wi-o-ga-tsa-nv, Qui-li-gi-yi wo-gi-no-lv-gi, na-s-gi nu-gv-wi-yu-sv ga-du-hv na-hna i-di-tlv Ma-si-do-ni, a-le na-s-gi u-na-da-nv-dv u-ni-du-hv tsi-gi.  Na-hna-no ga-du-hv i-lv-s-gi tsu-sv-hi-dv o-ge-do-lv-gi.

051613.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬᏃ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎣᎩᏄᎪᏨᎩ, ᎡᏉᏄᎶᏗ ᏬᎩᎶᏒᎩ ᎠᏓᏙᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎣᎦᏅᏅᏃ ᏙᏥᏬᏁᏔᏅ ᎠᏂᎨᏴ ᎾᎿ ᎭᎤᎾᏓᏟᏌᏅᎯ.
051613.english: And on the sabbath we went out of the city by a river side, where prayer was wont to be made; and we sat down, and spake unto the women which resorted thither.
051613.phonetic: U-na-do-da-qua-s-gv-no ga-du-hv o-gi-nu-go-tsv-gi, e-quo-nu-lo-di wo-gi-lo-sv-gi a-da-do-li-s-do-di-yi ge-sv-i; o-ga-nv-nv-no do-tsi-wo-ne-ta-nv a-ni-ge-yv na-hna u-na-da-tli-sa-nv-hi.

051614.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᎨᏴ ᎵᏗ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎩᎦᎨ ᏗᏄᏬ ᎦᏃᏗᏍᎩ, ᏓᏱᏓᎵ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎡᎯ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏓᏙᎵᏍᏔᏁᎯ, ᎤᏛᏓᏍᏔᏅᎩ.  ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏫᏱ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏍᏚᎢᏒᎩ, ᎤᏛᏓᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᏉᎳ ᎧᏁᎬᎢ.
051614.english: And a certain woman named Lydia, a seller of purple, of the city of Thyatira, which worshipped God, heard us: whose heart the Lord opened, that she attended unto the things which were spoken of Paul.
051614.phonetic: Gi-lo-no i-yu-s-di a-ge-yv Li-di tsu-do-i-dv, gi-ga-ge di-nu-wo ga-no-di-s-gi, Da-yi-da-li ga-du-hv e-hi, U-ne-la-nv-hi a-da-do-li-s-ta-ne-hi, u-dv-da-s-ta-nv-gi.  Na-s-gi u-na-wi-yi U-gv-wi-yu-hi u-s-du-i-sv-gi, u-dv-da-s-do-di-yi Quo-la ka-ne-gv-i.

051615.cherokee: ᎠᎦᏬᎥᏃ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᏓᏘᎾᎥᎢ, ᎣᎩᏔᏲᏎᎸᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᏒ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎪᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ ᏍᎩᏰᎵᏎᎮᏍᏗ, ᏥᏁᎸ ᎢᏥᏴᎭ ᎠᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᎢᏥᏁᏍᏗ.  ᎣᎩᏍᏗᏰᏗᏍᎬᏃ ᎣᎩᏎᎪᎩᏒᎩ.
051615.english: And when she was baptized, and her household, she besought us, saying, If ye have judged me to be faithful to the Lord, come into my house, and abide there. And she constrained us.
051615.phonetic: A-ga-wo-v-no u-wa-sv a-le na-s-gi du-da-ti-na-v-i, o-gi-ta-yo-se-lv-gi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; I-yu-no u-do-hi-yu-sv U-gv-wi-yu-hi go-hi-yu-hv-s-gi s-gi-ye-li-se-he-s-di, tsi-ne-lv i-tsi-yv-ha a-le na-hna i-tsi-ne-s-di.  O-gi-s-di-ye-di-s-gv-no o-gi-se-go-gi-sv-gi.

051616.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᎠᏓᏙᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ ᎣᎨᏅ, ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏛ, ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎠᏙᏂᏍᎩ ᎤᏯᎢ, ᏙᎦᏠᏒᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏣᏛ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᏓᏩᏛᎡᎲᎩ ᎬᏩᎾᏝᎢ ᎠᏙᏂᏍᎬ ᎬᏗᏍᎩ.
051616.english: And it came to pass, as we went to prayer, a certain damsel possessed with a spirit of divination met us, which brought her masters much gain by soothsaying:
051616.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-nv-gi, a-da-do-li-s-do-di-yi o-ge-nv, gi-lo i-yu-s-di a-dv, a-da-nv-do a-do-ni-s-gi u-ya-i, do-ga-tlo-sv-gi, na-s-gi u-tsa-dv tsu-gv-wa-lo-di da-wa-dv-e-hv-gi gv-wa-na-tla-i a-do-ni-s-gv gv-di-s-gi.

051617.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎣᎩᏍᏓᏩᏗᏒ ᏉᎳ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏴ, ᎤᏪᎷᏅᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎯᎠ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏩᏍᏛ ᎦᎸᎳᏗᏳ ᎡᎯ ᏧᏅᏏᏓᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏥᏂᎨᎬᏁᎭ ᏅᏃᎯ ᎠᎵᏍᏕᎸᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
051617.english: The same followed Paul and us, and cried, saying, These men are the servants of the most high God, which shew unto us the way of salvation.
051617.phonetic: Na-s-gi o-gi-s-da-wa-di-sv Quo-la a-le a-yv, u-we-lu-nv-gi hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Hi-a a-ni-s-ga-ya U-ne-la-nv-hi wa-s-dv ga-lv-la-di-yu e-hi tsu-nv-si-da-s-di, na-s-gi gv-ni-ge-sv tsi-ni-ge-gv-ne-ha nv-no-hi a-li-s-de-lv-do-di ge-sv-i.

051618.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏛ ᏄᏒᎸᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏛᏁᎲᎩ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᏉᎳ ᎤᏕᏯᏔᏁᎸ ᎤᎦᏔᎲᏒᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎸᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ; ᎬᏁᏥ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᏕᎤᏙᎥ ᎬᏗᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎪᎱᎪᏤᏗᏱ.  ᎾᎯᏳᏉᏃ ᎤᏄᎪᏨᎩ.
051618.english: And this did she many days. But Paul, being grieved, turned and said to the spirit, I command thee in the name of Jesus Christ to come out of her. And he came out the same hour.
051618.phonetic: A-le u-tsa-dv nu-sv-lv-gi na-s-gi na-dv-ne-hv-gi; a-se-no Quo-la u-de-ya-ta-ne-lv u-ga-ta-hv-sv-gi, hi-a nu-we-se-lv-gi na-s-gi a-da-nv-do; Gv-ne-tsi Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv de-u-do-v gv-di-ha, na-s-gi go-hu-go-tse-di-yi.  Na-hi-yu-quo-no u-nu-go-tsv-gi.

051619.cherokee: ᎬᏩᎾᏝᎢᏃ ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᎤᏚᎩ ᎤᏅᏒ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᎤᏂᏩᏛᏗᏱ ᎤᏲᏨᎢ, ᏗᏂᏂᏴᎲᎩ ᏉᎳ ᎠᎴ ᏌᏱᎳ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏃᏙᏗᏱ ᏫᏚᎾᏘᏃᎮᎸᎩ ᏗᎨᎦᏁᎶᏗ.
051619.english: And when her masters saw that the hope of their gains was gone, they caught Paul and Silas, and drew them into the marketplace unto the rulers,
051619.phonetic: Gv-wa-na-tla-i-no u-na-do-le-ho-sv u-du-gi u-nv-sv tsu-gv-wa-lo-di u-ni-wa-dv-di-yi u-yo-tsv-i, di-ni-ni-yv-hv-gi Quo-la a-le Sa-yi-la, a-le na-s-gi ga-no-do-di-yi wi-du-na-ti-no-he-lv-gi di-ge-ga-ne-lo-di.

051620.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏗᏄᎪᏗᏍᎩ ᏫᏚᎾᏘᏃᎮᎸᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎯᎠ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᎠᏧᏏ ᎤᏣᏔᏅᎯ ᎠᎾᏕᏯᏙᏗᎭ ᎢᎩᏚᎲᎢ,
051620.english: And brought them to the magistrates, saying, These men, being Jews, do exceedingly trouble our city,
051620.phonetic: A-le di-nu-go-di-s-gi wi-du-na-ti-no-he-lv-gi, hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Hi-a a-ni-s-ga-ya A-a-tsu-si u-tsa-ta-nv-hi a-na-de-ya-do-di-ha i-gi-du-hv-i,

051621.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎾᏅᏁᎭ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏰᎵ ᏗᎨᎩᏂᏴᏗ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎨᎩᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎢᏗᎶᎻ ᎨᏒ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ.
051621.english: And teach customs, which are not lawful for us to receive, neither to observe, being Romans.
051621.phonetic: A-le gv-ni-ge-sv na-nv-ne-ha di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di na-s-gi ye-li di-ge-gi-ni-yv-di a-le di-ge-gi-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di ni-ge-sv-na, I-di-lo-mi ge-sv i-yu-s-di.

051622.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᎨᏒ ᏕᎬᏩᏂᎦᏘᎸᏒᎩ; ᏗᏄᎪᏗᏍᎩᏃ ᏚᏂᏄᏪᏒ ᎤᏂᏁᏨᎩ ᏗᎨᏥᎵᎥᏂᏍᏗᏱ.
051622.english: And the multitude rose up together against them: and the magistrates rent off their clothes, and commanded to beat them.
051622.phonetic: U-ni-tsa-ti-no ge-sv de-gv-wa-ni-ga-ti-lv-sv-gi; di-nu-go-di-s-gi-no du-ni-nu-we-sv u-ni-ne-tsv-gi di-ge-tsi-li-v-ni-s-di-yi.

051623.cherokee: ᎤᏣᏘᏃ ᏂᏚᏅᏂᎸ, ᏗᏓᏍᏚᏗᏱ ᏚᏂᏴᏔᏅᎩ, ᎤᏂᏁᏤᎸᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏧᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗᏱ.
051623.english: And when they had laid many stripes upon them, they cast them into prison, charging the jailor to keep them safely:
051623.phonetic: U-tsa-ti-no ni-du-nv-ni-lv, di-da-s-du-di-yi du-ni-yv-ta-nv-gi, u-ni-ne-tse-lv-gi na-s-gi a-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-di o-s-dv tsu-ga-ti-di-s-di-yi.

051624.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎾᏥᏪᏎᎸ ᎠᏥᏁᏤᎸ, ᏫᏚᏴᏔᏅᎩ ᏗᏓᏍᏚᏗᏱ ᎪᏢᏒ ᎦᎵᏦᏕ ᎭᏫᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏍᏓᏯ ᏚᏅᏎᏙᏅ ᎠᏙᎯ.
051624.english: Who, having received such a charge, thrust them into the inner prison, and made their feet fast in the stocks.
051624.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no na-tsi-we-se-lv a-tsi-ne-tse-lv, wi-du-yv-ta-nv-gi di-da-s-du-di-yi go-tlv-sv ga-li-tso-de ha-wi-ni, a-le a-s-da-ya du-nv-se-do-nv a-do-hi.

051625.cherokee: ᏒᏃᏱᏃ ᎠᏰᎵ ᏉᎳ ᎠᎴ ᏌᏱᎳ ᎤᎾᏓᏙᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏚᏂᏃᎩᏍᏔᏁᎢ; ᏗᎨᏥᏍᏚᎯᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏁᎢ.
051625.english: And at midnight Paul and Silas prayed, and sang praises unto God: and the prisoners heard them.
051625.phonetic: sv-no-yi-no a-ye-li Quo-la a-le Sa-yi-la u-na-da-do-li-s-ta-ne-i, a-le U-ne-la-nv-hi du-ni-no-gi-s-ta-ne-i; di-ge-tsi-s-du-hi-no na-s-gi u-na-dv-ga-ne-i.

051626.cherokee: ᎤᏰᎶᎢᏍᏔᏁᏃ ᎦᏙᎯ ᎤᎵᏖᎸᏁᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᏗᏓᏍᏚᏘᏱ ᏚᎵᏍᏓᏱᏗᏍᏛ ᏚᎵᏖᎸᏁᎢ, ᎩᎳᏉᏃ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏓᏍᏚᎲ ᏚᎵᏍᏚᎢᏎᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏂᎥ ᏚᎾᏓᎸᏍᏛ ᏚᏢᏒᎮᎢ.
051626.english: And suddenly there was a great earthquake, so that the foundations of the prison were shaken: and immediately all the doors were opened, and every one's bands were loosed.
051626.phonetic: U-ye-lo-i-s-ta-ne-no ga-do-hi u-li-te-lv-ne-i; a-le di-da-s-du-ti-yi du-li-s-da-yi-di-s-dv du-li-te-lv-ne-i, gi-la-quo-no i-yv-dv ni-ga-dv da-s-du-hv du-li-s-du-i-se-i, a-le na-ni-v du-na-da-lv-s-dv du-tlv-sv-he-i.

051627.cherokee: ᏗᏓᏍᏚᏗᏱᏃ ᎠᎦᏘᏯ, ᎤᏰᏨ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᎦᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᏗᏓᏍᏚᏗᏱ ᏧᎵᏍᏚᎢᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎤᎸᎮ ᎠᏰᎳᏍᏗ ᎦᏅᎯᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏩᏒ ᏗᏓᎵᏎᎢ, ᏗᎨᏥᏍᏚᎯ ᎤᎾᎵᏒ ᎡᎵᏍᎨᎢ.
051627.english: And the keeper of the prison awaking out of his sleep, and seeing the prison doors open, he drew out his sword, and would have killed himself, supposing that the prisoners had been fled.
051627.phonetic: Di-da-s-du-di-yi-no a-ga-ti-ya, u-ye-tsv a-le u-do-le-ho-sv ga-lo-hi-s-di-yi di-da-s-du-di-yi tsu-li-s-du-i-dv ge-sv-i, u-lv-he a-ye-la-s-di ga-nv-hi-dv, a-le u-wa-sv di-da-li-se-i, di-ge-tsi-s-du-hi u-na-li-sv e-li-s-ge-i.

051628.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏉᎳ ᎠᏍᏓᏯ ᎤᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏞᏍᏗ ᏨᏒ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎤᏲ ᎢᏣᏓᏛᏁᎸᎩ, ᎠᏂᏰᏃ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎣᏥᏯᎠ.
051628.english: But Paul cried with a loud voice, saying, Do thyself no harm: for we are all here.
051628.phonetic: A-se-no Quo-la a-s-da-ya u-li-s-ta-ne-i, hi-a nu-we-se-i; Tle-s-di tsv-sv go-hu-s-di u-yo i-tsa-da-dv-ne-lv-gi, a-ni-ye-no ni-ga-dv o-tsi-ya-a.

051629.cherokee: ᎠᏨᏍᏙᏗᏃ ᎤᏔᏲᎸ ᎤᎵᏍᏗ ᏭᏴᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏭᎷᏤ ᎤᏪᎾᏫᏍᎨᎢ, ᎤᏓᏅᏂᎴ ᎢᎬᏱᏢ ᏉᎳ ᎠᎴ ᏌᏱᎳ ᏚᏃᎸᎢ.
051629.english: Then he called for a light, and sprang in, and came trembling, and fell down before Paul and Silas,
051629.phonetic: A-tsv-s-do-di-no u-ta-yo-lv u-li-s-di wu-yv-le-i, a-le wu-lu-tse u-we-na-wi-s-ge-i, u-da-nv-ni-le i-gv-yi-tlv Quo-la a-le Sa-yi-la du-no-lv-i.

051630.cherokee: ᏚᏄᎪᏫᏒᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᎢᏍᏗᏍᎦᏯ, ᎦᏙ ᏓᎦᏛᏁᎵ ᎥᎩᏍᏕᎸᏗᏱ?
051630.english: And brought them out, and said, Sirs, what must I do to be saved?
051630.phonetic: Du-nu-go-wi-sv-no hi-a nu-we-se-i; I-s-di-s-ga-ya, ga-do da-ga-dv-ne-li v-gi-s-de-lv-di-yi?

051631.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᎯᏲᎢᏳᎲᎦ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ, ᏓᏰᏣᏍᏕᎸᎯᏃ, ᏂᎯ ᎠᎴ ᏕᏣᏓᏘᎾᎥᎢ.
051631.english: And they said, Believe on the Lord Jesus Christ, and thou shalt be saved, and thy house.
051631.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-ni-we-se-i; Hi-yo-i-yu-hv-ga U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv, da-ye-tsa-s-de-lv-hi-no, ni-hi a-le de-tsa-da-ti-na-v-i.

051632.cherokee: ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯᏃ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᏂᏃᎮᎮᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏁᎸᎢ ᎠᏂᏯᎢ.
051632.english: And they spake unto him the word of the Lord, and to all that were in his house.
051632.phonetic: U-gv-wi-yu-hi-no u-tse-li ka-no-he-dv u-ni-no-he-he-le na-s-gi, a-le ni-ga-dv na-s-gi ga-ne-lv-i a-ni-ya-i.

051633.cherokee: ᎾᎯᏳᏉᏃ ᏒᏃᏱ ᏚᏯᏅᎮᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏬᏑᎴᎴ ᏕᎨᏥᎵᎥᏂᎸᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎠᎦᏬᎡ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏧᏤᎵᎦ.
051633.english: And he took them the same hour of the night, and washed their stripes; and was baptized, he and all his, straightway.
051633.phonetic: Na-hi-yu-quo-no sv-no-yi du-ya-nv-he-i, a-le du-wo-su-le-le de-ge-tsi-li-v-ni-lv-i; a-le gi-la-quo i-yv-dv a-ga-wo-e u-wa-sv a-le ni-ga-dv tsu-tse-li-ga.

051634.cherokee: ᎦᏁᎸᏃ ᏚᏴᏔᏂᎸ ᏚᏪᎳᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎪᎯᏳᎲᏍᎬ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᎵᎮᎵᏤᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎦᏁᎸᎢ.
051634.english: And when he had brought them into his house, he set meat before them, and rejoiced, believing in God with all his house.
051634.phonetic: Ga-ne-lv-no du-yv-ta-ni-lv du-we-la-s-ta-ne-i, a-le go-hi-yu-hv-s-gv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-li-he-li-tse-i, a-le na-s-quo ni-ga-dv ga-ne-lv-i.

051635.cherokee: ᏑᎾᎴᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ ᏗᏄᎪᏗᏍᎩ ᏚᏂᏅᏎ ᏗᎾᏓᏂᏱᏍᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏫᏄᏂᏪᏍᏗᏱ; ᏘᎧᎲᎦ Ꮎ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ.
051635.english: And when it was day, the magistrates sent the serjeants, saying, Let those men go.
051635.phonetic: Su-na-le-no nu-li-s-ta-nv di-nu-go-di-s-gi du-ni-nv-se di-na-da-ni-yi-s-gi, hi-a wi-nu-ni-we-s-di-yi; Ti-ka-hv-ga na a-ni-s-ga-ya.

051636.cherokee: ᏗᏓᏍᏚᏗᏱᏃ ᎠᎦᏘᏯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏧᏂᏪᏒ ᎤᏃᏁᎴ ᏉᎳ; ᏗᏄᎪᏗᏍᎩ ᏛᎾᏓᏅᎵ ᏗᏍᏛᏯᎪᏗᏱ; Ꭷ, ᏍᏗᏄᎪᎢ ᎠᎴ ᏅᏩᏙᎯᏩᏛ ᎢᏍᏕᎾ.
051636.english: And the keeper of the prison told this saying to Paul, The magistrates have sent to let you go: now therefore depart, and go in peace.
051636.phonetic: Di-da-s-du-di-yi-no a-ga-ti-ya na-s-gi nv-tsu-ni-we-sv u-no-ne-le Quo-la; Di-nu-go-di-s-gi dv-na-da-nv-li di-s-dv-ya-go-di-yi; ka, s-di-nu-go-i a-le nv-wa-do-hi-wa-dv i-s-de-na.

051637.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᏉᎳ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎴᎢ, ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏕᎪᎩᏂᎵᎥᏂᎸ ᏧᏚᎪᏔᏅᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎣᎩᏂᏍᎦᏅᏨᎢ, ᎣᏍᏗᎶᎻ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᏓᏍᏚᏗᏱ ᏕᎪᎩᏂᏴᏔᏅ, ᏥᎪᏃ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᏕᎵᏛ ᎢᎪᎩᏂᏄᎪᏫᏍᎦ?  ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ ᎥᏝ, ᎤᏅᏒᏍᎩᏂ ᏩᏂᎷᎩ ᏫᎪᎩᏂᏄᎪᏩ.
051637.english: But Paul said unto them, They have beaten us openly uncondemned, being Romans, and have cast us into prison; and now do they thrust us out privily? nay verily; but let them come themselves and fetch us out.
051637.phonetic: A-se-no quo-la hi-a ni-du-we-se-le-i, gv-ni-ge-sv de-go-gi-ni-li-v-ni-lv tsu-du-go-ta-nv-hi ni-ge-sv-na o-gi-ni-s-ga-nv-tsv-i, O-s-di-lo-mi, a-le di-da-s-du-di-yi de-go-gi-ni-yv-ta-nv, tsi-go-no hna-quo u-de-li-dv i-go-gi-ni-nu-go-wi-s-ga?  U-do-hi-yu-hi v-tla, u-nv-sv-s-gi-ni wa-ni-lu-gi wi-go-gi-ni-nu-go-wa.

051638.cherokee: ᏗᎾᏓᏂᏱᏍᎩᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒ ᏚᏂᏃᏁᎴ ᏗᏄᎪᏗᏍᎩ.  ᎤᏂᏍᎦᎴᏃ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ ᎠᏂᎶᎻ ᎨᏒᎢ.
051638.english: And the serjeants told these words unto the magistrates: and they feared, when they heard that they were Romans.
051638.phonetic: Di-na-da-ni-yi-s-gi-no na-s-gi hi-a nu-ni-we-sv du-ni-no-ne-le di-nu-go-di-s-gi.  U-ni-s-ga-le-no u-na-dv-ga-nv A-ni-lo-mi ge-sv-i.

051639.cherokee: ᎤᏂᎷᏤᏃ ᎠᎴ ᏚᏂᏍᏗᏰᏔᏁᎢ; ᏚᏂᏄᎪᏫᏒᏃ ᏚᏂᏔᏲᏎᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᎦᏚᎲ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏍᏗᏱ.
051639.english: And they came and besought them, and brought them out, and desired them to depart out of the city.
051639.phonetic: U-ni-lu-tse-no a-le du-ni-s-di-ye-ta-ne-i; du-ni-nu-go-wi-sv-no du-ni-ta-yo-se-le na-hna ga-du-hv u-na-da-nv-s-di-yi.

051640.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏄᎪᏨᏃ ᏗᏓᏍᏚᏗᏱ ᎵᏗ ᎦᏁᎸ ᏭᏂᏴᎸᎩ; ᏚᏂᎪᎲᏃ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏟ, ᏚᏂᎦᎵᏍᏓᏛᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏂᎩᏒᎩ.
051640.english: And they went out of the prison, and entered into the house of Lydia and when they had seen the brethren, they comforted them, and departed.
051640.phonetic: U-ni-nu-go-tsv-no di-da-s-du-di-yi Li-di ga-ne-lv wu-ni-yv-lv-gi; du-ni-go-hv-no a-na-da-nv-tli, du-ni-ga-li-s-da-dv-gi, a-le u-na-ni-gi-sv-gi.

051701.cherokee: ᎥᏈᎪᎵᏱᏃ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏆᎶᏂᏯ ᎤᏁᏙᏅ, ᏕᏏᎶᏂᎦ ᏭᏂᎷᏨᎩ, ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏓᏂᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᏓᏁᎸᎩ.
051701.english: Now when they had passed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where was a synagogue of the Jews:
051701.phonetic: V-qui-go-li-yi-no a-le A-qua-lo-ni-ya u-ne-do-nv, De-si-lo-ni-ga wu-ni-lu-tsv-gi, na-hna A-ni-tsu-si da-ni-la-wi-i-s-di-yi a-da-ne-lv-gi.

051702.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏛᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏄᏛᏁᎸᎩ, ᏚᏴᏎᎸᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏦᎢ ᏄᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᎬ ᎪᏪᎵ ᎬᏗᏍᎬ ᏕᎦᏬᏁᏗᏍᎬᎩ,
051702.english: And Paul, as his manner was, went in unto them, and three sabbath days reasoned with them out of the scriptures,
051702.phonetic: Quo-la-no, na-s-gi i-yu-dv-ne-di ge-sv nu-dv-ne-lv-gi, du-yv-se-lv-gi, a-le tso-i nu-na-do-da-qua-s-gv go-we-li gv-di-s-gv de-ga-wo-ne-di-s-gv-gi,

051703.cherokee: ᏕᎪᏏᏏᏍᎬᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᎬᏁᎲᎩ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎠᏎ ᎤᎩᎵᏲᎢᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏲᎱᏒ ᏧᎵᎯᏐᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏌ ᎠᏴ ᏥᏨᏯᎵᏥᏙᏁᎭ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ, ᎠᏗᏍᎬᎩ.
051703.english: Opening and alleging, that Christ must needs have suffered, and risen again from the dead; and that this Jesus, whom I preach unto you, is Christ.
051703.phonetic: de-go-si-si-s-gv-gi a-le gv-ni-ge-sv ni-gv-ne-hv-gi Ga-lo-ne-dv a-se u-gi-li-yo-i-s-di ge-sv-i, a-le u-yo-hu-sv tsu-li-hi-so-di ge-sv-i; a-le hi-a na-s-gi Tsi-sa a-yv tsi-tsv-ya-li-tsi-do-ne-ha na-s-gi Ga-lo-ne-dv, a-di-s-gv-gi.

051704.cherokee: ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎤᎾᏓᏑᏴ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎾᎵᎪᏁᎸᎩ ᏉᎳ ᎠᎴ ᏌᏱᎳ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏣᏔᏅᎯ ᎠᏂᎪᎢ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎾᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᎦᏲᎵᏉ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏄᏂᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏂᎨᏴ.
051704.english: And some of them believed, and consorted with Paul and Silas; and of the devout Greeks a great multitude, and of the chief women not a few.
051704.phonetic: I-ga-dv-no u-na-da-su-yv u-no-hi-yu-nv-gi, a-le du-na-li-go-ne-lv-gi Quo-la a-le Sa-yi-la, a-le u-ni-tsa-ta-nv-hi A-ni-go-i U-ne-la-nv-hi a-na-da-do-li-s-da-ne-hi, a-le a-ni-ga-yo-li-quo ni-ge-sv-na nu-ni-gv-wi-yu-sv a-ni-ge-yv.

051705.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏄᏃᎯᏳᏒᎾ, ᎠᏅᏳᎬ ᏅᏓᏳᎵᏍᏙᏔᏅᎩ, ᏫᏚᏂᏯᏅᎲᎩ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᏁᎫᏥᏛ ᎤᏁᏙᎯ, ᏚᏂᏟᏌᏅᏃ ᎤᏂᏖᎸᏅᎩ ᎦᏚᎲᎢ; ᏚᏂᎦᏘᎴᏅᏃ ᏤᏌᏂ ᎦᏁᎸᎢ, ᏴᏫ ᏧᏂᏄᎪᏫᏎᏗᏱ ᎤᎾᏁᎶᏔᏅᎩ.
051705.english: But the Jews which believed not, moved with envy, took unto them certain lewd fellows of the baser sort, and gathered a company, and set all the city on an uproar, and assaulted the house of Jason, and sought to bring them out to the people.
051705.phonetic: A-se-no A-ni-tsu-si nu-no-hi-yu-sv-na, a-nv-yu-gv nv-da-yu-li-s-do-ta-nv-gi, wi-du-ni-ya-nv-hv-gi gi-lo i-yu-na-s-di u-ni-ne-gu-tsi-dv u-ne-do-hi, du-ni-tli-sa-nv-no u-ni-te-lv-nv-gi ga-du-hv-i; du-ni-ga-ti-le-nv-no Tse-sa-ni ga-ne-lv-i, yv-wi tsu-ni-nu-go-wi-se-di-yi u-na-ne-lo-ta-nv-gi.

051706.cherokee: ᏫᏚᎾᏠᏨᏃ ᏫᏚᏂᎾᏌᏁᏒᎩ ᏤᏌᏂ ᎠᎴ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏟ, ᎦᏚᎲ ᎨᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗ ᏫᏚᎾᏘᏃᎮᎸᎩ, ᎤᏁᎷᎬᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎬᎩ; ᎯᎠ ᎡᎶᎯ ᏧᏂᎷᏆᏗᏅᏛ, ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏂ ᎢᎤᏂᎷᏨ;
051706.english: And when they found them not, they drew Jason and certain brethren unto the rulers of the city, crying, These that have turned the world upside down are come hither also;
051706.phonetic: Wi-du-na-tlo-tsv-no wi-du-ni-na-sa-ne-sv-gi Tse-sa-ni a-le gi-lo i-yu-na-s-di a-na-da-nv-tli, ga-du-hv ge-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-di wi-du-na-ti-no-he-lv-gi, u-ne-lu-gv-gi hi-a na-ni-we-s-gv-gi; hi-a e-lo-hi tsu-ni-lu-qua-di-nv-dv, na-s-quo a-ni i-u-ni-lu-tsv;

051707.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏤᏌᏂ ᏚᏓᏂᎸᏨ; ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏚᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲ ᎠᎾᏡᏗᎭ ᏏᏌ ᎤᏁᏨᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᎭ; ᏅᏩᏓᎴ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎡᎭ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏌ.
051707.english: Whom Jason hath received: and these all do contrary to the decrees of Caesar, saying that there is another king, one Jesus.
051707.phonetic: Na-s-gi Tse-sa-ni du-da-ni-lv-tsv; a-le na-s-gi ni-ga-dv du-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv a-na-tlu-di-ha Si-sa u-ne-tsv-i, hi-a na-ni-we-ha; Nv-wa-da-le u-gv-wi-yu-hi e-ha, na-s-gi Tsi-sa.

051708.cherokee: ᏚᎾᏕᏯᏙᏔᏅᎩᏃ ᏴᏫ ᎠᎴ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎨᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ.
051708.english: And they troubled the people and the rulers of the city, when they heard these things.
051708.phonetic: Du-na-de-ya-do-ta-nv-gi-no yv-wi a-le ga-du-hv ge-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-di, na-s-gi hi-a u-na-dv-ga-nv.

051709.cherokee: ᏤᏌᏂᏃ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏐᎢ ᏔᎵ ᎬᏩᎾᏛᏓᏁᎯ ᏚᏂᏩᏛᎲ ᏙᎨᏥᎧᏅᎩ.
051709.english: And when they had taken security of Jason, and of the other, they let them go.
051709.phonetic: Tse-sa-ni-no a-le a-ni-so-i ta-li gv-wa-na-dv-da-ne-hi du-ni-wa-dv-hv do-ge-tsi-ka-nv-gi.

051710.cherokee: ᎠᎾᎵᏅᏟᏃ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᏚᎾᏘᎾᏫᏛᎲᎩ ᏉᎳ ᎠᎴ ᏌᏱᎳ, ᏈᎵᏯ ᏒᏃᏱ ᏫᏚᎾᏘᏅᏍᏔᏅᎩ.  ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎾᎿᎭᏭᏂᎷᏨ, ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏧᏂᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᏭᏂᏴᎸᎩ.
051710.english: And the brethren immediately sent away Paul and Silas by night unto Berea: who coming thither went into the synagogue of the Jews.
051710.phonetic: A-na-li-nv-tli-no gi-la-quo i-yv-dv du-na-ti-na-wi-dv-hv-gi Quo-la a-le Sa-yi-la, Qui-li-ya sv-no-yi wi-du-na-ti-nv-s-ta-nv-gi.  Na-s-gi-no na-hna wu-ni-lu-tsv, A-ni-tsu-si tsu-ni-la-wi-i-s-di-yi wu-ni-yv-lv-gi.

051711.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎤᏟ ᎠᏃᏏᏳ ᎨᏒᎩ ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᏕᏏᎶᏂᎦ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᏚᎾᏓᏂᎸᏨᎩᏰᏃ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎠᎴ ᏂᏚᎩᏨᏂᏒ ᏓᏂᎪᎵᏰᏍᎬᎩ ᎪᏪᎵ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏍᏛᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏗᏱ.
051711.english: These were more noble than those in Thessalonica, in that they received the word with all readiness of mind, and searched the scriptures daily, whether those things were so.
051711.phonetic: Na-s-gi hi-a u-tli a-no-si-yu ge-sv-gi e-s-ga-quo De-si-lo-ni-ga a-ne-hi, du-na-da-ni-lv-tsv-gi-ye-no ka-no-he-dv a-le ni-du-gi-tsv-ni-sv da-ni-go-li-ye-s-gv-gi go-we-li, na-s-gi nu-s-dv a-le na-s-gi nu-s-dv-na ge-sv u-na-do-le-ho-hi-s-di-yi.

051712.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᏣᏛᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎨᏥᎸᏉᏗ ᎠᏂᎪᎢ ᎠᏂᎨᏴ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᎠᏂᎦᏲᎵᏉ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
051712.english: Therefore many of them believed; also of honourable women which were Greeks, and of men, not a few.
051712.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di u-ni-tsa-dv-gi na-s-gi u-no-hi-yu-nv-gi, a-le ge-tsi-lv-quo-di A-ni-go-i a-ni-ge-yv, a-le a-ni-s-ga-ya a-ni-ga-yo-li-quo ni-ge-sv-na.

051713.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏕᏏᎶᏂᎦ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ ᏉᎳ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏈᎵᏯ ᎠᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎬ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ, ᎾᎿᎭᎾᏍᏉ ᏭᏂᎷᏨᎩ, ᏴᏫ ᏚᏂᏖᎸᏅᎩ.
051713.english: But when the Jews of Thessalonica had knowledge that the word of God was preached of Paul at Berea, they came thither also, and stirred up the people.
051713.phonetic: A-se-no A-ni-tsu-si De-si-lo-ni-ga a-ne-hi, u-na-dv-ga-nv Quo-la na-s-quo Qui-li-ya a-li-tsi-do-hv-s-gv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga ka-no-he-dv, na-hna na-s-quo wu-ni-lu-tsv-gi, yv-wi du-ni-te-lv-nv-gi.

051714.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎩᎳᏉ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏟ ᎬᏩᏘᎿᎭᏫᏛᎲᎩ ᏉᎳ, ᎠᎺᏉᎯ ᎢᏗᏢ ᏫᎬᏩᏘᏅᏍᏔᏅᎩ.  ᏌᏱᎳᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎹᏗ ᎾᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᎾᏗᎩᏴᎩ.
051714.english: And then immediately the brethren sent away Paul to go as it were to the sea: but Silas and Timotheus abode there still.
051714.phonetic: Hna-quo-no gi-la-quo i-yv-dv a-na-da-nv-tli gv-wa-ti-hna-wi-dv-hv-gi Quo-la, a-me-quo-hi i-di-tlv wi-gv-wa-ti-nv-s-ta-nv-gi.  Sa-yi-la-s-gi-ni a-le Di-ma-di na-hna-quo u-na-di-gi-yv-gi.

051715.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᎬᏩᏘᎿᎭᏫᏛᏛ ᎡᏗᏂᏱ ᏫᎬᏩᏘᏃᎸᎩ; ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎨᏥᏁᏤᎸ ᏌᏱᎳ ᏗᎹᏗᏃ ᎤᎾᏛᎪᏗ, ᏄᎵᏍᏛᏉ ᎬᏩᎷᏤᏗᏱ, ᎤᎾᏂᎩᏒᎩ.
051715.english: And they that conducted Paul brought him unto Athens: and receiving a commandment unto Silas and Timotheus for to come to him with all speed, they departed.
051715.phonetic: Quo-la-no gv-wa-ti-hna-wi-dv-dv E-di-ni-yi wi-gv-wa-ti-no-lv-gi; a-le na-s-gi ge-tsi-ne-tse-lv Sa-yi-la Di-ma-di-no u-na-dv-go-di, nu-li-s-dv-quo gv-wa-lu-tse-di-yi, u-na-ni-gi-sv-gi.

051716.cherokee: ᎠᏏᏉᏃ ᏉᎳ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏓᎦᏘᏴᎩ ᎡᏗᏂᏱ, ᏧᏓᏅᏛ ᎤᏣᏘ ᎤᏕᏯᏔᏁᎸᎩ, ᎤᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏗᏰᎸᎯ ᏗᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ.
051716.english: Now while Paul waited for them at Athens, his spirit was stirred in him, when he saw the city wholly given to idolatry.
051716.phonetic: A-si-quo-no Quo-la na-s-gi da-ga-ti-yv-gi E-di-ni-yi, tsu-da-nv-dv u-tsa-ti u-de-ya-ta-ne-lv-gi, u-do-le-ho-sv ga-du-hv U-ne-la-nv-hi di-ye-lv-hi di-da-do-li-s-da-ne-hi ge-sv-i.

051717.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏕᎦᏬᏁᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎾᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᏚᎩᏨᏂᏒ ᎦᏃᏗᏗᏱ ᏕᎦᏬᏁᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᎾᎿᎭᏧᏩᏛᏔᏅᎯ.
051717.english: Therefore disputed he in the synagogue with the Jews, and with the devout persons, and in the market daily with them that met with him.
051717.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no i-yu-s-di de-ga-wo-ne-di-s-gv-gi di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi A-ni-tsu-si a-le U-ne-la-nv-hi a-na-da-do-li-s-da-ne-hi, a-le ni-du-gi-tsv-ni-sv ga-no-di-di-yi de-ga-wo-ne-di-s-gv-gi na-hna tsu-wa-dv-ta-nv-hi.

051718.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎿᎭᎢ ᎠᏂᏈᎫᎳ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏍᏙᎢᎦ ᎬᏩᎵᏃᎮᏔᏅᎩ; ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎦᏙ ᎤᏚᎵ ᎤᏛᏗᏱ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏰᎸᏛᎾ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎩ; ᎠᏂᏐᎢᏃ ᎯᎠ; ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴ ᏴᏫ ᏧᎾᏤᎵ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎠᏁᎯ ᏗᎦᎾᏄᎪᏫᏍᎩ ᏅᏩᏍᏗ; ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᏓᎵᏥᏙᏁᎲᎢ ᏥᏌ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎴᎯᏐᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ.
051718.english: Then certain philosophers of the Epicureans, and of the Stoicks, encountered him. And some said, What will this babbler say? other some, He seemeth to be a setter forth of strange gods: because he preached unto them Jesus, and the resurrection.
051718.phonetic: Gi-lo-no i-yu-na-s-di a-ni-ga-ta-hna-i A-ni-qui-gu-la a-le A-ni-s-do-i-ga gv-wa-li-no-he-ta-nv-gi; i-ga-dv-no hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Ga-do u-du-li u-dv-di-yi hi-a nu-ye-lv-dv-na ga-wo-ni-s-gi; a-ni-so-i-no hi-a; Nv-wa-na-da-le yv-wi tsu-na-tse-li ga-lv-la-di a-ne-hi di-ga-na-nu-go-wi-s-gi nv-wa-s-di; nv-da-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv-gi da-li-tsi-do-ne-hv-i Tsi-sa a-le di-le-hi-so-di ge-sv u-gv-wa-li.

051719.cherokee: ᏫᎬᏩᏯᏅᎲᏃ ᎡᎵᎣᏈᎦ ᎬᏩᏘᏃᎸᎩ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᏥᎪ ᎦᏲᎦᏛᎪᏗᏉ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎢᏤ ᏗᏕᏲᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᏃᎮᎭ?
051719.english: And they took him, and brought him unto Areopagus, saying, May we know what this new doctrine, whereof thou speakest, is?
051719.phonetic: Wi-gv-wa-ya-nv-hv-no E-li-o-qui-ga gv-wa-ti-no-lv-gi, hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Tsi-go ga-yo-ga-dv-go-di-quo nu-s-dv na-s-gi hi-a i-tse di-de-yo-di ge-sv na-s-gi tsi-no-he-ha?

051720.cherokee: ᎪᎱᏍᏗᏰᏃ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏕᎯᎾᏄᎪᏫᏎᎭ ᏦᏥᎴᏂ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎣᎦᏚᎵᎭ ᎣᎦᏙᎴᎰᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏍᏛ ᎦᏛᎬᎢ.
051720.english: For thou bringest certain strange things to our ears: we would know therefore what these things mean.
051720.phonetic: Go-hu-s-di-ye-no u-s-qua-ni-go-di de-hi-na-nu-go-wi-se-ha tso-tsi-le-ni; na-s-gi i-yu-s-di o-ga-du-li-ha o-ga-do-le-ho-hi-s-di-yi na-s-gi nu-s-dv ga-dv-gv-i.

051721.cherokee: ᏂᎦᏛᏰᏃ ᎡᏗᏂᏱ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎢᎸᎯᏢ ᏗᏁᎯ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏁᏙᎯ ᎥᏝ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏱᏚᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎮᎢ, ᎠᏂᏃᎮᏍᎬ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎾᏛᎩᏍᎬ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎢᏤᎢ.
051721.english: (For all the Athenians and strangers which were there spent their time in nothing else, but either to tell, or to hear some new thing.)
051721.phonetic: Ni-ga-dv-ye-no E-di-ni-yi a-ne-hi a-le i-lv-hi-tlv di-ne-hi na-hna a-ne-do-hi v-tla go-hu-s-di yi-du-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-he-i, a-ni-no-he-s-gv u-wa-sv a-le a-na-dv-gi-s-gv go-hu-s-di i-tse-i.

051722.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏉᎳ ᏕᎤᎴᏅ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎡᎵᎣᏈᎦ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏯ ᎡᏗᏂᏱ ᎢᏤᎯ, ᎦᏙᎴᎣᏍᎦ ᎢᏥᎧᎵᏨᎯ ᎨᏒ ᏕᏥᎾᏰᏍᎬ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎠᏁᎯ.
051722.english: Then Paul stood in the midst of Mars' hill, and said, Ye men of Athens, I perceive that in all things ye are too superstitious.
051722.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Quo-la de-u-le-nv a-ye-li E-li-o-qui-ga, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; I-tsi-s-ga-ya E-di-ni-yi i-tse-hi, ga-do-le-o-s-ga i-tsi-ka-li-tsv-hi ge-sv de-tsi-na-ye-s-gv ga-lv-la-di a-ne-hi.

051723.cherokee: ᎦᎢᏒᏰᏃ ᎠᎴ ᏓᎩᎪᎲ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᏗᏥᏰᎸᎯ, ᎠᎩᏩᏛᎲᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏥᎸ-ᎨᎳᏍᏗᏱ ᎯᎠ ᏅᎬᏅ ᎥᎪᏪᎸᎩ; "ᎾᏥᎦᏔᎲᎾ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ."  ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎰᏩ ᏁᏥᎦᏔᎲᎾ ᏤᏣᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎭ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᏨᏴᏁᎭ.
051723.english: For as I passed by, and beheld your devotions, I found an altar with this inscription, TO THE UNKNOWN GOD. Whom therefore ye ignorantly worship, him declare I unto you.
051723.phonetic: Ga-i-sv-ye-no a-le da-gi-go-hv ga-lv-quo-di-yu di-tsi-ye-lv-hi, a-gi-wa-dv-hv-gi na-s-quo a-tsi-lv--ge-la-s-di-yi hi-a nv-gv-nv v-go-we-lv-gi; "Na-Tsi-Ga-Ta-Hv-Na Ga-Lv-La-Di E-Hi U-Tse-Li-Ga."  Na-s-gi-no ho-wa ne-tsi-ga-ta-hv-na tse-tsa-da-do-li-s-da-ne-ha na-s-gi gv-ni-ge-sv ni-tsv-yv-ne-ha.

051724.cherokee: ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏬᏢᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ ᎡᎳᏂᎬᎢ ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏁᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎡᎶᎯ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ, ᎥᏝ ᏱᎦᏁᎸ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎩᎶ ᏧᏬᏱ ᏧᏮᏔᏅᎯ ᏧᏁᏍᎨᎲᎯ.
051724.english: God that made the world and all things therein, seeing that he is Lord of heaven and earth, dwelleth not in temples made with hands;
051724.phonetic: U-ne-la-nv-hi u-wo-tlv-nv-hi tsi-gi e-la-ni-gv-i a-le ni-ga-dv na-hna a-ne-hi, na-s-gi U-gv-wi-yu-hi ga-lv-la-di a-le e-lo-hi u-na-tse-li-ga, v-tla yi-ga-ne-lv di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi gi-lo tsu-wo-yi tsu-wv-ta-nv-hi tsu-ne-s-ge-hv-hi.

051725.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏴ ᏧᏃᏰᏂ ᏱᏚᎵᏍᏕᎸᏗ, ᏧᏂᎬᎪ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᎢ, ᏕᎠᏁᎮᏰᏃ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏓᏅᏅᎢ ᎠᎴ ᏓᏅᏬᎳᏕᏍᎬ ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ.
051725.english: Neither is worshipped with men's hands, as though he needed any thing, seeing he giveth to all life, and breath, and all things;
051725.phonetic: V-tla a-le na-s-gi a-yv tsu-no-ye-ni yi-du-li-s-de-lv-di, tsu-ni-gv-go go-hu-s-di na-s-gi-ya-i, de-a-ne-he-ye-no ni-ga-dv da-nv-nv-i a-le da-nv-wo-la-de-s-gv a-le ni-ga-v go-hu-s-di.

051726.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᏌᏉ ᎩᎬ ᏚᏬᏢᏔᏅ ᎾᏂᎥ ᏄᎾᏓᎴᏒ ᏴᏫ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᎾᏁᎳᏗᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᎦᏚᎢᏗᏢ ᎡᎶᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᎤᏭᎪᏔᏅ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏔᏂᏓᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏫᏚᏍᏖᏅ ᎾᎿᎭᏕᎨᏌᏗᏒ ᏧᎾᏁᎳᏗᏍᏗᏱ.
051726.english: And hath made of one blood all nations of men for to dwell on all the face of the earth, and hath determined the times before appointed, and the bounds of their habitation;
051726.phonetic: A-le sa-quo gi-gv du-wo-tlv-ta-nv na-ni-v nu-na-da-le-sv yv-wi na-s-gi tsu-na-ne-la-di-s-di-yi ni-gv-na-dv ga-du-i-di-tlv e-lo-hi, a-le de-u-wu-go-ta-nv i-yu-li-s-ta-ni-da-s-di-yi, a-le wi-du-s-te-nv na-hna de-ge-sa-di-sv tsu-na-ne-la-di-s-di-yi.

051727.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏂᏯᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᎾᏒᏂᏍᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏂᏩᏛᏗ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗᏱ, ᎥᏝᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎢᏗᏏᏴᏫᎭ ᏂᎦᏛᎿᎭᏕᎬᎢ ᎢᏅᎯᏳ ᏱᏄᏛᎿᎭᏕᎦ.
051727.english: That they should seek the Lord, if haply they might feel after him, and find him, though he be not far from every one of us:
051727.phonetic: Na-s-gi u-ni-ya-s-di-yi U-gv-wi-yu-hi, na-s-gi gv-wa-na-sv-ni-s-di a-le gv-wa-ni-wa-dv-di i-yu-li-s-do-di-yi, v-tla-s-gi-ni-no-nv i-di-si-yv-wi-ha ni-ga-dv-hna-de-gv-i i-nv-hi-yu yi-nu-dv-hna-de-ga.

051728.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᏄᏩᏅ ᏥᏕᏛᏅ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᏓᎵᏖᎸᎲᏍᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᏥᏕᎭ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᏗᎧᏃᎩᏍᏗ ᏗᏃᏪᎵᏍᎩ ᏗᏣᏤᎵᎦ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒ; "ᎠᏴᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᏪᏥ."
051728.english: For in him we live, and move, and have our being; as certain also of your own poets have said, For we are also his offspring.
051728.phonetic: Na-s-gi-ye-no nu-wa-nv tsi-de-dv-nv, a-le tsi-da-li-te-lv-hv-s-ga, a-le tsi-de-ha; na-s-gi na-s-quo gi-lo i-yu-na-s-di di-ka-no-gi-s-di di-no-we-li-s-gi di-tsa-tse-li-ga hi-a nu-ni-we-sv; "A-yv-ye-no na-s-quo na-s-gi tsu-we-tsi."

051729.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ, ᎠᏴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏧᏪᏥ ᏥᎩ, ᎥᏝ ᎯᎠᏉ ᏱᏂᎨᏕᎵᎭ; ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎾᏍᎩᏯᏉ ᎠᏕᎸ ᏓᎶᏂᎨ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏕᎸ ᎤᏁᎬ ᎠᎴ ᏅᏯ, ᏴᏫ ᎠᏂᏏᎾᏌᏅ ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏖᎸᎯ ᎬᏔᏅᎯ ᎪᏢᏅᎯ.
051729.english: Forasmuch then as we are the offspring of God, we ought not to think that the Godhead is like unto gold, or silver, or stone, graven by art and man's device.
051729.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no i-yu-s-di, a-yv U-ne-la-nv-hi tsu-we-tsi tsi-gi, v-tla hi-a-quo yi-ni-ge-de-li-ha; U-ne-la-nv-hi na-s-gi-ya-quo a-de-lv da-lo-ni-ge a-le a-de-lv u-ne-gv a-le nv-ya, yv-wi a-ni-si-na-sa-nv a-le u-na-da-nv-te-lv-hi gv-ta-nv-hi go-tlv-nv-hi.

051730.cherokee: ᎾᎯᏳᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᎦᏔᎲᎾ ᏥᎨᏎᎢ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᎤᏪᎵᏎᎢ; ᎠᏎᏃ ᎿᎭᏉ ᏕᎧᏁᏤᎭ ᎾᏂᎥ ᏴᏫ ᏂᎬᎾᏛᎢ ᏧᏂᏁᏟᏴᏍᏗᏱ ᏚᎾ ᏓᏅᏛᎢ;
051730.english: And the times of this ignorance God winked at; but now commandeth all men every where to repent:
051730.phonetic: Na-hi-yu-no na-s-gi hi-a na-ni-ga-ta-hv-na tsi-ge-se-i U-ne-la-nv-hi u-ne-la-gi u-we-li-se-i; a-se-no hna-quo de-ka-ne-tse-ha na-ni-v yv-wi ni-gv-na-dv-i tsu-ni-ne-tli-yv-s-di-yi du-na da-nv-dv-i;

051731.cherokee: ᏕᎤᏒᏍᏔᏅᏰᏃ ᎢᎦ ᎾᎯᏳ ᎡᎶᎯ ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᏧᏭᎪᏓᏁᏗᏱ, ᎬᏗᏍᎬ ᎠᏍᎦᏯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏪᎧᏅᎯ, ᏄᏜᏓᏏᏛᏒᎾ ᏂᏚᏩᏁᎸ ᎾᏂᎥ ᏴᏫ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏕᎤᎴᏔᏅᎢ ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎢ.
051731.english: Because he hath appointed a day, in the which he will judge the world in righteousness by that man whom he hath ordained; whereof he hath given assurance unto all men, in that he hath raised him from the dead.
051731.phonetic: De-u-sv-s-ta-nv-ye-no i-ga na-hi-yu e-lo-hi du-yu-go-dv tsu-wu-go-da-ne-di-yi, gv-di-s-gv a-s-ga-ya na-s-gi u-we-ka-nv-hi, nu-dla-si-dv-sv-na ni-du-wa-ne-lv na-ni-v yv-wi na-s-gi de-u-le-ta-nv-i u-yo-hu-sv-i.

051732.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅᏃ ᎠᏲᎱᏒ ᏗᎴᎯᏐᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎢᎦᏛ ᎤᏂᏰᎵᎥᎩ, ᎢᎦᏛᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᏔᎵᏁ ᎯᎠ ᏛᏨᏯᏛᎦᏁᎵ.
051732.english: And when they heard of the resurrection of the dead, some mocked: and others said, We will hear thee again of this matter.
051732.phonetic: U-na-dv-ga-nv-no a-yo-hu-sv di-le-hi-so-di ge-sv-i, i-ga-dv u-ni-ye-li-v-gi, i-ga-dv-no hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Ta-li-ne hi-a dv-tsv-ya-dv-ga-ne-li.

051733.english: So Paul departed from among them.

051734.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᎬᏩᎵᎪᏁᎸᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏅᎩ; ᎾᎿᎭᎨᎸᎩ ᏓᏲᏂᏏᏯ ᎡᎵᎣᏈᎦ ᎦᎲᏍᎩ ᏗᎫᎪᏘᏍᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎠᎨᏴ ᏕᎺᎵ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ.
051734.english: Howbeit certain men clave unto him, and believed: among the which was Dionysius the Areopagite, and a woman named Damaris, and others with them.
051734.phonetic: Gi-lo-s-gi-ni-no-nv i-yu-na-s-di gv-wa-li-go-ne-lv-gi a-le u-no-hi-yu-nv-gi; na-hna ge-lv-gi Da-yo-ni-si-ya E-li-o-qui-ga ga-hv-s-gi di-gu-go-ti-s-gi a-le a-ge-yv De-me-li tsu-do-i-dv, a-le nv-wa-na-da-le na-s-quo.

051801.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏂᏙᎸ ᏉᎳ ᎡᏗᏂᏱ ᎤᏓᏅᏒᎩ ᎪᎵᏂᏗᏱ ᏭᎷᏨᎩ.
051801.english: After these things Paul departed from Athens, and came to Corinth;
051801.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no nu-li-s-ta-ni-do-lv Quo-la E-di-ni-yi u-da-nv-sv-gi Go-li-ni-di-yi wu-lu-tsv-gi.

051802.cherokee: ᎤᏩᏛᎲᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏧᏏ ᎡᏈᎳ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᏋᏗᏱ ᎤᏕᏅᎯ, ᎩᎳᎢ ᎤᎷᏦᏅᎯ ᎢᏓᎵ ᏅᏓᏳᎶᏒᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏓᎵᎢ ᏈᏏᎳ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᏠᏗᏯ ᎤᏁᏨ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎶᎻ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏍᏗᏱ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᏩᏛᎯᎸᎩ.
051802.english: And found a certain Jew named Aquila, born in Pontus, lately come from Italy, with his wife Priscilla; (because that Claudius had commanded all Jews to depart from Rome:) and came unto them.
051802.phonetic: U-wa-dv-hv-no gi-lo i-yu-s-di A-tsu-si E-qui-la tsu-do-i-dv, Quv-di-yi u-de-nv-hi, gi-la-i u-lu-tso-nv-hi I-da-li nv-da-yu-lo-sv-hi, a-le na-s-quo u-da-li-i Qui-si-la tsu-do-i-dv, nv-da-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv Tlo-di-ya u-ne-tsv ni-ga-dv A-ni-tsu-si Lo-mi u-na-da-nv-s-di-yi, na-s-gi du-wa-dv-hi-lv-gi.

051803.cherokee: ᎤᏠᏱᏃ ᏧᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎯ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᏁᎸ ᎤᏪᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸᎩ; ᏚᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲᏰᏃ ᏗᎦᎵᏦᏙᏗ ᏗᏃᏢᏍᎩ ᎨᏒᎢ.
051803.english: And because he was of the same craft, he abode with them, and wrought: for by their occupation they were tentmakers.
051803.phonetic: U-tlo-yi-no tsu-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hi ge-sv-i, na-s-gi i-yu-s-di a-ni-ne-lv u-we-nv-gi, a-le du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv-gi; du-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv-ye-no di-ga-li-tso-do-di di-no-tlv-s-gi ge-sv-i.

051804.cherokee: ᏄᎾᏙᏓᏈᏒᏃ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏧᏂᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎬᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏓᏍᏗᏰᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᎪᎢ.
051804.english: And he reasoned in the synagogue every sabbath, and persuaded the Jews and the Greeks.
051804.phonetic: Nu-na-do-da-qui-sv-no A-ni-tsu-si tsu-ni-la-wi-i-s-di-yi ga-wo-ni-s-gv-gi, a-le da-s-di-ye-di-s-gv-gi A-ni-tsu-si a-le A-ni-go-i.

051805.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏌᏱᎳ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎹᏗ ᎤᏂᎷᏨ, ᎹᏏᏙᏂ ᏧᏂᎶᏒ, ᏉᎳ ᎤᏓᏅᏛ ᎤᏱᎸᏒᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᏚᏩᏁᎸᎩ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏥᏌ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ.
051805.english: And when Silas and Timotheus were come from Macedonia, Paul was pressed in the spirit, and testified to the Jews that Jesus was Christ.
051805.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Sa-yi-la a-le Di-ma-di u-ni-lu-tsv, Ma-si-do-ni tsu-ni-lo-sv, Quo-la u-da-nv-dv u-yi-lv-sv-gi, a-le na-s-gi gv-ni-ge-sv ni-du-wa-ne-lv-gi A-ni-tsu-si Tsi-sa na-s-gi Ga-lo-ne-dv ge-sv-i.

051806.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᏂᏲᏍᏙᏔᏅ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏂᏐᏢᎢᏍᏔᏅ, ᎤᏄᏬ ᎤᏩᏣᎬᏅᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎢᏥᎩᎬ ᏗᏥᏍᎪᎵ ᎢᏨᏒ ᏕᏣᏓᏅᎵᏰᎡᏍᏗ, ᎠᏴ ᎥᏝ ᏯᏆᏓᏅᎵᏰᎥ; ᎿᎭᏉ ᎪᎯ ᎢᏳᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏧᎾᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏴᏫ ᏗᏁᎲ ᏮᏓᏥᎶᏏ.
051806.english: And when they opposed themselves, and blasphemed, he shook his raiment, and said unto them, Your blood be upon your own heads; I am clean: from henceforth I will go unto the Gentiles.
051806.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-ni-yo-s-do-ta-nv a-le u-ni-so-tlv-i-s-ta-nv, u-nu-wo u-wa-tsa-gv-nv-gi hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; I-tsi-gi-gv di-tsi-s-go-li i-tsv-sv de-tsa-da-nv-li-ye-e-s-di, a-yv v-tla ya-qua-da-nv-li-ye-v; hna-quo go-hi i-yu-da-le-nv-dv tsu-na-da-le-nv-dv yv-wi di-ne-hv wv-da-tsi-lo-si.

051807.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎤᏂᎩᏒ, ᏭᏴᎸᎩ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎦᏁᎸ ᏣᏍᏓ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏁᎸ ᏚᏓᏯᎸᎩ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ.
051807.english: And he departed thence, and entered into a certain man's house, named Justus, one that worshipped God, whose house joined hard to the synagogue.
051807.phonetic: Na-hna-no u-ni-gi-sv, wu-yv-lv-gi gi-lo i-yu-s-di ga-ne-lv tsa-s-da tsu-do-i-dv, U-ne-la-nv-hi a-da-do-li-s-da-ne-hi, na-s-gi ga-ne-lv du-da-ya-lv-gi di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi.

051808.cherokee: ᏟᏍᏆᏃ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗ ᎤᏬᎯᏳᏅᎩ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ, ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏚᏓᏘᎾᎥᎢ, ᎤᏂᏣᏘᏃ ᎪᎵᏂᏗᏱ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏅᎩ; ᎠᎴ ᏕᎨᎦᏬᎥᎩ.
051808.english: And Crispus, the chief ruler of the synagogue, believed on the Lord with all his house; and many of the Corinthians hearing believed, and were baptized.
051808.phonetic: Tli-s-qua-no di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi nu-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-di u-wo-hi-yu-nv-gi U-gv-wi-yu-hi, a-le ni-ga-dv du-da-ti-na-v-i, u-ni-tsa-ti-no go-li-ni-di-yi a-ne-hi u-na-dv-ga-nv u-no-hi-yu-nv-gi; a-le de-ge-ga-wo-v-gi.

051809.cherokee: ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎴ ᏉᎳ ᏒᏃᏱ ᎤᏁᎳᏫᏎᎲᎢ; ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏍᎦᎢᎮᏍᏗ, ᎯᏂᎨᏍᏗᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᎴ ᏞᏍᏗ ᎡᎳᏪ ᏨᏅᎩ,
051809.english: Then spake the Lord to Paul in the night by a vision, Be not afraid, but speak, and hold not thy peace:
051809.phonetic: U-gv-wi-yu-hi-no hi-a nu-we-se-le Quo-la sv-no-yi u-ne-la-wi-se-hv-i; Tle-s-di yi-s-ga-i-he-s-di, hi-ni-ge-s-di-quo-s-gi-ni a-le tle-s-di e-la-we tsv-nv-gi,

051810.cherokee: ᎠᏴᏰᏃ ᏕᎬᎦᎿᎭᏩᏗᏙᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᏱᏙᎨᏣᏘᎷᎦ ᎤᏐᏅ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏨᏁᏗᏱ, ᎤᏂᏣᏔᏰᏃ ᏴᏫ ᎠᏂ ᎦᏚᎲ ᏓᎩᎧᎭ.
051810.english: For I am with thee, and no man shall set on thee to hurt thee: for I have much people in this city.
051810.phonetic: A-yv-ye-no de-gv-ga-hna-wa-di-do-ha, a-le v-tla gi-lo yi-do-ge-tsa-ti-lu-ga u-so-nv go-hu-s-di tsv-ne-di-yi, u-ni-tsa-ta-ye-no yv-wi a-ni ga-du-hv da-gi-ka-ha.

051811.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᏑᏕᏘᏴᏛ ᏑᏓᎵᏃ ᎢᏯᏅᏙ ᎤᏪᏙᎸᎩ, ᏓᏕᏲᎲᏍᎬᎩ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏁᎲᎢ.
051811.english: And he continued there a year and six months, teaching the word of God among them.
051811.phonetic: Na-hna-no su-de-ti-yv-dv su-da-li-no i-ya-nv-do u-we-do-lv-gi, da-de-yo-hv-s-gv-gi ka-no-he-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga na-hna a-ne-hv-i.

051812.cherokee: ᎨᎵᏲᏃ ᏂᎯᏳ ᎡᎧᏯ ᎠᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏛᎢ, ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏌᏉ ᎢᎦᎦᏛ ᎬᏩᏁᎷᎩᎡᎸᎩ ᏉᎳ, ᎠᎴ ᎬᏩᏘᏃᎸ ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᎦᏍᎩᎸᎢ,
051812.english: And when Gallio was the deputy of Achaia, the Jews made insurrection with one accord against Paul, and brought him to the judgment seat,
051812.phonetic: Ge-li-yo-no ni-hi-yu E-ka-ya a-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-dv-i, A-ni-tsu-si sa-quo i-ga-ga-dv gv-wa-ne-lu-gi-e-lv-gi Quo-la, a-le gv-wa-ti-no-lv di-gu-go-do-di-yi ga-s-gi-lv-i,

051813.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏓᏍᏗᏰᏗᎭ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎠᎾᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎲ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᏲᏍᏙᏗᏱ.
051813.english: Saying, This fellow persuadeth men to worship God contrary to the law.
051813.phonetic: Hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; hi-a na-s-gi da-s-di-ye-di-ha yv-wi U-ne-la-nv-hi a-na-da-do-li-s-da-ne-hv di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di u-ni-yo-s-do-di-yi.

051814.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏉᎳ ᏛᏍᏚᎢᏏᏒᎩ ᎠᎰᎵ, ᎨᎵᏲ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ; ᎢᏳᏃ ᎤᏲ ᎢᏳᏛᏁᎸᎯ ᏱᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏍᎦᏅᏨᎯ ᏱᎩ, ᎢᏥᏧᏏ, ᏚᏳᎪᏛ ᏱᎩ ᎢᏨᏯᏛᏓᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ.
051814.english: And when Paul was now about to open his mouth, Gallio said unto the Jews, If it were a matter of wrong or wicked lewdness, O ye Jews, reason would that I should bear with you:
051814.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Quo-la dv-s-du-i-si-sv-gi a-ho-li, Ge-li-yo hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi A-ni-tsu-si; I-yu-no u-yo i-yu-dv-ne-lv-hi yi-gi, a-le u-s-ga-nv-tsv-hi yi-gi, I-tsi-tsu-si, du-yu-go-dv yi-gi i-tsv-ya-dv-da-s-da-ne-di-yi.

051815.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏍᎩᏂ ᎧᏁᏨᎯ ᎠᎴ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏚᏙᎥ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᏍᏗ ᎢᏣᏤᎵᎦ ᎧᏃᎮᏗ ᏱᎩ, ᎢᏨᏒ ᏗᏥᎸᏫᏍᏓᏏ, ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏴ ᏱᏙᎦᎫᎪᏓ.
051815.english: But if it be a question of words and names, and of your law, look ye to it; for I will be no judge of such matters.
051815.phonetic: I-yu-s-gi-ni ka-ne-tsv-hi a-le go-hu-s-di du-do-v a-le di-ka-hna-wa-dv-s-di i-tsa-tse-li-ga ka-no-he-di yi-gi, i-tsv-sv di-tsi-lv-wi-s-da-si, v-tla-ye-no na-s-gi i-yu-s-di a-yv yi-do-ga-gu-go-da.

051816.english: And he drave them from the judgment seat.

051817.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᏂᎪᎢ ᎤᏂᏂᏴᎲ ᏐᏍᏗᏂ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏧᏂᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᎠᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗ, ᎤᏂᎵᎥᏂᎸᎩ ᎢᎬᏱᏢ ᏗᎫᎪᏙᏗᏱ ᎦᏍᎩᎸᎢ.  ᎨᎵᏲᏃ ᎥᏝ ᏳᎦᏌᏯᏍᏔᏁ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏄᏍᏗᏕᎬᎢ.
051817.english: Then all the Greeks took Sosthenes, the chief ruler of the synagogue, and beat him before the judgment seat. And Gallio cared for none of those things.
051817.phonetic: Hna-quo-no ni-ga-dv A-ni-go-i u-ni-ni-yv-hv So-s-di-ni A-ni-tsu-si tsu-ni-la-wi-i-s-di-yi nu-gv-wi-yu-sv a-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-di, u-ni-li-v-ni-lv-gi i-gv-yi-tlv di-gu-go-do-di-yi ga-s-gi-lv-i.  Ge-li-yo-no v-tla yu-ga-sa-ya-s-ta-ne na-s-gi nu-s-di-de-gv-i.

051818.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᎠᏏ ᎤᏬᎯᏨᎩ ᎤᏪᏙᎸᎩ; ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏚᏲᎵᎸ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏟ, ᏥᏳᎯ ᏭᏣᏅᎩ ᏏᎵᏱ ᏭᎶᏒᎩ; ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᏈᏏᎳ ᎠᎴ ᎡᏈᎳ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏒᎩ, ᎤᎵᏍᏙᏰᎲᎯ ᎨᏒᎩ ᏒᏟᏯ ᎢᏴᏛ, ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯᏰᏃ ᎤᏚᎢᏍᏓᏁᎸᎯ ᎨᏒᎩ.
051818.english: And Paul after this tarried there yet a good while, and then took his leave of the brethren, and sailed thence into Syria, and with him Priscilla and Aquila; having shorn his head in Cenchrea: for he had a vow.
051818.phonetic: Quo-la-no a-si u-wo-hi-tsv-gi u-we-do-lv-gi; hna-quo-no du-yo-li-lv a-na-da-nv-tli, tsi-yu-hi wu-tsa-nv-gi Si-li-yi wu-lo-sv-gi; a-le na-s-quo Qui-si-la a-le E-qui-la gv-wa-s-da-wa-dv-sv-gi, u-li-s-do-ye-hv-hi ge-sv-gi Sv-tli-ya i-yv-dv, U-ne-la-nv-hi-ye-no u-du-i-s-da-ne-lv-hi ge-sv-gi.

051819.cherokee: ᎡᏈᏌᏃ ᏭᎷᏨᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎾᎿᎭᏚᏩᏕᎲᎩ, ᎤᏩᏒᏍᎩᏂ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ ᏭᏴᎸᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏚᏬᏁᏔᏅᎩ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ.
051819.english: And he came to Ephesus, and left them there: but he himself entered into the synagogue, and reasoned with the Jews.
051819.phonetic: E-qui-sa-no wu-lu-tsv-gi a-le na-hna du-wa-de-hv-gi, u-wa-sv-s-gi-ni di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi wu-yv-lv-gi a-le du-wo-ne-ta-nv-gi A-ni-tsu-si.

051820.english: When they desired him to tarry longer time with them, he consented not;

051821.cherokee: ᏚᏲᎵᎸᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎠᏎ ᎠᎩᏍᏆᏂᎪᏙᏗ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᎯᎠ ᏗᎵᏍᏓᏴᏗᏱ ᏨᏛᏍᏆᎸᎯ.  ᎠᏎᏃ ᏔᎵᏁ ᏛᏨᏩᏛᎯᎵ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏏᏳ ᏳᏰᎸᏅ.  ᎡᏈᏌᏃ ᏥᏳᎯ ᏭᏣᏅᎩ.
051821.english: But bade them farewell, saying, I must by all means keep this feast that cometh in Jerusalem: but I will return again unto you, if God will. And he sailed from Ephesus.
051821.phonetic: Du-yo-li-lv-quo-s-gi-ni hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; A-se a-gi-s-qua-ni-go-do-di Tsi-lu-si-li-mi hi-a di-li-s-da-yv-di-yi tsv-dv-s-qua-lv-hi.  A-se-no ta-li-ne dv-tsv-wa-dv-hi-li, i-yu-no U-ne-la-nv-hi u-si-yu yu-ye-lv-nv.  E-qui-sa-no tsi-yu-hi wu-tsa-nv-gi.

051822.cherokee: ᏏᏌᎵᏱᏃ ᎤᏚᎩᏒ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬ ᏫᏚᏲᎵᎸ, ᎥᏘᎣᎩ ᏭᎶᏒᎩ.
051822.english: And when he had landed at Caesarea, and gone up, and saluted the church, he went down to Antioch.
051822.phonetic: Si-sa-li-yi-no u-du-gi-sv, a-le tsu-na-ne-lo-di u-na-da-tlu-gv wi-du-yo-li-lv, V-ti-o-gi wu-lo-sv-gi.

051823.cherokee: ᏰᎵᏃ ᎤᏬᎯᏨ ᎤᏪᏙᎸ, ᎤᏂᎩᏒᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎨᎴᏏᏱ ᎠᎴ ᏈᏥᏱ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎤᏱᎸᏒᎩ ᎤᏪᏐᎸᎩ, ᏓᎵᏂᎪᎯᏍᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ
051823.english: And after he had spent some time there, he departed, and went over all the country of Galatia and Phrygia in order, strengthening all the disciples.
051823.phonetic: Ye-li-no u-wo-hi-tsv u-we-do-lv, u-ni-gi-sv-gi, a-le Ge-le-si-yi a-le Qui-tsi-yi o-s-dv u-yi-lv-sv-gi u-we-so-lv-gi, da-li-ni-go-hi-s-di-s-gv-gi ni-ga-dv a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi

051824.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏧᏏ ᎠᏉᎳ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎡᎵᎩᏱ ᎤᏛᏒᎯ, ᎠᏏᎾᏍᏛ ᎤᏬᏂᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᏗᎦᎸᏉᏗ ᎪᏪᎵ ᏗᏏᎾᏍᏛ, ᎡᏈᏌ ᎤᎷᏨᎩ.
051824.english: And a certain Jew named Apollos, born at Alexandria, an eloquent man, and mighty in the scriptures, came to Ephesus.
051824.phonetic: Gi-lo-no i-yu-s-di A-tsu-si A-quo-la tsu-do-i-dv, E-li-gi-yi u-dv-sv-hi, a-si-na-s-dv u-wo-ni-hi-s-di-yi, a-le di-ga-lv-quo-di go-we-li di-si-na-s-dv, E-qui-sa u-lu-tsv-gi.

051825.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎠᎨᏲᏅᎯ ᎨᏒᎩ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏅᏅᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏛ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏓᏅᏛᎢ, ᎧᏃᎮᏍᎬᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏓᏕᏲᎲᏍᎬᎩ ᎾᏑᎵᎪᎬᎾ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏣᏂ ᎤᏤᎵ ᏗᏓᏬᏍᏗ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎠᎦᏔᎲᎩ.
051825.english: This man was instructed in the way of the Lord; and being fervent in the spirit, he spake and taught diligently the things of the Lord, knowing only the baptism of John.
051825.phonetic: Na-s-gi hi-a a-ge-yo-nv-hi ge-sv-gi U-gv-wi-yu-hi u-nv-nv-i, a-le u-li-ni-gi-dv ge-sv u-da-nv-dv-i, ka-no-he-s-gv-gi a-le da-de-yo-hv-s-gv-gi na-su-li-go-gv-na U-gv-wi-yu-hi u-tse-li u-gv-wa-li ge-sv-i, Tsa-ni u-tse-li di-da-wo-s-di u-wa-sv a-ga-ta-hv-gi.

051826.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎴᏅᎲᎩ ᎾᏍᎦᎢᎲᎾ ᎤᏬᏂᏒᎩ ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱ.  ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎡᏈᎳ ᎠᎴ ᏈᏏᎳ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏁᎸ ᎤᏂᏯᏅᎲᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏟ ᎢᏲᏍᏛ ᏚᏃᏏᏌᏁᎸᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵ ᎦᏅᏅᎢ.
051826.english: And he began to speak boldly in the synagogue: whom when Aquila and Priscilla had heard, they took him unto them, and expounded unto him the way of God more perfectly.
051826.phonetic: Na-s-gi u-le-nv-hv-gi na-s-ga-i-hv-na u-wo-ni-sv-gi di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi.  Na-s-gi-no E-qui-la a-le Qui-si-la u-na-dv-ga-ne-lv u-ni-ya-nv-hv-gi, a-le u-tli i-yo-s-dv du-no-si-sa-ne-lv-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li ga-nv-nv-i.

051827.cherokee: ᎤᏚᎸᎲᏃ ᎡᎧᏯ ᎤᏪᏅᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏟ ᎤᏃᏪᎳᏅᎩ ᏗᏂᏔᏲᏎᎸᎩ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ ᏗᎬᏩᏓᏂᎸᎢᏍᏗᏱ; ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏭᎷᏨ, ᎤᏣᏘ ᏚᏍᏕᎸᎲᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏣᏛ ᎢᏳᏩᏂᏌᏛ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏅᎯ.
051827.english: And when he was disposed to pass into Achaia, the brethren wrote, exhorting the disciples to receive him: who, when he was come, helped them much which had believed through grace:
051827.phonetic: U-du-lv-hv-no E-ka-ya u-we-nv-s-di-yi, a-na-da-nv-tli u-no-we-la-nv-gi di-ni-ta-yo-se-lv-gi a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi di-gv-wa-da-ni-lv-i-s-di-yi; na-s-gi-no wu-lu-tsv, u-tsa-ti du-s-de-lv-hv-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi u-da-do-li-tsa-dv i-yu-wa-ni-sa-dv u-no-hi-yu-nv-hi.

051828.cherokee: ᎤᎵᏂᎩᏛᏯᏰᏃ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎬ ᎬᏗᏍᎬ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏗᏱ ᏄᏩᏁᎸᎩ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏕᎦᎳᏫᎥᎢ, ᏗᎦᎸᏉᏗ ᎪᏪᎵ ᏕᎬᏗᏍᎬᎢ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᎬᏁᎲ ᏥᏌ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᎨᏒᎢ.
051828.english: For he mightily convinced the Jews, and that publickly, shewing by the scriptures that Jesus was Christ.
051828.phonetic: U-li-ni-gi-dv-ya-ye-no ga-wo-ni-s-gv gv-di-s-gv u-no-hi-yu-di-yi nu-wa-ne-lv-gi A-ni-tsu-si de-ga-la-wi-v-i, di-ga-lv-quo-di go-we-li de-gv-di-s-gv-i gv-ni-ge-sv ni-gv-ne-hv Tsi-sa na-s-gi Ga-lo-ne-dv ge-sv-i.

051901.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᎾᎯᏳ ᎠᏉᎳ ᎪᎵᏂᏗᏱ ᎡᏙᎲᎩ, ᏉᎳ ᎠᏰᎵ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎶᏒ, ᎡᏈᏌ ᏭᎷᏨᎩ.  ᏚᏩᏛᎲᏃ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ,
051901.english: And it came to pass, that, while Apollos was at Corinth, Paul having passed through the upper coasts came to Ephesus: and finding certain disciples,
051901.phonetic: Hi-a-no nu-li-s-ta-nv-gi, na-hi-yu A-quo-la go-li-ni-di-yi e-do-hv-gi, Quo-la a-ye-li ge-sv u-lo-sv, E-qui-sa wu-lu-tsv-gi.  Du-wa-dv-hv-no gi-lo i-yu-na-s-di a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi,

051902.cherokee: ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎡᏥᏁᎸᏍᎪ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎨᏦᎯᏳᏅᎯ?  ᎯᎠᏃ ᏅᎬᏩᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎥᏝ ᎣᎦᏛᎦᏅᎯᎤᏅ ᏱᎩ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎡᎲᎢ.
051902.english: He said unto them, Have ye received the Holy Ghost since ye believed? And they said unto him, We have not so much as heard whether there be any Holy Ghost.
051902.phonetic: Hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; E-tsi-ne-lv-s-go ga-lv-quo-di-yu a-da-nv-do ge-tso-hi-yu-nv-hi?  Hi-a-no nv-gv-wa-we-se-lv-gi; v-tla o-ga-dv-ga-nv-hi-u-nv yi-gi Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do e-hv-i.

051903.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏃ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎦᏙ ᎤᏍᏗ ᏗᏓᏬᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏕᏣᏬᏍᏔᏁᎢ?  ᏣᏂ ᏧᏓᏬᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒ, ᎤᎾᏛᏅᎩ.
051903.english: And he said unto them, Unto what then were ye baptized? And they said, Unto John's baptism.
051903.phonetic: Hi-a-no ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; Ga-do u-s-di di-da-wo-s-do-di ge-sv de-tsa-wo-s-ta-ne-i?  Tsa-ni tsu-da-wo-s-do-di ge-sv, u-na-dv-nv-gi.

051904.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᏣᏂ ᎤᏙᎯᏳᎯ ᏓᏓᏬᏍᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᏗᏓᏬᏍᏙᏗ ᎦᏁᏟᏴᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎣᏓᏅᏛ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ, ᏕᎧᏁᏤᎲ ᏴᏫ, ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏗᏱ ᎣᏂ ᏅᏓᏰᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ ᏥᏌ ᏕᎪᏎᎲᎩ.
051904.english: Then said Paul, John verily baptized with the baptism of repentance, saying unto the people, that they should believe on him which should come after him, that is, on Christ Jesus.
051904.phonetic: Quo-la-no hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; Tsa-ni u-do-hi-yu-hi da-da-wo-s-di-s-gv-gi di-da-wo-s-do-di ga-ne-tli-yv-s-di ge-sv o-da-nv-dv u-gv-wa-li, de-ka-ne-tse-hv yv-wi, u-no-hi-yu-di-yi o-ni nv-da-ye-gi, na-s-gi Ga-lo-ne-dv Tsi-sa de-go-se-hv-gi.

051905.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ ᏚᏙᎥ ᏕᎨᎦᏬᏍᏔᏅᎩ.
051905.english: When they heard this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
051905.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-na-dv-ga-nv U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa du-do-v de-ge-ga-wo-s-ta-nv-gi.

051906.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᏉᎳ ᏚᏏᏔᏛ, ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎤᏂᎷᏤᎸᎩ, ᏚᏂᏬᏂᏒᎩᏃ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᏗᎦᏬᏂᎯᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᏒᎩ.
051906.english: And when Paul had laid his hands upon them, the Holy Ghost came on them; and they spake with tongues, and prophesied.
051906.phonetic: Hna-quo-no Quo-la du-si-ta-dv, Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do u-ni-lu-tse-lv-gi, du-ni-wo-ni-sv-gi-no tsu-da-le-nv-dv di-ga-wo-ni-hi-s-di ge-sv-i, a-le u-na-do-le-ho-sv-gi.

051907.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏔᎳᏚ ᎢᏴᏛ ᎾᏂᎥᎩ.
051907.english: And all the men were about twelve.
051907.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no a-ni-s-ga-ya ni-ga-dv ta-la-du i-yv-dv na-ni-v-gi.

051908.cherokee: ᏗᎦᎳᏫᎢᏍᏗᏱᏃ ᏭᏴᎸ ᎾᏍᎦᎢᎲᎾ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎬᎩ, ᏦᎢ ᎢᏯᏅᏙ ᎢᎦᎯᏛ ᏕᎦᏬᏁᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏓᏍᏗᏰᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏗᏱ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ.
051908.english: And he went into the synagogue, and spake boldly for the space of three months, disputing and persuading the things concerning the kingdom of God.
051908.phonetic: Di-ga-la-wi-i-s-di-yi-no wu-yv-lv na-s-ga-i-hv-na ga-wo-ni-s-gv-gi, tso-i i-ya-nv-do i-ga-hi-dv de-ga-wo-ne-di-s-gv-gi a-le da-s-di-ye-di-s-gv-gi u-no-hi-yu-di-yi U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-go-hi u-gv-wa-li.

051909.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᎤᏂᏍᏓᏲᏒ ᎠᎴ ᏄᏃᎯᏳᏅᎾ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅ, ᎠᎴ ᎣᏐᏅᏉ ᎤᏂᏃᎮᎸ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏅᏅ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲᎢ, ᏚᏓᏅᎡᎸᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏓᏓᎴᏔᏅᎩ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ ᏕᎦᏬᏁᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᏂᏚᎩᏨᏂᏒ ᎩᎶ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏓᎳᎾ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ ᏧᏕᏲᏗᏱ.
051909.english: But when divers were hardened, and believed not, but spake evil of that way before the multitude, he departed from them, and separated the disciples, disputing daily in the school of one Tyrannus.
051909.phonetic: Gi-lo-no i-yu-na-s-di u-ni-s-da-yo-sv a-le nu-no-hi-yu-nv-na nu-li-s-ta-nv, a-le o-so-nv-quo u-ni-no-he-lv na-s-gi ga-nv-nv u-gv-wa-li u-ni-tsa-ti a-ni-ga-ta-hv-i, du-da-nv-e-lv-gi, a-le du-da-da-le-ta-nv-gi a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi de-ga-wo-ne-di-s-gv-gi ni-du-gi-tsv-ni-sv gi-lo i-yu-s-di Da-la-na tsu-do-i-dv tsu-de-yo-di-yi.

051910.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏛᏁᎸᎩ ᏔᎵ ᏧᏕᏘᏴᏛ ᎢᎪᎯᏛ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏅᏓᏳᎵᏍᏙᏔᏅᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎡᏏᏱ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᎪᎢ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅᎩ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ.
051910.english: And this continued by the space of two years; so that all they which dwelt in Asia heard the word of the Lord Jesus, both Jews and Greeks.
051910.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no hi-a nu-dv-ne-lv-gi ta-li tsu-de-ti-yv-dv i-go-hi-dv, na-s-gi nv-da-yu-li-s-do-ta-nv-gi ni-ga-dv E-si-yi a-ne-hi A-ni-tsu-si a-le A-ni-go-i u-na-dv-ga-nv-gi ka-no-he-dv U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa u-tse-li-ga.

051911.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏯᏃ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᏚᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲᎩ ᏉᎳ ᎠᎬᏗᏍᎬᎩ;
051911.english: And God wrought special miracles by the hands of Paul:
051911.phonetic: A-le U-ne-la-nv-hi ya-no i-yu-li-s-do-di ni-ge-sv-na u-s-qua-ni-go-di du-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv-gi Quo-la a-gv-di-s-gv-gi;

051912.cherokee: ᎠᏰᎸᏰᏃ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᎯ ᏗᏯᏠ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏞᏌᏙ ᏓᏂᏃᎮᎲᎩ ᏧᏂᏢᎩ, ᎥᏳᎩᏃ ᎠᎵᏛᏗᏍᎬᎩ, ᎤᏂᏲᏃ ᏗᏓᏅᏙ ᎬᏩᏂᏄᎪᎬᎩ.
051912.english: So that from his body were brought unto the sick handkerchiefs or aprons, and the diseases departed from them, and the evil spirits went out of them.
051912.phonetic: A-ye-lv-ye-no tsu-da-le-nv-hi di-ya-tlo a-le di-tle-sa-do da-ni-no-he-hv-gi tsu-ni-tlv-gi, v-yu-gi-no a-li-dv-di-s-gv-gi, u-ni-yo-no di-da-nv-do gv-wa-ni-nu-go-gv-gi.

051913.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏅᏁᏙᎯ, ᎠᏂᏍᎩᎾ ᏗᏂᏄᎪᏫᏍᎩ, ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏖᎴ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ ᏕᎤᏙᎥ ᏧᏂᏁᎢᏍᏓᏁᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏲ ᏗᏓᏅᏙ ᏧᏂᏯᎢ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏎᎢ; ᎢᏨᏁᎢᏍᏓᏏ ᏥᏌ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏉᎳ ᎠᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎬ ᏥᎦᏃᎮᎭ.
051913.english: Then certain of the vagabond Jews, exorcists, took upon them to call over them which had evil spirits the name of the Lord Jesus, saying, We adjure you by Jesus whom Paul preacheth.
051913.phonetic: Gi-lo-no i-yu-na-s-di A-ni-tsu-si nv-ne-do-hi, a-ni-s-gi-na di-ni-nu-go-wi-s-gi, u-na-da-nv-te-le U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa de-u-do-v tsu-ni-ne-i-s-da-ne-di-yi u-ni-yo di-da-nv-do tsu-ni-ya-i, hi-a nu-ni-we-se-i; I-tsv-ne-i-s-da-si Tsi-sa, na-s-gi Quo-la a-li-tsi-do-hv-s-gv tsi-ga-no-he-ha.

051914.cherokee: ᎠᏁᎮᏃ ᎦᎵᏉᎩ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᏏᏫ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ ᏧᏪᏥ ᎠᏧᏏ ᏄᎬᏫᏳᎡ ᎠᏥᎸᎨᎶᎯ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎾᎾᏛᏁᎮᎢ.
051914.english: And there were seven sons of one Sceva, a Jew, and chief of the priests, which did so.
051914.phonetic: A-ne-he-no ga-li-quo-gi i-ya-ni-dv Si-wi tsu-do-i-dv tsu-we-tsi A-tsu-si nu-gv-wi-yu-e a-tsi-lv-ge-lo-hi na-s-gi na-na-dv-ne-he-i.

051915.cherokee: ᎤᏲᏃ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏎᎢ; ᏥᏌ ᏥᎦᏔᎭ, ᎠᎴ ᏉᎳ ᏥᎦᏔᎭ, ᏂᎯᏍᎩᏂ ᎦᎪ ᎢᏣᏍᏗ?
051915.english: And the evil spirit answered and said, Jesus I know, and Paul I know; but who are ye?
051915.phonetic: U-yo-no a-da-nv-do u-ne-tsv hi-a nu-we-se-i; Tsi-sa tsi-ga-ta-ha, a-le Quo-la tsi-ga-ta-ha, ni-hi-s-gi-ni ga-go i-tsa-s-di?

051916.cherokee: ᎠᏍᎦᏯᏃ ᎤᏲ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎤᏯᎢ ᏚᏁᎷᎩᎡᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎡᏍᎦ ᏂᏚᏩᏁᎴᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᏎᎪᎩᏎᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎤᎾᎵᏎ ᎾᎿᎭᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᎤᏂᏄᎪᏤ ᏧᏂᏰᎸᎢᎭ ᎠᎴ ᏗᎨᏥᏐᏅᏅᎯ.
051916.english: And the man in whom the evil spirit was leaped on them, and overcame them, and prevailed against them, so that they fled out of that house naked and wounded.
051916.phonetic: A-s-ga-ya-no u-yo a-da-nv-do u-ya-i du-ne-lu-gi-e-le-i, a-le e-s-ga ni-du-wa-ne-le-i, a-le du-se-go-gi-se-i, na-s-gi i-yu-s-di u-na-li-se na-hna a-da-ne-lv u-ni-nu-go-tse tsu-ni-ye-lv-i-ha a-le di-ge-tsi-so-nv-nv-hi.

051917.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᎪᎢ ᎡᏈᏌ ᎠᏁᎯ; ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᎤᏂᎾᏰᏒᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ ᏚᏙᎥ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎩ.
051917.english: And this was known to all the Jews and Greeks also dwelling at Ephesus; and fear fell on them all, and the name of the Lord Jesus was magnified.
051917.phonetic: A-le na-s-gi hi-a u-na-dv-ga-nv-gi ni-ga-dv A-ni-tsu-si a-le A-ni-go-i E-qui-sa a-ne-hi; ni-ga-dv-no u-ni-na-ye-sv-gi, a-le U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa du-do-v ga-lv-quo-di-yu nu-li-s-ta-nv-gi.

051918.cherokee: ᎤᏂᏣᏛᎩᏃ ᎤᏃᎯᏳᏅᎯ ᎤᏂᎷᏨᎩ, ᎠᏂᏃᎲᏍᎬ ᎠᎴ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎾᏅᏁᎲ ᏚᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎸᎢ.
051918.english: And many that believed came, and confessed, and shewed their deeds.
051918.phonetic: U-ni-tsa-dv-gi-no u-no-hi-yu-nv-hi u-ni-lu-tsv-gi, a-ni-no-hv-s-gv a-le gv-ni-ge-sv na-nv-ne-hv-du-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-lv-i.

051919.cherokee: ᎾᏍᏉ ᎤᏂᏣᏛᎩ ᎤᏍᏆᏂᎪᏗ ᎠᎾᏛᏁᎵᏍᎩ ᏚᏂᏟᏌᏅᎩ ᎪᏪᎵ, ᎠᎴ ᏚᎾᎪᎲᏍᏔᏅᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲᎢ.  ᎤᎾᏎᎸᏃ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗᎯ ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᏒᎩ ᎯᏍᎦᏍᎪᎯ ᎢᏯᎦᏴᎵ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏂᏁᎬ ᎠᏕᎸ.
051919.english: Many of them also which used curious arts brought their books together, and burned them before all men: and they counted the price of them, and found it fifty thousand pieces of silver.
051919.phonetic: Na-s-quo u-ni-tsa-dv-gi u-s-qua-ni-go-di a-na-dv-ne-li-s-gi du-ni-tli-sa-nv-gi go-we-li, a-le du-na-go-hv-s-ta-nv-gi ni-ga-dv a-ni-ga-ta-hv-i.  U-na-se-lv-no tsu-gv-wa-lo-di-hi u-na-do-le-ho-sv-gi hi-s-ga-s-go-hi i-ya-ga-yv-li ge-sv u-ni-ne-gv a-de-lv.

051920.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏧᎵᏂᎬᎬᎩ ᎤᏛᏃᏒᎩ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏰᎵᏒᎩ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ.
051920.english: So mightily grew the word of God and prevailed.
051920.phonetic: Na-s-gi tsu-li-ni-gv-gv-gi u-dv-no-sv-gi a-le u-ye-li-sv-gi ka-no-he-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-ga.

051921.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏂᏙᎸ, ᏉᎳ ᎤᏓᏅᏖᎸᎩ ᎤᏓᏅᏛᎢ, ᎿᎭᏉ ᎹᏏᏙᏂ ᎠᎴ ᎡᎧᏯ ᎤᎶᏐᏅᎯ, ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏭᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎾᎿᎭᎬᏇᏙᏅᎯ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᎶᎻ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏎ ᏛᏥᎪᎢ.
051921.english: After these things were ended, Paul purposed in the spirit, when he had passed through Macedonia and Achaia, to go to Jerusalem, saying, After I have been there, I must also see Rome.
051921.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no hi-a nu-li-s-ta-ni-do-lv, Quo-la u-da-nv-te-lv-gi u-da-nv-dv-i, hna-quo Ma-si-do-ni a-le E-ka-ya u-lo-so-nv-hi, Tsi-lu-si-li-mi wu-lo-hi-s-di-yi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; na-hna gv-que-do-nv-hi ge-se-s-di Lo-mi na-s-quo a-se dv-tsi-go-i.

051922.cherokee: ᎰᏩᏃ ᎹᏏᏙᏂ ᏕᎤᏅᏒᎩ ᎠᏂᏔᎵ ᎬᏩᏍᏕᎸᎯᏙᎯ, ᏗᎹᏗ ᎠᎴ ᎢᎳᏍᏓ, ᎤᏩᏒᏍᎩᏂ ᎤᏗᎩᏴᎩ ᎡᏏᏱ ᎢᎸᏍᎩ ᎢᎪᎯᏛ.
051922.english: So he sent into Macedonia two of them that ministered unto him, Timotheus and Erastus; but he himself stayed in Asia for a season.
051922.phonetic: Ho-wa-no Ma-si-do-ni de-u-nv-sv-gi a-ni-ta-li gv-wa-s-de-lv-hi-do-hi, Di-ma-di a-le I-la-s-da, u-wa-sv-s-gi-ni u-di-gi-yv-gi E-si-yi i-lv-s-gi i-go-hi-dv.

051923.cherokee: ᎾᎯᏳᏃ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏍᏗᏉ ᎤᏕᏯᏙᏗ ᏱᏄᎵᏍᏔᏁᎢ, ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᏅᏅ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ.
051923.english: And the same time there arose no small stir about that way.
051923.phonetic: Na-hi-yu-no v-tla u-s-di-quo u-de-ya-do-di yi-nu-li-s-ta-ne-i, nv-da-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv-gi na-s-gi ga-nv-nv u-gv-wa-li.

051924.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏰᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏗᎻᏟᏯ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ, ᎠᏕᎸ ᎠᏢᏅᎥᏍᎩ, ᎠᏕᎸ ᏗᎪᏢᏔᏅᎯ ᏓᏓᏁᎸ ᏕᎪᏢᎾᏁᎲ ᏓᏰᏂ, ᎥᏝ ᎤᏍᏗᏉ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᏱᏓᏩᏛᎡᎮ ᎠᏃᏢᏅᎥᏍᎩ.
051924.english: For a certain man named Demetrius, a silversmith, which made silver shrines for Diana, brought no small gain unto the craftsmen;
051924.phonetic: Gi-lo-ye-no i-yu-s-di Di-mi-tli-ya tsu-do-i-dv, a-de-lv a-tlv-nv-v-s-gi, a-de-lv di-go-tlv-ta-nv-hi da-da-ne-lv de-go-tlv-na-ne-hv Da-ye-ni, v-tla u-s-di-quo tsu-gv-wa-lo-di yi-da-wa-dv-e-he a-no-tlv-nv-v-s-gi.

051925.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᏚᏪᏟᏌᏅ ᎠᎴ ᏅᏩᎾᏓᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᏧᏂᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎯ ᏚᏪᏟᏌᏅ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏯ, ᎢᏥᎦᏔᎭ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᏧᎬᏩᎶᏗ ᎢᏗᏩᏛᏗᏍᎬᎢ.
051925.english: Whom he called together with the workmen of like occupation, and said, Sirs, ye know that by this craft we have our wealth.
051925.phonetic: Na-s-gi du-we-tli-sa-nv a-le nv-wa-na-da-le na-s-gi i-yu-s-di tsu-ni-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hi du-we-tli-sa-nv hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; I-tsi-s-ga-ya, i-tsi-ga-ta-ha hi-a na-s-gi di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv tsu-gv-wa-lo-di i-di-wa-dv-di-s-gv-i.

051926.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎢᏥᎪᏩᏘᎭ ᎠᎴ ᎢᏣᏛᎩᎭ, ᎥᏝ ᎡᏈᏌᏉ ᎤᏩᏒ, ᎾᏍᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᎴᏉ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᎡᏏᏱ, ᎯᎠ ᏉᎳ ᏓᏍᏗᏰᏗᏍᎬ ᏚᎦᏔᎲᏒ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᏴᏫ, ᎯᎠ ᏂᏕᎦᏪᏎᎲᎢ, ᎥᏝ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎠᏁᎯ ᏱᎩ ᎩᎶ ᏧᏬᏰᏂᏉ ᏧᏬᏢᏔᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ.
051926.english: Moreover ye see and hear, that not alone at Ephesus, but almost throughout all Asia, this Paul hath persuaded and turned away much people, saying that they be no gods, which are made with hands:
051926.phonetic: A-le i-tsi-go-wa-ti-ha a-le i-tsa-dv-gi-ha, v-tla E-qui-sa-quo u-wa-sv, na-s-quo-s-gi-ni a-le-quo ni-gv-na-dv E-si-yi, hi-a Quo-la da-s-di-ye-di-s-gv du-ga-ta-hv-sv u-ni-tsa-ti yv-wi, hi-a ni-de-ga-we-se-hv-i, v-tla ga-lv-la-di a-ne-hi yi-gi gi-lo tsu-wo-ye-ni-quo tsu-wo-tlv-ta-nv-hi tsi-gi.

051927.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᎯᎠᏉ ᏗᎩᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎤᏁᎳᎩ ᏫᎬᏩᏲᏥᏓᏍᏗ ᏱᎩ; ᎾᏍᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᏓᏁᎸ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᏓᏰᏂ ᎤᏤᎵᎦ ᎠᏎᏉᏉ ᎢᎬᏩᎵᏍᏔᏂᏓᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏥᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎨᏒ ᏫᎬᏩᏲᏥᏓᏍᏗ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎡᏏᏱ ᎠᎴ ᏂᎬᎾᏛ ᎡᎶᎯ ᏥᎬᏩᎸᏉᏗᎭ.
051927.english: So that not only this our craft is in danger to be set at nought; but also that the temple of the great goddess Diana should be despised, and her magnificence should be destroyed, whom all Asia and the world worshippeth.
051927.phonetic: A-le v-tla hi-a-quo di-gi-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv u-wa-sv u-ne-la-gi wi-gv-wa-yo-tsi-da-s-di yi-gi; na-s-quo-s-gi-ni a-da-ne-lv ga-lv-quo-di-yu ga-lv-la-di e-hi Da-ye-ni u-tse-li-ga a-se-quo-quo i-gv-wa-li-s-ta-ni-da-s-di, a-le na-s-gi a-tsi-lv-quo-di-yu ge-sv wi-gv-wa-yo-tsi-da-s-di, na-s-gi ni-ga-dv E-si-yi a-le ni-gv-na-dv e-lo-hi tsi-gv-wa-lv-quo-di-ha.

051928.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎤᎾᏛᎦᏅ ᎤᏂᏔᎳᏬᏍᎬᎢ ᎤᏂᎧᎵᏨᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏁᎷᏅᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᏓᏰᏂ ᎡᏈᏌ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ.
051928.english: And when they heard these sayings, they were full of wrath, and cried out, saying, Great is Diana of the Ephesians.
051928.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no u-na-dv-ga-nv u-ni-ta-la-wo-s-gv-i u-ni-ka-li-tsv-gi, a-le u-ne-lu-nv-gi hi-a nu-ni-we-sv-gi; Ga-lv-quo-di-yu Da-ye-ni E-qui-sa a-ne-hi u-na-tse-li-ga.

051929.cherokee: ᏂᎬᏃ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎤᎾᏓᏑᏰᏛᏉ ᏄᎵᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᏚᏂᏂᏴᎲᏃ ᎦᏯ ᎠᎴ ᎡᎵᏍᏔᎦ ᎹᏏᏙᏂ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᏉᎳ ᎤᎾᎵᎪᎯ ᎠᏁᏙᎲᎢ, ᎤᎴᏃᏅᎯ ᎤᎾᏁᎷᎩᏒᎩ ᏭᏂᏴᎸᎩ ᎤᎾᏛᏁᎸᏗᏱ.
051929.english: And the whole city was filled with confusion: and having caught Gaius and Aristarchus, men of Macedonia, Paul's companions in travel, they rushed with one accord into the theatre.
051929.phonetic: Ni-gv-no ga-du-hv u-na-da-su-ye-dv-quo nu-li-s-ta-nv-gi, du-ni-ni-yv-hv-no Ga-ya a-le E-li-s-ta-ga Ma-si-do-ni a-ne-hi, Quo-la u-na-li-go-hi a-ne-do-hv-i, u-le-no-nv-hi u-na-ne-lu-gi-sv-gi wu-ni-yv-lv-gi u-na-dv-ne-lv-di-yi.

051930.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᏫᏚᏴᏎᏗᏱ ᏴᏫ ᎤᏓᏅᏖᎸ, ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᏅᏍᏓᏕᎸᎩ.
051930.english: And when Paul would have entered in unto the people, the disciples suffered him not.
051930.phonetic: Quo-la-no wi-du-yv-se-di-yi yv-wi u-da-nv-te-lv, a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi gv-wa-nv-s-da-de-lv-gi.

051931.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᏳᎾᏍᏗ ᏗᎨᎦᏁᎶᏗ ᎡᏏᏱ ᎠᏁᎯ, ᏉᎳ ᏧᎵᎢ, ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏒᎩ ᏫᎬᏩᏔᏲᏎᎸᎩ, ᏭᏴᏍᏗᏱ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎤᎾᏛᏁᎸᏗᏱ.
051931.english: And certain of the chief of Asia, which were his friends, sent unto him, desiring him that he would not adventure himself into the theatre.
051931.phonetic: Gi-lo-no i-yu-na-s-di di-ge-ga-ne-lo-di E-si-yi a-ne-hi, Quo-la tsu-li-i, u-na-da-nv-sv-gi wi-gv-wa-ta-yo-se-lv-gi, wu-yv-s-di-yi ni-ge-sv-na u-na-dv-ne-lv-di-yi.

051932.cherokee: ᎤᏁᎷᏅᎯ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛᏉ ᏂᏚᏂᏪᏒᎩ; ᏓᏂᎳᏫᎥᏰᏃ ᎤᎾᏓᏑᏰᏛᏉ ᎨᏒᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏟ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᎥᏝ ᏯᏂᎦᏔᎮ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᎤᎾᏓᏟᏌᏅᎢ.
051932.english: Some therefore cried one thing, and some another: for the assembly was confused; and the more part knew not wherefore they were come together.
051932.phonetic: U-ne-lu-nv-hi tsu-da-le-nv-dv-quo ni-du-ni-we-sv-gi; da-ni-la-wi-v-ye-no u-na-da-su-ye-dv-quo ge-sv-gi, a-le u-tli i-ya-ni-dv v-tla ya-ni-ga-ta-he nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv u-na-da-tli-sa-nv-i.

051933.cherokee: ᏭᏃᏯᏁᏒᎩᏃ ᎡᎵᎩ ᎤᏂᏣᏘ ᎠᏂᏙᎾᎥᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎠᏂᏅᏫᏍᏗᏍᎬᎩ; ᎡᎵᎩᏃ ᎤᏕᎸᏅ ᎤᏬᏰᏂ ᎤᏚᎵᏍᎬᎩ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎠᎵᏍᏕᎵᏍᎬᎢ ᏧᏬᏁᏙᏗᏱ ᏴᏫ.
051933.english: And they drew Alexander out of the multitude, the Jews putting him forward. And Alexander beckoned with the hand, and would have made his defence unto the people.
051933.phonetic: Wu-no-ya-ne-sv-gi-no E-li-gi u-ni-tsa-ti a-ni-do-na-v-i, na-s-gi A-ni-tsu-si a-ni-nv-wi-s-di-s-gv-gi; E-li-gi-no u-de-lv-nv u-wo-ye-ni u-du-li-s-gv-gi u-wa-sv a-li-s-de-li-s-gv-i tsu-wo-ne-do-di-yi yv-wi.

051934.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎤᎾᏙᎴᎰᏒ ᎠᏧᏏ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᏌᏉ ᏥᎧᏁᎪ ᎾᏍᎩᏯ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᏁᎷᏅᎩ, ᏔᎵᎭ ᎢᏳᏟᎶᏛ ᎢᎪᎯᏛ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎬᎩ; ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᏓᏰᏂ ᎡᏈᏌ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎦ.
051934.english: But when they knew that he was a Jew, all with one voice about the space of two hours cried out, Great is Diana of the Ephesians.
051934.phonetic: A-se-no u-na-do-le-ho-sv A-tsu-si ge-sv-i, sa-quo tsi-ka-ne-go na-s-gi-ya ni-ga-dv u-ne-lu-nv-gi, ta-li-ha i-yu-tli-lo-dv i-go-hi-dv hi-a na-ni-we-s-gv-gi; Ga-lv-quo-di-yu Da-ye-ni E-qui-sa a-ne-hi u-na-tse-li-ga.

051935.cherokee: ᏗᎪᏪᎵᏍᎩᏃ ᏚᏲᏍᏙᏓᏁᎸ ᏴᏫ, ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᎢᏥᏍᎦᏯ ᎡᏈᏌ ᎢᏤᎯ, ᎦᎪ ᎡᎭ ᎾᎦᏔᎲᎾ ᎦᏚᎲ ᎡᏈᏌ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎡᏓᏓᏙᎵᏍᏓᏁᎯ ᎨᏒ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᏓᏰᏂ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᏉᏙ ᏅᏓᏳᎶᎣᏒᎯ ᏗᏟᎶᏍᏔᏅᎯ?
051935.english: And when the townclerk had appeased the people, he said, Ye men of Ephesus, what man is there that knoweth not how that the city of the Ephesians is a worshipper of the great goddess Diana, and of the image which fell down from Jupiter?
051935.phonetic: Di-go-we-li-s-gi-no du-yo-s-do-da-ne-lv yv-wi, hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; I-tsi-s-ga-ya E-qui-sa i-tse-hi, ga-go e-ha na-ga-ta-hv-na ga-du-hv E-qui-sa na-s-gi e-da-da-do-li-s-da-ne-hi ge-sv ga-lv-quo-di-yu ga-lv-la-di e-hi Da-ye-ni, a-le Tsu-quo-do nv-da-yu-lo-o-sv-hi di-tli-lo-s-ta-nv-hi?

051936.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏜᏓᏏᏛᎡᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᏥᎩ, ᏙᎯᏱᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᏱᏂᏣᏛᏅ, ᎠᎴ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎬᏁᎳᎩ ᏂᏣᏛᏁᎸᎾ ᏱᎩ.
051936.english: Seeing then that these things cannot be spoken against, ye ought to be quiet, and to do nothing rashly.
051936.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no i-yu-s-di hi-a nu-dla-si-dv-e-di ni-ge-sv-na tsi-gi, do-hi-yi-quo-s-gi-ni yi-ni-tsa-dv-nv, a-le go-hu-s-di gv-ne-la-gi ni-tsa-dv-ne-lv-na yi-gi.

051937.cherokee: ᏕᏣᏘᏃᎦᏰᏃ ᎯᎠ Ꮎ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎸᏉᏗ ᏕᎨᏒ ᎠᏂᏃᏍᎩᏍᎩ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏣᏤᎵ ᎦᎸᎳᏗ ᎡᎯ ᎠᏂᏐᏢᎢᏍᏗᏍᎩ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ.
051937.english: For ye have brought hither these men, which are neither robbers of churches, nor yet blasphemers of your goddess.
051937.phonetic: De-tsa-ti-no-ga-ye-no hi-a na a-ni-s-ga-ya, na-s-gi ga-lv-quo-di de-ge-sv a-ni-no-s-gi-s-gi ni-ge-sv-na, a-le i-tsa-tse-li ga-lv-la-di e-hi a-ni-so-tlv-i-s-di-s-gi ni-ge-sv-na.

051938.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᏳᏃ ᏗᎻᏟᏯ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏣᏁᎭ ᎠᏃᏢᏅᎥᏍᎩ ᎠᏄᎯᏍᏗᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎩᎶᎢ, ᎤᏜᏓᏅᏛ ᏗᏓᏱᎵᏓᏍᏗᏱ, ᎠᎴ ᎨᏥᎦᏘᏗᏍᏗ ᎠᏁᎭ; ᏫᏓᎾᏓᏱᎵᏓ.
051938.english: Wherefore if Demetrius, and the craftsmen which are with him, have a matter against any man, the law is open, and there are deputies: let them implead one another.
051938.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di i-yu-no Di-mi-tli-ya a-le na-s-gi tsa-ne-ha a-no-tlv-nv-v-s-gi a-nu-hi-s-di-s-ge-s-di gi-lo-i, u-dla-nv-dv di-da-yi-li-da-s-di-yi, a-le ge-tsi-ga-ti-di-s-di a-ne-ha; wi-da-na-da-yi-li-da.

051939.cherokee: ᎢᏳᏍᎩᏂ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏅᏩᏓᎴ ᎢᏥᏱᎵᏙᎮᏍᏗ, ᎤᎵᏁᏨᎯ ᏕᎦᎳᏫᎥ ᏓᏚᎪᏔᏅᎭ.
051939.english: But if ye inquire any thing concerning other matters, it shall be determined in a lawful assembly.
051939.phonetic: I-yu-s-gi-ni go-hu-s-di nv-wa-da-le i-tsi-yi-li-do-he-s-di, u-li-ne-tsv-hi de-ga-la-wi-v da-du-go-ta-nv-ha.

051940.cherokee: ᎨᎦᎵᏱᎵᏕᏗᏰᏃ ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ ᎤᏓᏑᏰᏛ ᏥᏓᎵᏖᎸᎲᎦ, ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏯᎭ ᎨᎩᏃᎮᏗ ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᎯᎠ ᎪᎯ ᏥᏓᏓᎶᏌ.
051940.english: For we are in danger to be called in question for this day's uproar, there being no cause whereby we may give an account of this concourse.
051940.phonetic: Ge-ga-li-yi-li-de-di-ye-no go-hi i-ga u-da-su-ye-dv tsi-da-li-te-lv-hv-ga, v-tla-ye-no go-hu-s-di ya-ha ge-gi-no-he-di nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv hi-a go-hi tsi-da-da-lo-sa.

051941.english: And when he had thus spoken, he dismissed the assembly.

052001.cherokee: ᎤᏓᏑᏰᏛᏃ ᎠᎾᎵᏖᎸᎲᏍᎬ ᎤᎵᏙᎯᏍᏔᏅ, ᏉᎳ ᏫᏚᏯᏅᎲᎩ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ, ᏚᏲᎵᎸᏃ, ᎤᏂᎩᏒᎩ ᏑᏏᏙᏂ ᎤᏪᏅᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏰᎸᏅᎩ.
052001.english: And after the uproar was ceased, Paul called unto him the disciples, and embraced them, and departed for to go into Macedonia.
052001.phonetic: U-da-su-ye-dv-no a-na-li-te-lv-hv-s-gv u-li-do-hi-s-ta-nv, Quo-la wi-du-ya-nv-hv-gi a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi, du-yo-li-lv-no, u-ni-gi-sv-gi Su-si-do-ni u-we-nv-s-di-yi u-ye-lv-nv-gi.

052002.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎤᏪᏙᏅ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏣᏘ ᏚᏪᏁᏔᏅ, ᎠᏂᎪᎢ ᎤᎾᏤᎵᎪᎯ ᏭᎷᏨᎩ.
052002.english: And when he had gone over those parts, and had given them much exhortation, he came into Greece,
052002.phonetic: Na-hna-no u-we-do-nv a-le u-tsa-ti du-we-ne-ta-nv, A-ni-go-i u-na-tse-li-go-hi wu-lu-tsv-gi.

052003.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᏦᎢ ᎢᏯᏅᏙ ᎤᏪᏙᎸ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᏧᏄᎪᏔᏅ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎬᏩᏁᏗᏱ, ᏏᎵᏱ ᏓᏰᏏᏒᎩ ᏥᏳᎯ ᏛᏣᏂᏒᎩ, ᎤᏓᏅᏖᎸᎩ ᏛᎤᏨᏍᏗᏱ ᎼᏏᏙᏂ ᏭᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ.
052003.english: And there abode three months. And when the Jews laid wait for him, as he was about to sail into Syria, he purposed to return through Macedonia.
052003.phonetic: Na-hna-no tso-i i-ya-nv-do u-we-do-lv, a-le A-ni-tsu-si tsu-nu-go-ta-nv go-hu-s-di gv-wa-ne-di-yi, Si-li-yi da-ye-si-sv-gi tsi-yu-hi dv-tsa-ni-sv-gi, u-da-nv-te-lv-gi dv-u-tsv-s-di-yi Mo-si-do-ni wu-lo-hi-s-di-yi.

052004.cherokee: ᎡᏏᏱᏃ ᎬᏗᏍᎩ ᎬᏩᏍᏓᏩᏛᏒᎩ ᏐᏇᏓ ᏈᎵᏱ ᎡᎯ, ᏕᏏᎶᏂᎦᏃ ᎠᏁᎯ ᎡᎵᏍᏓᎦ ᎠᎴ ᏏᎬᏓ, ᎦᏯᏃ ᏓᏈ ᎡᎯ, ᏗᎹᏗᏃ, ᎡᏏᏱᏃ ᎠᏁᎯ ᏗᎩᎦ ᎠᎴ ᏠᏆᎹ.
052004.english: And there accompanied him into Asia Sopater of Berea; and of the Thessalonians, Aristarchus and Secundus; and Gaius of Derbe, and Timotheus; and of Asia, Tychicus and Trophimus.
052004.phonetic: E-si-yi-no gv-di-s-gi gv-wa-s-da-wa-dv-sv-gi So-que-da Qui-li-yi e-hi, De-si-lo-ni-ga-no a-ne-hi E-li-s-da-ga a-le Si-gv-da, Ga-ya-no Da-qui e-hi, Di-ma-di-no, E-si-yi-no a-ne-hi di-gi-ga a-le Tlo-qua-ma.

052005.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᎢᎬᏱ ᏄᏁᏅᏒᎩ ᏠᎠᏏ ᏓᎪᎩᏚᏘᏴᎩ.
052005.english: These going before tarried for us at Troas.
052005.phonetic: Na-s-gi hi-a i-gv-yi nu-ne-nv-sv-gi Tlo-a-si da-go-gi-du-ti-yv-gi.

052006.cherokee: ᎾᎪᏔᏅᎾᏃ ᎦᏚ ᎠᎩᏍᏗᏱ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎶᏐᏅ, ᏈᎵᎩᏱ ᏥᏳᎯ ᎣᎦᏣᏅᎩ, ᎯᏍᎩᏃ ᏫᏄᏒᎸ ᏠᎠᏏ ᏫᏙᏥᏩᏛᎲᎩ; ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᎦᎵᏉᎩ ᏧᏒᎯᏛ ᎣᎨᏙᎸᎩ.
052006.english: And we sailed away from Philippi after the days of unleavened bread, and came unto them to Troas in five days; where we abode seven days.
052006.phonetic: Na-go-ta-nv-na-no ga-du a-gi-s-di-yi ge-sv u-lo-so-nv, Qui-li-gi-yi tsi-yu-hi o-ga-tsa-nv-gi, hi-s-gi-no wi-nu-sv-lv Tlo-a-si wi-do-tsi-wa-dv-hv-gi; na-hna-no ga-li-quo-gi tsu-sv-hi-dv o-ge-do-lv-gi.

052007.cherokee: ᎢᎬᏱᏱᏃ ᎢᎦ ᏑᎾᏙᏓᏆᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ ᎦᏚ ᎠᎬᎭᎷᏯᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᎬᏩᎵ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ ᎤᎾᏓᏟᏌᏅ, ᏉᎳ ᏚᎵᏥᏙᏁᎸᎩ, ᎤᎩᏨᏛᏉ ᎤᏪᏅᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ; ᎠᎴ ᎡᏃᏱ ᎠᏰᎵ ᏫᎬᏩᏛᏅᎩ ᎦᏬᏂᏍᎬᎩ.
052007.english: And upon the first day of the week, when the disciples came together to break bread, Paul preached unto them, ready to depart on the morrow; and continued his speech until midnight.
052007.phonetic: I-gv-yi-yi-no i-ga su-na-do-da-qua-s-di ge-sv-i ga-du a-gv-ha-lu-ya-s-di-yi u-gv-wa-li a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi u-na-da-tli-sa-nv, Quo-la du-li-tsi-do-ne-lv-gi, u-gi-tsv-dv-quo u-we-nv-s-di ge-sv-i; a-le e-no-yi a-ye-li wi-gv-wa-dv-nv-gi ga-wo-ni-s-gv-gi.

052008.english: And there were many lights in the upper chamber, where they were gathered together.
052008.phonetic: A-le u-tsa-dv-gi da-tsv-s-dv-gi ga-lv-la-di na-hna da-ni-la-wi-v-i.

052009.cherokee: ᎩᎶᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎠᏫᏄᏣ ᏳᏘᎦ ᏧᏙᎢᏛ ᎠᏦᎳᏅ ᎤᏬᎸᎩ, ᎠᏍᏓᏯ ᎦᎵᎲᎩ; ᏉᎳᏃ ᎪᎯᏗᏳ ᎠᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎬ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ, ᎦᎵᎲ ᎤᏪᏇᏴᏒᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏦᎢᏁ ᏗᏲᏓᏝᎲ ᏓᏳᏙᎣᏒᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᏥᏁᏒᎩ ᎤᏲᎱᏒᎯ ᎨᏒᎩ.
052009.english: And there sat in a window a certain young man named Eutychus, being fallen into a deep sleep: and as Paul was long preaching, he sunk down with sleep, and fell down from the third loft, and was taken up dead.
052009.phonetic: Gi-lo-no i-yu-s-di a-wi-nu-tsa Yu-ti-ga tsu-do-i-dv a-tso-la-nv u-wo-lv-gi, a-s-da-ya ga-li-hv-gi; Quo-la-no go-hi-di-yu a-li-tsi-do-hv-s-gv i-yu-s-di, ga-li-hv u-we-que-yv-sv-gi a-le tso-i-ne di-yo-da-tla-hv da-yu-do-o-sv-gi, a-le a-tsi-ne-sv-gi u-yo-hu-sv-hi ge-sv-gi.

052010.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏃ ᎤᏠᎣᏒ ᎤᎵᏢᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᏄᎶᎸ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒᎩ; ᏞᏍᏗ ᏱᏣᏕᏯᏔᏁᎴᏍᏗ, ᎬᏅᏰᏃ ᎠᏏᏉ ᎠᏯᎠ.
052010.english: And Paul went down, and fell on him, and embracing him said, Trouble not yourselves; for his life is in him.
052010.phonetic: Quo-la-no u-tlo-o-sv u-li-tlv-nv-gi, a-le u-nu-lo-lv hi-a nu-we-sv-gi; tle-s-di yi-tsa-de-ya-ta-ne-le-s-di, gv-nv-ye-no a-si-quo a-ya-a.

052011.cherokee: ᏔᎵᏁᏃ ᎢᎤᎩᎳᏫᏒ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎬᎭᎷᏴ ᎦᏚ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎬ, ᎠᎴ ᎪᎯᏛ ᎤᏬᏂᏒ, ᎤᎩᏨᏕᏱ ᎬᏗᏍᎩ, ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᏂᎩᏒᎩ.
052011.english: When he therefore was come up again, and had broken bread, and eaten, and talked a long while, even till break of day, so he departed.
052011.phonetic: Ta-li-ne-no i-u-gi-la-wi-sv, a-le u-gv-ha-lu-yv ga-du, a-le u-gv, a-le go-hi-dv u-wo-ni-sv, u-gi-tsv-de-yi gv-di-s-gi, hna-quo u-ni-gi-sv-gi.

052012.cherokee: ᎤᎾᏘᎿᎭᏫᏛᎲᎩᏃ ᎠᏫᏄᏣ ᎬᏃᏛ, ᎠᎴ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏍᏗᏉ ᎣᏍᏛ ᏳᎾᏓᏅᏓᏕᎢ.
052012.english: And they brought the young man alive, and were not a little comforted.
052012.phonetic: U-na-ti-hna-wi-dv-hv-gi-no a-wi-nu-tsa gv-no-dv, a-le v-tla u-s-di-quo o-s-dv yu-na-da-nv-da-de-i.

052013.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎠᏴ ᎢᎬᏱ ᏃᎨᏅᏒᎩ ᏥᏳ ᏗᏔᎸᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎣᎦᏣᏅ, ᎡᏐᏏ ᏬᎩᎶᏒᎩ; ᎾᎿᎭᏉᎳ ᎣᏥᏲᏗᏱ ᎣᎩᏰᎸᏒᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩᏰᏃ ᎢᏳᏪᏛ ᎨᏒᎩ, ᎤᏩᏒ ᎡᎳᏗᏉ ᏭᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎤᏰᎸᏒᎩ.
052013.english: And we went before to ship, and sailed unto Assos, there intending to take in Paul: for so had he appointed, minding himself to go afoot.
052013.phonetic: A-le a-yv i-gv-yi no-ge-nv-sv-gi tsi-yu di-ta-lv-i, a-le o-ga-tsa-nv, E-so-si wo-gi-lo-sv-gi; na-hna Quo-la o-tsi-yo-di-yi o-gi-ye-lv-sv-gi, na-s-gi-ye-no i-yu-we-dv ge-sv-gi, u-wa-sv e-la-di-quo wu-lo-hi-s-di-yi u-ye-lv-sv-gi.

052014.cherokee: ᎡᏐᏏᏃ ᎣᎩᏩᏛᎲ ᎣᏥᏴᏔᏅᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎻᏗᎵᏂ ᏬᎩᎷᏨᎩ;
052014.english: And when he met with us at Assos, we took him in, and came to Mitylene.
052014.phonetic: E-so-si-no o-gi-wa-dv-hv o-tsi-yv-ta-nv-gi, a-le Mi-di-li-ni wo-gi-lu-tsv-gi;

052015.cherokee: ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᏫᏱᎦᏣᏅ ᎤᎩᏨᏛ ᎦᏲᏏ ᏧᏳᎪᏗ ᎣᏥᏂᏒᎩ.  ᎤᎩᏨᏛᏃ ᏎᎼᏏ ᏬᎩᎷᏨᎩ; ᏠᏥᎵᏯᏃ ᎣᎩᏒᎸ ᎤᎩᏨᏛ ᎹᎵᏔ ᏬᎩᎷᏨᎩ.
052015.english: And we sailed thence, and came the next day over against Chios; and the next day we arrived at Samos, and tarried at Trogyllium; and the next day we came to Miletus.
052015.phonetic: Na-hna-no wi-yi-ga-tsa-nv u-gi-tsv-dv Ga-yo-si tsu-yu-go-di o-tsi-ni-sv-gi.  U-gi-tsv-dv-no Se-mo-si wo-gi-lu-tsv-gi; Tlo-tsi-li-ya-no o-gi-sv-lv u-gi-tsv-dv Ma-li-ta wo-gi-lu-tsv-gi.

052016.cherokee: ᏉᎳᏰᏃ ᏧᏭᎪᏔᏅᎯ ᎨᏒᎩ ᎤᎶᎯᏍᏗᏱᏉ ᎡᏈᏌ, ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬᎩ ᏄᏚᎵᏍᎬᎾ ᎨᏒ ᎡᏏᏱ ᎤᏪᏓᏍᏗᏱ; ᎤᏩᏅᎬᎩᏰᏃ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᏰᎵ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᎬᏩᏛᏁᏗ ᏱᎩ, ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏭᎷᎯᏍᏗᏱ ᎯᏍᎦᏍᎪᎯᏁ ᎢᎦ ᎠᏍᏆᎵᏍᎬᎢ.
052016.english: For Paul had determined to sail by Ephesus, because he would not spend the time in Asia: for he hasted, if it were possible for him, to be at Jerusalem the day of Pentecost.
052016.phonetic: Quo-la-ye-no tsu-wu-go-ta-nv-hi ge-sv-gi u-lo-hi-s-di-yi-quo E-qui-sa, nv-da-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv-gi nu-du-li-s-gv-na ge-sv E-si-yi u-we-da-s-di-yi; u-wa-nv-gv-gi-ye-no, i-yu-no ye-li na-s-gi i-gv-wa-dv-ne-di yi-gi, Tsi-lu-si-li-mi wu-lu-hi-s-di-yi hi-s-ga-s-go-hi-ne i-ga a-s-qua-li-s-gv-i.

052017.cherokee: ᎹᎵᏔᏃ ᎤᏓᏅᏒᎩ ᎡᏈᏌ ᏫᏚᏯᏅᎲᎩ ᏗᎨᎦᏁᎶᏗ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬᎢ.
052017.english: And from Miletus he sent to Ephesus, and called the elders of the church.
052017.phonetic: Ma-li-ta-no u-da-nv-sv-gi E-qui-sa wi-du-ya-nv-hv-gi di-ge-ga-ne-lo-di tsu-na-ne-lo-di u-na-da-tlu-gv-i.

052018.cherokee: ᎬᏩᎷᏤᎸᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏂᏚᏪᏎᎸᎩ; ᎢᏥᎦᏔᎭ ᎢᎬᏱᏱ ᎢᎦ ᎡᏏᏱ ᏣᎩᎾᏄᎪᏥᎸᎩ ᏅᏓᎬᏩᏓᎴᏅᏛ, ᏄᏍᏛ ᎠᏆᎵᏂᏙᎸ ᎢᏤᎲ ᏂᎪᎯᎸᎢ.
052018.english: And when they were come to him, he said unto them, Ye know, from the first day that I came into Asia, after what manner I have been with you at all seasons,
052018.phonetic: Gv-wa-lu-tse-lv-no hi-a ni-du-we-se-lv-gi; I-tsi-ga-ta-ha i-gv-yi-yi i-ga E-si-yi tsa-gi-na-nu-go-tsi-lv-gi nv-da-gv-wa-da-le-nv-dv, nu-s-dv a-qua-li-ni-do-lv i-tse-hv ni-go-hi-lv-i.

052019.cherokee: ᏕᏥᎧᎿᎭᏩᏛᎡᎲ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎾᏆᎵᏍᎦᏍᏛᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᏥᎦᏌᏬᎢᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎩᎪᎵᏰᏍᎩ ᎠᏆᎵᏩᏛᎡᎲᎢ, ᏅᏗᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎬᏋᏁᏗᏱ ᏚᏄᎪᏔᏅᎢ.
052019.english: Serving the Lord with all humility of mind, and with many tears, and temptations, which befell me by the lying in wait of the Jews:
052019.phonetic: De-tsi-ka-hna-wa-dv-e-hv U-gv-wi-yu-hi ni-ga-v go-hu-s-di na-qua-li-s-ga-s-dv-na ge-sv-i, a-le de-tsi-ga-sa-wo-i-hv-i, a-le a-gi-go-li-ye-s-gi a-qua-li-wa-dv-e-hv-i, nv-di-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv A-ni-tsu-si go-hu-s-di gv-quv-ne-di-yi du-nu-go-ta-nv-i.

052020.cherokee: [ᎠᎴ ᎢᏥᎦᏔᎭ] ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᎢᏣᎵᏍᏕᎸᏙᏗ ᎠᏋᏍᎦᎳᏅᎯ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎢᏨᎾᏄᎪᏫᏎᎸᏉᏍᎩᏂ ᎠᎴ ᏕᏨᏰᏲᏅ ᏕᎦᎳᏫᎥᎢ ᎠᎴ ᏓᏓᏁᎳᏗᏒᎢ,
052020.english: And how I kept back nothing that was profitable unto you, but have shewed you, and have taught you publickly, and from house to house,
052020.phonetic: [A-le i-tsi-ga-ta-ha] go-hu-s-di i-tsa-li-s-de-lv-do-di a-quv-s-ga-la-nv-hi ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i, i-tsv-na-nu-go-wi-se-lv-quo-s-gi-ni a-le de-tsv-ye-yo-nv de-ga-la-wi-v-i a-le da-da-ne-la-di-sv-i,

052021.cherokee: ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᎦᏥᏴᏁᎲ ᎠᏂᏧᏏ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎠᏂᎪᎢ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎦᏁᏟᏴᎡᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎣᏓᏅᏛᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎪᎯᏳᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᎢᎦᏤᎵ ᏥᏌ ᎦᎶᏁᏛ.
052021.english: Testifying both to the Jews, and also to the Greeks, repentance toward God, and faith toward our Lord Jesus Christ.
052021.phonetic: Gv-ni-ge-sv ni-ga-tsi-yv-ne-hv A-ni-tsu-si a-le na-s-quo A-ni-go-i na-s-gi U-ne-la-nv-hi ga-ne-tli-yv-e-di ge-sv o-da-nv-dv-i, a-le go-hi-yu-di ge-sv U-gv-wi-yu-hi i-ga-tse-li Tsi-sa Ga-lo-ne-dv.

052022.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᎠᏆᏓᏅᏛ ᎦᎸᎢᏛ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏫᏥᎦᏘ, ᏂᏥᎦᏔᎲᎾ ᏅᏗᏆᎵᏍᏓᏁᎵᏒ ᎾᎿᎭᏂ,
052022.english: And now, behold, I go bound in the spirit unto Jerusalem, not knowing the things that shall befall me there:
052022.phonetic: Hna-quo-no gv-ni-yu-quo a-qua-da-nv-dv ga-lv-i-dv Tsi-lu-si-li-mi wi-tsi-ga-ti, ni-tsi-ga-ta-hv-na nv-di-qua-li-s-da-ne-li-sv na-hna-ni,

052023.cherokee: ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳᏍᎩᏂᏃᏅ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᎬᏁᎭ ᏕᎦᏚᏩᏗᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎬᎩᎦᏘᏴ ᎥᏆᎸᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎤᏲ ᎢᏯᏆᎵᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
052023.english: Save that the Holy Ghost witnesseth in every city, saying that bonds and afflictions abide me.
052023.phonetic: Ga-lv-quo-di-yu-s-gi-ni-no-nv A-da-nv-do gv-ni-ge-sv ni-gv-ne-ha de-ga-du-wa-di-sv-i, na-s-gi gv-gi-ga-ti-yv v-qua-lv-s-di ge-sv a-le u-yo i-ya-qua-li-s-da-ne-di ge-sv-i.

052024.cherokee: ᎠᏎᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎯᎠ ᏧᏓᎴᏅᏛ ᎥᏝ ᎪᎱᏍᏗ ᏯᎩᏖᎸᎲᏍᎦ, ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎬᏅ ᎥᏝ ᎤᏣᏘ ᏯᎩᎸᏉᏗ, ᎢᏳᏃ ᎤᎵᎮᎵᏍᏗ ᏯᏆᏓᏅᏔ ᏯᎩᏍᏆᏛ ᎦᎴᏂᏙᎲᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ ᎠᎩᏁᎸᎯ ᏗᎦᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᎬᏁᎲ ᎣᏍᏛ ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎬᏩᎦᏘᏯ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎧᏃᎮᏍᎩ.
052024.english: But none of these things move me, neither count I my life dear unto myself, so that I might finish my course with joy, and the ministry, which I have received of the Lord Jesus, to testify the gospel of the grace of God.
052024.phonetic: A-se-no na-s-gi hi-a tsu-da-le-nv-dv v-tla go-hu-s-di ya-gi-te-lv-hv-s-ga, a-le na-s-quo gv-nv v-tla u-tsa-ti ya-gi-lv-quo-di, i-yu-no u-li-he-li-s-di ya-qua-da-nv-ta ya-gi-s-qua-dv ga-le-ni-do-hv-i, a-le U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa a-gi-ne-lv-hi di-ga-lv-wi-s-da-ne-di ge-sv-i, gv-ni-ge-sv ni-gv-ne-hv o-s-dv ka-no-he-dv U-ne-la-nv-hi gv-wa-ga-ti-ya u-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv ka-no-he-s-gi.

052025.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎬᏂᏳᏉ ᏥᎦᏔᎭ ᏂᎯ ᏂᏥᎥᎢ, ᎢᏨᏰᎳᏗᏙᎸᎯ ᎦᎵᏥᏙᎲᏍᎬ ᏥᏃᎮᏍᎬ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏤᎵᎪᎯ, ᎠᏆᎧᏛ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎢᏥᎪᏩᏛᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
052025.english: And now, behold, I know that ye all, among whom I have gone preaching the kingdom of God, shall see my face no more.
052025.phonetic: Hna-quo-no gv-ni-yu-quo tsi-ga-ta-ha ni-hi ni-tsi-v-i, i-tsv-ye-la-di-do-lv-hi ga-li-tsi-do-hv-s-gv tsi-no-he-s-gv U-ne-la-nv-hi u-tse-li-go-hi, a-qua-ka-dv hna-quo i-tsi-go-wa-dv-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i.

052026.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎪᎯ ᎢᎦ ᎢᏥᎦᏔᎯ ᏂᏨᏴᎦ, ᎩᎶ ᎤᏂᎩᎬ ᎾᏆᏓᏅᎦᎸᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.
052026.english: Wherefore I take you to record this day, that I am pure from the blood of all men.
052026.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no i-yu-s-di go-hi i-ga i-tsi-ga-ta-hi ni-tsv-yv-ga, gi-lo u-ni-gi-gv na-qua-da-nv-ga-lv-na ge-sv-i.

052027.cherokee: ᎥᏝᏰᏃ ᎠᎩᏍᎦᎸᎯ ᏱᎩ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᎢᏨᏴᏁᏗᏱ ᏂᎦᎥ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎤᏓᏅᏖᎸᎢ.
052027.english: For I have not shunned to declare unto you all the counsel of God.
052027.phonetic: V-tla-ye-no a-gi-s-ga-lv-hi yi-gi gv-ni-ge-sv i-tsv-yv-ne-di-yi ni-ga-v U-ne-la-nv-hi u-da-nv-te-lv-i.

052028.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᏣᏓᎦᏌᏯᏍᏕᏍᏗ ᎢᏨᏒ, ᎠᎴ ᏕᏥᎦᏌᏯᏍᏕᏍᏗ ᏂᎦᏛ ᏑᎾᏓᏡᎩ ᎨᏒᎢ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᏥᎦᏘᏯ ᎢᏨᏁᎸᎯ ᏥᎩ ᎦᎸᏉᏗᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ, ᏗᏤᎳᏍᏗᏱ ᏧᎾᏁᎶᏗ ᎤᎾᏓᏡᎬ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᏧᏤᎵᎦ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᏩᏒ ᎤᎩᎬ ᏧᎫᏴᏔᏅᎯ ᏥᎩ.
052028.english: Take heed therefore unto yourselves, and to all the flock, over the which the Holy Ghost hath made you overseers, to feed the church of God, which he hath purchased with his own blood.
052028.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di i-tsa-da-ga-sa-ya-s-de-s-di i-tsv-sv, a-le de-tsi-ga-sa-ya-s-de-s-di ni-ga-dv su-na-da-tlu-gi ge-sv-i, na-s-gi di-tsi-ga-ti-ya i-tsv-ne-lv-hi tsi-gi Ga-lv-quo-di-yu A-da-nv-do, di-tse-la-s-di-yi tsu-na-ne-lo-di u-na-da-tlu-gv U-ne-la-nv-hi tsu-tse-li-ga, na-s-gi u-wa-sv u-gi-gv tsu-gu-yv-ta-nv-hi tsi-gi.

052029.cherokee: ᎯᎠᏰᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏥᎦᏔᎭ, ᎿᎭᏉ ᎠᎩᏁᎴᏍᏗ, ᎤᏂᎬᎥᏍᎩ ᏩᏯ ᏓᎨᏣᏓᏑᏴᏂ, ᏂᏓᏂᏙᎵᎬᎾ ᎨᏎᏍᏗ ᏑᎾᏓᏡᎩ ᎨᏒᎢ.
052029.english: For I know this, that after my departing shall grievous wolves enter in among you, not sparing the flock.
052029.phonetic: Hi-a-ye-no na-s-gi tsi-ga-ta-ha, hna-quo a-gi-ne-le-s-di, u-ni-gv-v-s-gi wa-ya da-ge-tsa-da-su-yv-ni, ni-da-ni-do-li-gv-na ge-se-s-di su-na-da-tlu-gi ge-sv-i.

052030.cherokee: ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎢᏨᏒ ᎢᏣᏓᏡᎬ ᏛᏂᎾᏄᎪᏥ ᎠᏂᏍᎦᏯ ᎤᏣᏘᏂ ᎾᏂᏪᏍᎨᏍᏗ ᎠᏂᏬᏂᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ ᏧᎾᏘᎿᎭᏫᏛᏗᏱ ᎤᏂᏰᎸᏎᏍᏗ.
052030.english: Also of your own selves shall men arise, speaking perverse things, to draw away disciples after them.
052030.phonetic: A-le na-s-quo i-tsv-sv i-tsa-da-tlu-gv dv-ni-na-nu-go-tsi a-ni-s-ga-ya u-tsa-ti-ni na-ni-we-s-ge-s-di a-ni-wo-ni-s-ge-s-di, a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi tsu-na-ti-hna-wi-dv-di-yi u-ni-ye-lv-se-s-di.

052031.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏳᏍᏗ ᎢᏥᏯᏫᏍᎨᏍᏗ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏣᏅᏖᏍᏗ ᏦᎢ ᏧᏕᏘᏴᏛ ᏂᏥᏑᎵᎪᎬᎾ ᏒᏃᏱ ᎠᎴ ᎢᎦ ᏗᎦᏥᎦᏌᏬᎢᎯ ᎢᏥᏏᏴᏫᎭ ᎢᏨᏰᏯᏔᏅᎢ.
052031.english: Therefore watch, and remember, that by the space of three years I ceased not to warn every one night and day with tears.
052031.phonetic: Na-s-gi i-yu-s-di i-tsi-ya-wi-s-ge-s-di, a-le i-tsa-nv-te-s-di tso-i tsu-de-ti-yv-dv ni-tsi-su-li-go-gv-na sv-no-yi a-le i-ga di-ga-tsi-ga-sa-wo-i-hi i-tsi-si-yv-wi-ha i-tsv-ye-ya-ta-nv-i.

052032.cherokee: Ꭷ, ᎢᏓᏓᏅᏟ, ᎿᎭᏉ ᏕᏨᏲᎯ ᎤᏁᎳᏅᎯ ᎢᏥᏙᎵᏍᏗᏱ, ᎤᏮᏙᏗᏱ, ᎧᏃᎮᏛ ᎬᏩᎦᏘᏯ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏗ ᎨᏒ ᎧᏃᎮᏍᎩ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᏰᎵ ᎨᏣᏁᏍᎨᏗ ᏥᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏣᏤᎵ ᏰᎵ ᎢᎨᏨᏁᏗ ᏥᎩ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎤᎾᏓᏅᏘ ᎢᎨᎬᏁᎸᎯ ᎤᎾᏤᎵ ᎢᏳᎵᏍᏙᏗ ᎨᏒᎢ.
052032.english: And now, brethren, I commend you to God, and to the word of his grace, which is able to build you up, and to give you an inheritance among all them which are sanctified.
052032.phonetic: Ka, i-da-da-nv-tli, hna-quo de-tsv-yo-hi U-ne-la-nv-hi i-tsi-do-li-s-di-yi, u-wv-do-di-yi, ka-no-he-dv gv-wa-ga-ti-ya u-da-do-li-s-di ge-sv ka-no-he-s-gi, na-s-gi ye-li ge-tsa-ne-s-ge-di tsi-gi, a-le i-tsa-tse-li ye-li i-ge-tsv-ne-di tsi-gi na-s-gi ni-ga-dv u-na-da-nv-ti i-ge-gv-ne-lv-hi u-na-tse-li i-yu-li-s-do-di ge-sv-i.

052033.cherokee: ᎥᏝ ᎩᎶ ᏥᏯᏚᎸᎡᎸᎯ ᏱᎩ ᎠᏕᎸ ᎤᏁᎬ ᎠᎴ ᎠᏕᎸ ᏓᎶᏂᎨ ᎠᎴ ᏗᏄᏬ.
052033.english: I have coveted no man's silver, or gold, or apparel.
052033.phonetic: V-tla gi-lo tsi-ya-du-lv-e-lv-hi yi-gi a-de-lv u-ne-gv a-le a-de-lv da-lo-ni-ge a-le di-nu-wo.

052034.cherokee: ᎢᏨᏒᏰᏃ ᎢᏥᎦᏔᎭ ᎯᎠ ᎾᏍᎩ ᏗᏉᏰᏂ ᏓᏋᏔᏅᎯ ᎠᎩᏩᏛᎲ ᎠᎩᏂᎬᏎᎲ ᎠᏋᏒ ᎠᎴ ᎾᏍᏉ ᎣᏤᎯ.
052034.english: Yea, ye yourselves know, that these hands have ministered unto my necessities, and to them that were with me.
052034.phonetic: I-tsv-sv-ye-no i-tsi-ga-ta-ha hi-a na-s-gi di-quo-ye-ni da-quv-ta-nv-hi a-gi-wa-dv-hv a-gi-ni-gv-se-hv a-quv-sv a-le na-s-quo o-tse-hi.

052035.cherokee: ᏂᎦᏛ ᎬᏂᎨᏒ ᏂᏨᏴᏁᎸ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎢᏣᏛᏁᏗᏱ ᏕᏥᎸᏫᏍᏓᏁᎲ ᏗᏥᏍᏕᎸᏗᏱ ᏗᏂᏩᎾᎦᎳᎢ, ᎠᎴ ᎢᏣᏅᏓᏗᏍᏗᏱ ᏧᏁᏤ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᎯ ᏥᏌ, ᎯᎠ ᏥᏄᏪᏎᎢ, "ᎤᏟ ᎣᏏᏳ ᎠᏓᏅᏓᏗᏍᏗ ᏴᏓᏁᎭ, ᎡᏍᎦᏉ ᏴᏓᏗᏁᎭ."
052035.english: I have shewed you all things, how that so labouring ye ought to support the weak, and to remember the words of the Lord Jesus, how he said, It is more blessed to give than to receive.
052035.phonetic: Ni-ga-dv gv-ni-ge-sv ni-tsv-yv-ne-lv na-s-gi i-tsa-dv-ne-di-yi de-tsi-lv-wi-s-da-ne-hv di-tsi-s-de-lv-di-yi di-ni-wa-na-ga-la-i, a-le i-tsa-nv-da-di-s-di-yi tsu-ne-tse U-gv-wi-yu-hi Tsi-sa, hi-a tsi-nu-we-se-i, "U-tli o-si-yu a-da-nv-da-di-s-di yv-da-ne-ha, e-s-ga-quo yv-da-di-ne-ha."

052036.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎯᎠ ᏄᏪᏒ, ᏚᎵᏂᏆᏅᏅᎩ ᎠᎴ ᏂᎦᏛ ᎠᏂᎦᏔᎲ ᎤᏓᏙᎵᏍᏔᏅᎩ.
052036.english: And when he had thus spoken, he kneeled down, and prayed with them all.
052036.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no hi-a nu-we-sv, du-li-ni-qua-nv-nv-gi a-le ni-ga-dv a-ni-ga-ta-hv u-da-do-li-s-ta-nv-gi.

052037.cherokee: ᎾᎦᏛᏃ ᎤᏣᏘ ᏚᎾᏠᏱᎸᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏉᎳ ᎠᎩᎵᎨᏂ ᎤᏂᏯᎸᏨ ᎬᏩᏔᏪᏙᏅᎩ.
052037.english: And they all wept sore, and fell on Paul's neck, and kissed him,
052037.phonetic: Na-ga-dv-no u-tsa-ti du-na-tlo-yi-lv-gi, a-le Quo-la a-gi-li-ge-ni u-ni-ya-lv-tsv gv-wa-ta-we-do-nv-gi.

052038.cherokee: ᎤᏲ ᎤᏂᏰᎸᏒᎩ Ꮀ ᎤᎬᏫᏳᏒ ᏅᏓᎦᎵᏍᏙᏗᏍᎬ ᎤᏁᏨ ᎾᏍᎩ ᎤᎧᏛ ᎿᎭᏉ ᎤᏂᎪᏩᏛᏗ ᏂᎨᏒᎾ ᎨᏒᎢ.  ᏥᏳᏃ ᏗᏔᎸᎢ ᏫᎬᏪᎧᏅᎩ.
052038.english: Sorrowing most of all for the words which he spake, that they should see his face no more. And they accompanied him unto the ship.
052038.phonetic: U-yo u-ni-ye-lv-sv-gi ho u-gv-wi-yu-sv nv-da-ga-li-s-do-di-s-gv u-ne-tsv na-s-gi u-ka-dv hna-quo u-ni-go-wa-dv-di ni-ge-sv-na ge-sv-i.  Tsi-yu-no di-ta-lv-i wi-gv-we-ka-nv-gi.

052101.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏙᎦᎦᎴᏅᎲ ᎠᎴ ᏥᏳᎯ ᎣᎦᏣᏅ, ᏧᏳᎪᏗ ᏬᎩᏅᏍᏔᏅᎩ ᎪᎣᏏ ᏬᎩᎷᏨᎩ, ᎤᎩᏨᏛᏃ ᎶᏗᏱ, ᎾᎿᎭᏃ ᏫᎣᎨᏅ ᏆᏖᎵ ᏬᎩᎷᏨᎩ.
052101.english: And it came to pass, that after we were gotten from them, and had launched, we came with a straight course unto Coos, and the day following unto Rhodes, and from thence unto Patara:
052101.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no do-ga-ga-le-nv-hv a-le tsi-yu-hi o-ga-tsa-nv, tsu-yu-go-di wo-gi-nv-s-ta-nv-gi Go-o-si wo-gi-lu-tsv-gi, u-gi-tsv-dv-no Lo-di-yi, na-hna-no wi-o-ge-nv Qua-te-li wo-gi-lu-tsv-gi.

052102.cherokee: ᎣᎩᏴᏩᏛᎲᏃ ᏥᏳ ᏈᏂᏏ ᎠᏂᏂᏒᎢ, ᎣᎦᏣᏅᎩ ᎣᎩᏴᏫᏛᎲᎩ.
052102.english: And finding a ship sailing over unto Phenicia, we went aboard, and set forth.
052102.phonetic: O-gi-yv-wa-dv-hv-no tsi-yu Qui-ni-si a-ni-ni-sv-i, o-ga-tsa-nv-gi o-gi-yv-wi-dv-hv-gi.

052103.cherokee: ᎿᎭᏉᏃ ᎣᎩᎪᎲ ᏌᏈ, ᎠᎴ ᎠᎦᏘᏏᏗᏢ ᎣᎩᎶᏒ, ᏏᎵᏱ ᏬᎩᏅᏍᏔᏅᎩ, ᏓᏯᏃ ᎣᎦᏣᎢᏒᎩ; ᎾᎿᎭᏰᏃ ᏥᏳ ᎤᎵᏁᏌᎸ ᎤᎵᏁᏌᎴᏍᏗ ᎨᏒᎩ.
052103.english: Now when we had discovered Cyprus, we left it on the left hand, and sailed into Syria, and landed at Tyre: for there the ship was to unlade her burden.
052103.phonetic: Hna-quo-no o-gi-go-hv Sa-qui, a-le a-ga-ti-si-di-tlv o-gi-lo-sv, Si-li-yi wo-gi-nv-s-ta-nv-gi, Da-ya-no o-ga-tsa-i-sv-gi; na-hna-ye-no tsi-yu u-li-ne-sa-lv u-li-ne-sa-le-s-di ge-sv-gi.

052104.cherokee: ᏙᏥᏩᏛᎲᏃ ᎠᏃᎯᏳᎲᏍᎩ ᎾᎿᎭᎣᎦᏅᏅᎩ ᎦᎵᏉᎩ ᏧᏙᏓᏆᏛ; ᎾᏍᎩ ᏉᎳ ᎯᎠ ᏂᎬᏩᏪᏎᎸᎩ ᎠᏓᏅᏙ ᎢᏳᏩᏂᏌᏛ; ᏞᏍᏗ ᏥᎷᏏᎵᎻ ᏤᏅᏒᎩ.
052104.english: And finding disciples, we tarried there seven days: who said to Paul through the Spirit, that he should not go up to Jerusalem.
052104.phonetic: Do-tsi-wa-dv-hv-no a-no-hi-yu-hv-s-gi na-hna o-ga-nv-nv-gi ga-li-quo-gi tsu-do-da-qua-dv; na-s-gi Quo-la hi-a ni-gv-wa-we-se-lv-gi a-da-nv-do i-yu-wa-ni-sa-dv; tle-s-di Tsi-lu-si-li-mi tse-nv-sv-gi.

052105.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᎦᎵᏉᎩ ᏧᏙᏓᏆᏛ ᎤᎶᏐᏅ, ᎣᎦᏂᎩᏒᎩ, ᏬᏥᎦᏛ ᎢᏗᏢ ᏬᎩᎶᏒᎩ; ᏂᎦᏛᏃ ᎾᏍᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏧᎾᏓᎵᎢ ᎠᎴ ᏧᏁᏥ ᏗᎬᎾᏘᏁᎯ ᎦᏚᎲ ᏫᎦᎾᏄᎪᎬ ᏫᏕᎪᎩᎧᏅᎩ.  ᎠᎹᏳᎶᏗᏃ ᏙᎦᎵᏂᏆᏅᏅ ᎣᎦᏓᏙᎵᏍᏔᏅᎩ.
052105.english: And when we had accomplished those days, we departed and went our way; and they all brought us on our way, with wives and children, till we were out of the city: and we kneeled down on the shore, and prayed.
052105.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no ga-li-quo-gi tsu-do-da-qua-dv u-lo-so-nv, o-ga-ni-gi-sv-gi, wo-tsi-ga-dv i-di-tlv wo-gi-lo-sv-gi; ni-ga-dv-no na-s-gi, a-le tsu-na-da-li-i a-le tsu-ne-tsi di-gv-na-ti-ne-hi ga-du-hv wi-ga-na-nu-go-gv wi-de-go-gi-ka-nv-gi.  A-ma-yu-lo-di-no do-ga-li-ni-qua-nv-nv o-ga-da-do-li-s-ta-nv-gi.

052106.cherokee: ᏙᎦᏓᏲᎵᎸᏃ, ᏥᏳᎯ ᎣᎦᏣᏅᎩ; ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏙᏧᏁᏅᏒ ᏮᎤᏂᎶᏒᎩ.
052106.english: And when we had taken our leave one of another, we took ship; and they returned home again.
052106.phonetic: Do-ga-da-yo-li-lv-no, tsi-yu-hi o-ga-tsa-nv-gi; na-s-gi-no do-tsu-ne-nv-sv wv-u-ni-lo-sv-gi.

052107.cherokee: ᎣᎩᏍᏆᏛᏃ ᎣᎦᏅᏔᏩᏗᏒ ᏓᏯ ᏦᎦᏂᎩᏒᎢ ᏙᎵᎹᏱ ᎣᎩᎷᏨᎩ, ᎠᎴ ᏙᏥᏲᎵᎸ ᎠᎾᏓᏅᏟ, ᎾᎿᎭᏑᏙᏓᏆᏛ ᎣᎨᏙᎸᎩ.
052107.english: And when we had finished our course from Tyre, we came to Ptolemais, and saluted the brethren, and abode with them one day.
052107.phonetic: O-gi-s-qua-dv-no o-ga-nv-ta-wa-di-sv da-ya tso-ga-ni-gi-sv-i do-li-ma-yi o-gi-lu-tsv-gi, a-le do-tsi-yo-li-lv a-na-da-nv-tli, na-hna su-do-da-qua-dv o-ge-do-lv-gi.

052108.cherokee: ᎤᎩᏨᏛᏃ ᏉᎳ ᎣᎦᎵᎪᎯ ᎣᎦᏂᎩᏒᎩ ᏏᏌᎵᏱ ᏬᎩᎷᏨᎩ.  ᏬᎩᏴᎸᏃ ᎦᏁᎸ ᏈᎵᎩ ᎠᎵ ᏥᏙᏂᏙᎯ, ᎾᏍᎩ ᎦᎸᏉᎩ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᎠᎦᎵᎪᏔᏅᎯ, ᎾᎿᎭᎣᎦᏅᏅᎩ.
052108.english: And the next day we that were of Paul's company departed, and came unto Caesarea: and we entered into the house of Philip the evangelist, which was one of the seven; and abode with him.
052108.phonetic: U-gi-tsv-dv-no Quo-la o-ga-li-go-hi o-ga-ni-gi-sv-gi Si-sa-li-yi wo-gi-lu-tsv-gi.  Wo-gi-yv-lv-no ga-ne-lv Qui-li-gi a-li tsi-do-ni-do-hi, na-s-gi ga-lv-quo-gi i-ya-ni-dv a-ga-li-go-ta-nv-hi, na-hna o-ga-nv-nv-gi.

052109.cherokee: ᎾᏍᎩᏃ ᏧᏪᏥ ᏅᎩ ᎢᏯᏂᏛ ᎠᏁᎲᎩ ᎠᎾᏛ ᎠᎾᏙᎴᎰᏍᎩ.
052109.english: And the same man had four daughters, virgins, which did prophesy.
052109.phonetic: Na-s-gi-no tsu-we-tsi nv-gi i-ya-ni-dv a-ne-hv-gi a-na-dv a-na-do-le-ho-s-gi.
